confidence_rubric.high: 4+ independent signals converge at single verse

pivot.verse_ar: إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ
pivot.notes: Only unmirrored verse; doubled pronominal fronting (إِيَّاكَ / إِيَّاكَ) unique in surah; shortest verse by syllable count; grammatical person pivot (3rd→2nd)
pivot.center_logic_note: geometric center v.5 (4/5); no secondaries. Sole surah with 4/5 primary and no competing zone. The address-shift at إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ is unambiguous but the prosodic signal is weaker than the structural.

boundaries.label: Frame A
boundaries.notes: الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ — exact repetition vv. 1 & 3; rhyme anchor [-iːm]

boundaries.label: Frame B
boundaries.notes: صِرَاطَ / الصِّرَاطَ — concatenation vv. 6–7; petition frame closes

signal_table.lexical_detail: الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ — frame opens
الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ — exact repetition of v. 1

signal_table.theme_detail: 3rd-person divine characterization → 1st-person direct petition
signal_table.discourse_detail: huwa/absent (3rd) → anta/present (2nd); declarative praise → direct worship+petition
signal_table.prosody_note: rhythm break — shortest verse; doubled إِيَّاكَ pronominal fronting unique in surah

signal_table.lexical_detail: الصِّرَاطَ — root صرط introduced

signal_table.verse_ar: صِرَاطَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ
signal_table.lexical_detail: صِرَاطَ — root صرط repeated, concatenation closes
signal_table.prosody_note: thematic closure; longest verse — structural weight at end

verses.ar: صِرَاطَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (283/286, 98.9%).

pivot.verse_ar: وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَاكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطًا لِتَكُونُوا شُهَدَاءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيدًا … فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ

signals.thematic: v.142: foolish say 'what turned them?' → east/west belongs to God. v.143: أُمَّةً وَسَطًا → witnesses over people + Messenger witness → qiblah test → لَرَءُوف رَحِيم. vv.144/149/150: المسجد الحرام ×3. v.148: فَاسْتَبِقُوا الخَيْرَات. v.151: Messenger recites/purifies/teaches. v.152: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ.
signals.lexical: أُمَّةً وَسَطًا (v.143: unique). شُهَدَاء ×2 (v.143). المَسْجِد الحَرَام ×3 (vv.144/149/150). الحَقّ ×3 (vv.144/147/149). فَاسْتَبِقُوا (v.148: unique imperative). فَاذْكُرُونِي (v.152: unique reciprocal).
signals.mirror: D (11v) at offset −0.002. Flanked by C₀ (38v) and C₀′ (25v). Confirmed by Farrin/Robinson/El-Tahry.
signals.discourse: v.142: reported speech. v.143: divine declaration. v.144: command. v.145: conditional. v.146: report. v.147: declaration. v.148: command. v.149: command. v.150: command + purpose. v.151: parallel. v.152: reciprocal command. Twelve discourse modes in eleven verses.
signals.rhyme: مُسْتَقِيمٍ / رَحِيمٌ / يَعْمَلُونَ / الظَّالِمِينَ / يَعْلَمُونَ / الْمُمْتَرِينَ / قَدِيرٌ / تَعْمَلُونَ / تَهْتَدُونَ / تَعْلَمُونَ / تَكْفُرُونِ — pure nasal.

Martyrs alive (v.154); إِنَّا لِلَّه; tawhīd (v.163); creation-signs (v.164); لَيْسَ البِرَّ: 13-component righteousness (v.177)
قِصَاص; FASTING/Ramadan (v.185); قَرِيب (v.186); HAJJ; one أُمَّة (v.213); wine (v.219); MARRIAGE/DIVORCE; ṬĀLŪT/DĀWŪD
آيَة الكُرْسِيّ (v.255: الحَيّ القَيُّوم); لَا إِكْرَاه (v.256); اللَّه وَلِيّ; Abraham vs. king; 4 birds
SPENDING: seed→700; أُولُو الألباب (v.269); RIBĀ; DEBT (v.282, longest); full creed (v.285); لَا يُكَلِّف + three رَبَّنَا + مَوْلَانَا فَانصُرْنَا

structure_summary.note: LONGEST SURAH. 9-block ring (most complex). Axis v.143 confirmed Farrin/Robinson/El-Tahry. أُمَّةً وَسَطًا at the وَسَط. لَا خَوْف ×6. المسجد الحرام ×3. 'That nation passed' ×2 → new أُمَّة born. B: خَلِيفَة. B′: الحَيّ القَيُّوم.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (197/200, 98.5%). المُفلحون at pivot (v.104) and close (v.200).

pivot.verse_ar: وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا … وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ

signals.thematic: v.100: if obey People of Book → turned back. v.101: how could you disbelieve while verses recited + Messenger among you? → hold to God = straight path. v.102: fear God → die only as Muslims. v.103: اعتصموا بحبل الله → enemies → brothers → edge of Fire → saved. v.104: let an أُمَّة arise → enjoin right + forbid wrong = المُفلحون. v.105: don't divide. vv.106–107: faces whitened/blackened. v.108: God wants no injustice.
signals.mirror: D (9v) at offset +0.018. Flanked by C (36v) and C′ (12v).
signals.discourse: v.100: warning. v.101: rhetorical question. v.102: command. v.103: command + reminder. v.104: command (let arise). v.105: prohibition. vv.106–107: eschatological scene. v.108: declaration. Eight discourse modes in nine verses.
signals.rhyme: كَافِرِينَ / الْمُسْتَقِيمِ / مُسْلِمُونَ / تَهْتَدُونَ / الْمُفْلِحُونَ / عَظِيمٌ / تَكْفُرُونَ / خَالِدُونَ / الْعَالَمِينَ — pure nasal [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm].

CHOSEN FAMILIES (v.33, title); Mary chosen (v.42); Jesus: كَلِمَة + المَسِيح (v.45); disciples = Muslims; خير الماكرين (v.54); take + raise (v.55); Jesus = Adam (v.59); مُبَاهَلَة (v.61)
كَلِمَة سَوَاء (v.64); Abraham حَنِيف مُسْلِم (v.67); مِيثَاق of prophets (v.81); غَيْر الإسلام → not accepted (v.85); مِلَّة إبراهيم (v.95); first House = بَكَّة + Hajj (vv.96–97)

خَيْر أُمَّة → enjoin + forbid (v.110); not all same: upright community (v.113); don't take بِطَانَة
UHUD: Badr recalled (v.123); angels + victory from God; Muhammad only a messenger (v.144); forgave (v.152); consult (v.159); martyrs alive (v.169); حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ (v.173)
Every soul tastes death (v.185); أُولِي الألباب (v.190 = v.7); five رَبَّنَا prayers + God responds (vv.191–195); People of Book: believers (v.199); persevere/endure/station → تُفْلِحُونَ (v.200)

structure_summary.note: أُولُو الألباب frame (vv.7/190). Theology → formation (pivot) → test (Uhud). v.7: محكمات/متشابهات. v.18: triple testimony. v.59: Jesus = Adam. v.64: كَلِمَة سَوَاء. v.103: hold rope. v.104/200: المُفلحون. v.110: best أُمَّة. v.144: Muhammad only a messenger. v.159: شَاوِرْهُم. v.169: martyrs alive.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal + divine-attribute closings (174/176, 98.9%). كَانَ اللَّهُ + attribute pair ~40+ times. رَقِيبًا opens → عَلِيمٌ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ وَلَوْ كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ لَوَجَدُوا فِيهِ اخْتِلَافًا كَثِيرًا … اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ … وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللَّهِ حَدِيثًا … فَتَبَيَّنُوا

signals.thematic: v.82: Quran = no contradiction (self-authentication). v.83: security/fear → refer to authority. v.84: fight — responsible for self only. v.85: good/evil intercession. v.86: return greetings better. v.87: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ → most TRUTHFUL (حَدِيثًا). vv.88–91: four hypocrite categories (treaty/strained/neutral/hostile). vv.92–93: believer killing (accidental: diya / intentional: Hell eternal). v.94: فَتَبَيَّنُوا — VERIFY. v.95: sitters ≠ strivers → degrees.
signals.lexical: الْقُرْآنَ (v.82: only deployment as object of تَدَبُّر). اخْتِلَافًا (v.82: contradiction). أَصْدَقُ (v.87: most truthful). الْمُنَافِقِينَ (v.88: hypocrites concentrated). مُؤْمِنًا (vv.92–93: believer ×5 in two verses). فَتَبَيَّنُوا (v.94: verify — imperative).
signals.mirror: D (14v) at offset 0.000. Flanked by C (38v) and C′ (39v). THE ONLY ZERO-OFFSET IN 114 SURAHS.
signals.discourse: v.82: rhetorical question (epistemological). v.83: conditional. v.84: command. v.85: principle. v.86: social rule. v.87: declaration (tawhīd). vv.88–91: legislation (four categories). vv.92–93: legislation (homicide). v.94: command (verify). v.95: comparison. Ten discourse modes in fourteen verses.
signals.rhyme: كَثِيرًا / قَلِيلًا / مُقِيتًا / حَسِيبًا / حَدِيثًا / سَبِيلًا ×3 / حَكِيمًا / عَظِيمًا / خَبِيرًا / رَحِيمًا / فَتِيلًا — predominantly [-iːla/-iːma/-iːra]. حَدِيثًا (v.87) unique terminal.

boundaries.label: Frame A
boundaries.notes: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ: نَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ (v.1); orphans; INHERITANCE (كَلَالَة ①); حُدُود اللَّه

boundaries.label: Body C
boundaries.notes: SHIRK ① (v.48); obedience hierarchy (v.59); fight for oppressed; obey Messenger = obey God (v.80)

boundaries.label: Pivot D

boundaries.label: Body C′
boundaries.notes: SHIRK ② (v.116 = v.48); prayer in danger; Abraham خَلِيلًا (v.125); sovereignty ×6

boundaries.label: Body B′

boundaries.label: Frame A′

Women's rights; عَاشِرُوهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (v.19); ضَعِيفًا (v.28); worship + social (v.36); atom's weight (v.40); Prophet as WITNESS (v.41); tayammum
SHIRK ① unforgivable (v.48); Abraham's family; trusts/justice (v.58); OBEY hierarchy (v.59); prophets/truthful/martyrs/righteous (v.69); fight for oppressed; obey Messenger = obey God (v.80)
PIVOT (0.000, ONLY ZERO-OFFSET) — Quran = no contradiction (v.82); tawhīd + most truthful (v.87); HYPOCRITE CATEGORIES (vv.88–91); BELIEVER'S LIFE SACRED (vv.92–93); فَتَبَيَّنُوا VERIFY (v.94)
SHIRK ② (v.116 = v.48); PRAYER IN DANGER (vv.101–103); Abraham خَلِيلًا (v.125); لِلَّهِ مَا فِي ×6; women's rulings
Justice against self (v.135); believe all Books; hypocrites → lowest Fire (v.145); no desire to punish (v.147); don't discriminate messengers
Crucifixion denied (v.157); raised (v.158); prophetic chain (v.163); Moses: direct speech (v.164); Christology: كَلِمَتُهُ + رُوحٌ مِنْهُ + لَا ثَلَاثَة (v.171); كَلَالَة ② (v.176)

structure_summary.note: ONLY ZERO-OFFSET IN 114 SURAHS. كَلَالَة frame (vv.12/176). v.1: one soul. v.48/116: shirk ×2. v.59: obedience hierarchy. v.82: Quran = no contradiction. v.87: most truthful. v.92–93: believer killing. v.94: verify. v.125: Abraham خَلِيل. v.147: no desire to punish. v.157: crucifixion denied. v.171: comprehensive Christology.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (118/120, 98.3%). يُرِيدُ opens → قَدِيرٌ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصَارَىٰ أَوْلِيَاءَ … مَنْ يَرْتَدَّ … فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ … إِنَّمَا وَلِيُّكُمُ اللَّهُ … بَلِّغْ مَا أُنْزِلَ … مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ

signals.thematic: v.51: don't take Jews/Christians as أَوْلِيَاء → allies of each other. v.54: whoever reverts → God brings replacement: يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ (mutual love) + humble/powerful/strive/no blame-fear. v.55: walī = God + Messenger + believers (prayer/zakāh/bow). v.56: حِزْبُ اللَّهِ: predominant. v.64: 'God's hand chained' → both extended. v.65: if believed → Gardens. v.66: if upheld Torah/Gospel → consumed from above/below. v.67: بَلِّغْ → God protects. v.69: believers/Jews/Sabians/Christians → believes + righteous → no fear/grief.
signals.lexical: أَوْلِيَاء ×3 (vv.51/55/57: walāya concentrated). يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ (v.54: mutual love — unique). حِزْبُ اللَّهِ (v.56: 'God's party'). بَلِّغْ (v.67: delivery-command). لَا خَوْفٌ … لَا يَحْزَنُونَ (v.69: no-fear/no-grief at pivot close).
signals.mirror: D (19v) at offset −0.004. Flanked by B (39v) and B′ (31v).
signals.discourse: v.51: prohibition (يَا أَيُّهَا). v.54: divine promise (replacement). v.55: declaration (your walī). v.56: identification (God's party). v.57: prohibition. vv.59–60: rhetorical challenge (يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ). v.64: refutation. v.65: conditional. v.67: command to Prophet (بَلِّغْ). v.68: command to People of Book. v.69: universal declaration. Eleven discourse modes in nineteen verses.
signals.rhyme: الظَّالِمِينَ / نَادِمِينَ / خَاسِرِينَ / عَلِيمٌ / الْغَالِبُونَ / مُؤْمِنِينَ / يَعْقِلُونَ / فَاسِقُونَ / السَّبِيلِ / يَكْتُمُونَ / يَعْمَلُونَ / الْمُفْسِدِينَ / النَّعِيمِ / يَعْمَلُونَ / الْكَافِرِينَ / قَدِيرٌ / يَحْزَنُونَ — pure nasal [-ūn/-iːn] throughout.

boundaries.label: Frame A
boundaries.notes: أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ (v.1) opens surah; أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ (v.3, religion perfected); wuḍū'/tayammum; مِيثَاق; اعْدِلُوا (v.8)

boundaries.label: Body B
boundaries.notes: TWO COVENANTS (Israel/Christians); Moses/Holy Land; Cain/Abel (v.32: one soul = all mankind); SCRIPTURAL CHAIN (Torah→Gospel→Quran as مُهَيْمِن); حُكْمَ الْجَاهِلِيَّة

boundaries.label: Pivot D
boundaries.notes: WALĀYA legislation + mutual love (يُحِبُّهُمْ v.54) + triple walāya (v.55) + بَلِّغ (v.67) + pluralist-salvation (v.69)

boundaries.label: Body B′
boundaries.notes: Israel: denied/killed; 'God IS Messiah' corrected; 'third of three' → one God; both ate food; Christians nearest; intoxicants final prohibition; evil ≠ good

boundaries.label: Frame A′
boundaries.notes: Day: messengers → عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ; Jesus's eschatological dialogue; المَائِدَة (title); يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ; لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ (dominion ×4)

block_map.label: أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ (v.1); lawful/prohibited foods; أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ (v.3); wuḍū'/tayammum; مِيثَاق; اعْدِلُوا; God withheld hands

TWO COVENANTS: Israel (broke → cursed → hardened) + Christians (forgot → animosity); نُورٌ وَكِتَابٌ مُبِينٌ; 'God IS Messiah' → dominion; MOSES/HOLY LAND (40 years); CAIN/ABEL (one soul = all mankind v.32); SCRIPTURAL CHAIN: Torah→كَافِرُونَ / eye-for-eye→ظَالِمُونَ / Gospel→فَاسِقُونَ / Quran as مُهَيْمِن + شِرْعَة وَمِنْهَاج (v.48); حُكْمَ الْجَاهِلِيَّة

PIVOT (−0.004) — WALĀYA: أَوْلِيَاء ×3; يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ (v.54, mutual divine love); إِنَّمَا وَلِيُّكُمُ اللَّهُ (v.55); حِزْبُ اللَّهِ (v.56); بَلِّغْ + God will protect (v.67); PLURALIST-SALVATION: believers/Jews/Sabians/Christians → no fear/grief (v.69)

Israel: denied/killed messengers; 'God IS Messiah' → shirk = no Paradise (v.72); 'third of three' → one God (v.74); both ate food (v.75); Christians nearest in affection (v.82); eyes overflow with tears (v.83); intoxicants = رِجْس (v.90); evil ≠ good (v.100)

Day: messengers → عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ (v.109); JESUS's eschatological dialogue (vv.116–117); المَائِدَة from heaven (vv.112–115); يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ (v.119); لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ (v.120)

structure_summary.note: Second-tightest center (−0.004). v.1: أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ. v.3: religion perfected. v.32: one soul = all mankind. vv.44–47: three-judgment refrain. v.48: Quran as مُهَيْمِن. v.54: mutual love at pivot. v.55: triple walāya. v.67: بَلِّغ + protection. v.69: pluralist-salvation (= Q.2:62). v.75: both ate food. v.82: Christians nearest. v.90: final alcohol prohibition. vv.116–117: Jesus's eschatological dialogue. v.119: truthfulness benefits. v.120: dominion ×4.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (163/165, 98.8%). يَعْدِلُونَ opens → رَحِيمٌ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ … الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمَانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ … أُولَئِكَ لَهُمُ الْأَمْنُ

signals.thematic: Abraham's celestial argument (star→moon→sun→each sets→'I'm free→حَنِيفًا', vv.76–79) + v.82: theological verdict ('those who believed and did not mix their belief with injustice → security + guidance') + 18-prophet genealogy (vv.84–86, most comprehensive single-passage prophetic list) + command: 'from their guidance take an example → ذِكْرَى لِلْعَالَمِينَ' (v.90). Pivot answers v.1's 'disbelievers equate' with Abraham's proof that nothing in the cosmos qualifies as Lord.
signals.lexical: حَنِيفًا (v.79 → v.161: مِلَّة إبراهيم inclusio). الْأَمْن (v.82: security verdict — unique in surah). النُّبُوَّة (v.89: only deployment). ذِكْرَى لِلْعَالَمِينَ (v.90). الْمُحْسِنِينَ (v.84). مُهْتَدُون (v.82).
signals.mirror: D (17v) at offset −0.006 — tightest center-offset in the entire project. Five-block ring flanked by B (38v) and B′ (45v).
signals.discourse: Ten discourse modes in seventeen verses: address to father (v.74) → observation/narrative (vv.76–78) → declaration (v.79) → argument (vv.80–81) → verdict (v.82) → divine commentary (v.83) → genealogy (vv.84–86) → commentary (vv.87–88) → entrustment (v.89) → command اقْتَدِهْ (v.90).
signals.rhyme: مُبِينٍ/الْمُوقِنِينَ/الْآفِلِينَ/الضَّالِّينَ/الْمُشْرِكِينَ/تَتَذَكَّرُونَ/مُهْتَدُونَ/الْمُحْسِنِينَ/الصَّالِحِينَ/الْعَالَمِينَ — pure nasal [-iːn/-ūn] throughout pivot.

block_map.label: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ + creation/darknesses/light → disbelievers equate; كَتَبَ الرَّحْمَةَ ① (v.12); الْقَاهِرُ ① (v.18); 'greatest testimony = God → this Quran to warn'; messengers patient → victory; لَا مُبَدِّلَ ① (v.34)

Communities like you + nothing neglected in Book (v.38); الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ② (v.45: after elimination); كَتَبَ الرَّحْمَةَ ② (v.54); إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ (v.57); مَفَاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ + not a leaf falls (v.59); الْقَاهِرُ ② (v.61); God's guidance IS the guidance (v.71)

PIVOT (−0.006, tightest in project) — Abraham: star/moon/sun → 'I'm free → حَنِيفًا' (v.79); v.82: 'believed + no injustice = الْأَمْن + guided' (theological verdict); 18-prophet genealogy (vv.84–86, most comprehensive); 'from their guidance take an example → ذكرى للعالمين' (v.90)

'God revealed nothing?' → Moses=light; CREATION SIGNS (grain/daybreak/stars/one soul/rain, vv.95–99); لَا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصَارُ (v.103, divine imperceptibility); لَا مُبَدِّلَ ② (v.115); dead→life ≠ darknesses; دَارُ السَّلَام (v.127); وَرَبُّكَ الْغَنِيُّ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ (v.133)

Food customs refuted; TEN COMMANDMENTS (وَصَايَا, vv.151–153: three refrains لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ/تَذَكَّرُونَ/تَتَّقُونَ); مِلَّة إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا (v.161: Muhammad claims pivot's Abrahamic مِلَّة); 'صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ' (vv.162–163, total dedication); لَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ (v.164); successors on earth → test → غَفُور رَحِيم (v.165)

inclusios.note: God obligates Himself to mercy — twice. v.12 cosmically (will gather you); v.54 personally (who does evil then repents → غَفُور رَحِيم). The surah's most theologically radical inclusio; no other surah deploys this formula twice.
v.1: praise on creation. v.45: praise after elimination of wrongdoers. Two حمد declarations: creative + judicial.
Only surah deploying this name-formula twice.
'None can alter His words' — A (v.34) ↔ B′ (v.115).
Abraham declares حنيف at the center (v.79); Muhammad claims the same مِلَّة at the close (v.161). The pivot's Abrahamic declaration IS the closing's Muhammadan claim.
Surah's most repeated divine title, concentrated at key junctures (vv.45/71/90/162/164).

special_features: v.12 + v.54: كَتَبَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ الرَّحْمَةَ — 'He decreed upon Himself mercy': Quran's most theologically radical self-obligation; God binds Himself to mercy. Deployed twice, framing the inner ring.

v.38: وَمَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ … إِلَّا أُمَمٌ أَمْثَالُكُمْ — 'no creature/bird except communities like you → We neglected nothing in the Book': every animal community = like humanity; the Book covers all.

v.59: وَعِنْدَهُ مَفَاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ — 'with Him are the keys of the unseen → only He knows → not a leaf falls but He knows it → no grain in the earth's darknesses → no moist or dry → except in a clear record': Quran's only deployment of مَفَاتِح الغَيْب; most comprehensive divine-omniscience verse (cosmic → ecological → microscopic).

vv.76–79: Abraham's celestial argument — star/moon/sun: each sets → 'what sets cannot be Lord' → 'I'm free from what you associate → حَنِيفًا → not of polytheists': Quran's most famous rational-theological demonstration; observation → reasoning → elimination → monotheism.

v.82: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمَانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ أُولَئِكَ لَهُمُ الْأَمْنُ وَهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ — theological verdict at the geometric center. Companions were troubled until Prophet clarified ظُلْم = شِرْك (citing Luqmān 31:13). Faith unmixed with shirk = security + guidance.

vv.84–86: 18-prophet genealogy in three thematic waves — (1) Isaac/Jacob/Noah/David/Solomon/Job/Joseph/Moses/Aaron → الْمُحْسِنِينَ; (2) Zechariah/John/Jesus/Elias → righteous; (3) Ishmael/Elisha/Jonah/Lot → preferred over worlds. Most comprehensive single-passage prophetic list in the Quran; NOT chronological — thematic.
v.103: لَا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصَارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصَارَ وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ الْخَبِيرُ — divine imperceptibility: God sees; creation cannot see God. Asymmetry IS the theology.

vv.151–153: Ten Commandments (وَصَايَا) — ten prohibitions/commands across three verses, each closing with ذَلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ + different rationale: لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (reason) → لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (remember) → لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (conscience). Three refrains address three faculties: intellect → memory → conscience.

vv.162–163: 'قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ' — most complete personal-dedication declaration: prayer + sacrifice + living + dying = for God → no partner → first Muslim.
v.164: لَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَى — Quran's foundational individual-responsibility verse (parallel in Q.17:15/35:18/39:7/53:38).
Prosodic arc: يَعْدِلُونَ (v.1: 'they equate' = shirk) → رَحِيمٌ (v.165: غَفُور رَحِيم). Opens on humanity's greatest error; closes on God's greatest attribute.
cross_surah_flags: Q.10:25/Q.6:127 — دَارُ السَّلَامِ EXACT: two surahs sharing 'the Home of Peace.'
Q.17:22–39 — parallel Decalogue: Q.6:151–153 (وَصَايَا with three وَصَّاكُمْ refrains) ↔ Q.17:22–39 (Decalogue with الْحِكْمَة frame). Two parallel ethical codes.
Q.21:51–73 — Abraham parallel: Q.6:74–83 (celestial argument: rational) ↔ Q.21:51–71 (idol-smashing: dramatic). Two modes of Abrahamic demonstration.
Q.17:15/35:18/39:7/53:38 — لَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ thread: Q.6:164 + four other surahs share the individual-responsibility verse.
Q.1:2/Q.18:1/Q.34:1/Q.35:1 — الْحَمْدُ opening thread: Q.6 is one of five; only one that follows الحمد immediately with shirk in the same verse.
Q.12:39/Q.6:57 — إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ thread: Q.6:57 (sovereignty) ↔ Q.12:40 (Joseph in prison).
Q.2:124–136 — Abrahamic مِلَّة thread: Q.6:161 (مِلَّة إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا) ↔ Q.2:130/135.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (203/206, 98.5%).

pivot.verse_ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرَى آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا … أَفَأَمِنُوا مَكْرَ اللَّهِ … وَمَا وَجَدْنَا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ مِنْ عَهْدٍ

signals.thematic: Post-cycle theological summary. v.94: every city → prophet → seized with poverty/hardship → perhaps humble. v.95: replaced bad with good → prospered → 'our fathers too' → seized suddenly while unaware. v.96: 'if only the cities believed and feared → We would have opened upon them blessings from heaven and earth → but they denied → seized for what they earned' (conditional prosperity, Quran's most explicit). vv.97–98: rhetorical security questions (night/morning). v.99: 'no one feels secure from God's plan except the losing people.' v.100: sealed hearts. v.101: messengers came → wouldn't believe. v.102: 'We did not find for most any covenant → most were defiantly disobedient.' The pivot is the hinge between C (five-nation cycle) and C′ (Moses extended): it names the human condition. C′ responds with v.156: 'My mercy encompasses ALL THINGS.'
signals.lexical: الْقُرَى ×5 (vv.94/96/97/98/101: 'the cities' — dense concentration). مَكْرَ اللَّهِ ×2 (v.99). عَهْد (v.102: 'covenant' — the pivot verdict). فَاسِقِينَ (v.102). يَطْبَعُ/نَطْبَعُ (vv.100/101: 'seal'). الْقُرَى concentration at pivot unique in surah.
signals.mirror: D (9v) at offset −0.027. Seven-block ring (A/B/C/D/C′/B′/A′). Flanked by C (35v: five compressed destructions) and C′ (69v: Moses extended). Tightest center-offset in the dataset for a surah of this length.
signals.discourse: Seven discourse modes in nine verses: narrative summary (vv.94–95) → conditional declaration (v.96) → two rhetorical questions (vv.97–98) → universal principle (v.99) → rhetorical question (v.100) → summary (v.101) → verdict (v.102).
signals.rhyme: يَضَّرَّعُونَ/يَشْعُرُونَ/يَكْسِبُونَ/نَائِمُونَ/يَلْعَبُونَ/الْخَاسِرُونَ/يَسْمَعُونَ/الْكَافِرِينَ/فَاسِقِينَ — pure nasal [-ūn/-iːn]; الْخَاسِرُونَ ('the losing people') marks v.99's universal principle.

block_map.label: المص + Book revealed → warn + reminder; cities destroyed; الْوَزْنُ الْحَقُّ (scales: heavy/light, vv.8–9); established on earth → little grateful

ADAM/IBLĪS (48v): created+formed → prostrate → Iblīs refused ('I'm better') → expelled → reprieved → four-direction strategy → Adam fell → رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنْفُسَنَا (v.23, Quran's first human prayer) → يَا بَنِي آدَمَ ×4 (vv.26/27/31/35) → الأَعْرَاف people (vv.46–49: liminal discerners, title element) → أَلَا لَهُ الْخَلْقُ وَالْأَمْرُ (v.54) → إِنَّ رَحْمَتَ اللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (v.56)
FIVE-NATION CYCLE (35v): Noah → Hūd/ʿĀd → Ṣāliḥ/Thamūd → Lot → Shuʿayb/Madyan; formulaic: 'worship God alone → denied → saved + destroyed'; خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ (v.87, Shuʿayb)

PIVOT (−0.027) — post-cycle covenant verdict: 'if cities believed → blessings from heaven/earth' (v.96, conditional prosperity at center); 'no one secure from God's plan except losers' (v.99); 'We found most without covenant — most were فَاسِقِينَ' (v.102, surah's devastating verdict); الْقُرَى ×5

MOSES EXTENDED (69v): Pharaoh → magicians prostrate → five plagues → crossing sea → Sinai: لَنْ تَرَانِي (v.143, only theophany) → tablets → golden calf → earthquake → 'My mercy encompasses ALL THINGS' (v.156, most comprehensive mercy) → النَّبِيّ الْأُمِّيّ (vv.157–158: found in Torah+Gospel → 'O mankind, to you ALL'); Israel; sabbath-breakers → apes; mountain raised

COVENANT OF THE PROGENY (27v): أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ → 'Yes, we testify' (v.172, primordial pre-temporal covenant answering B's يَا بَنِي آدَمَ ×4); detached one → dog parable; hearts/eyes/ears useless; الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى (v.180, foundational); إِنَّ وَلِيِّيَ اللَّهُ (v.196)

خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجَاهِلِينَ (v.199, three-part ethical imperative); when Quran recited → listen + pay attention (v.204); SAJDA (v.206): 'those near Your Lord: not arrogant → prostrate' — closes the surah's arrogance/prostration arc (Iblīs refused v.11 → angels never refuse v.206)

inclusios.note: v.11: Iblīs refuses prostration ('I'm better — fire vs. clay') ↔ v.206: 'those near God are not prevented by arrogance → they prostrate.' The 206-verse surah is framed by the prostration question.
vv.26/27/31/35 (B: four ethical commands) ↔ v.172 (B′: primordial covenant أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ). The four commands presuppose the prior testimony.
v.35 (for the obedient) → v.49 (for the Aʿrāf people admitted to Paradise). No-fear/no-grief declared twice in B for two different groups.
v.56 (mercy near the doers of good) → v.161 (increased) → v.170 (reward not lost). Doers-of-good thread B→C′→C′.
Arrogance = surah's master sin. Iblīs (v.13) → deniers (vv.36/40/48/75/76/88/133/146) → those near God: NOT arrogant (v.206). The surah's moral spine.

special_features: v.23: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنْفُسَنَا — 'Our Lord, we wronged ourselves': Quran's first human prayer — content is self-accusation, not excuse. Contrast with Iblīs (v.12): blames God ('You put me in error'). Moral axis: self-accusation → mercy; blaming God → Hell

vv.46–49: الأَعْرَاف (title element) — men on elevated ground between Paradise and Fire; recognize inhabitants by marks; call peace; confront arrogant Fire-dwellers; finally admitted to Paradise. They embody discernment (بَصِيرَة) between two outcomes — the surah's moral exemplars
v.40: حَتَّى يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِيَاطِ — 'until a camel enters the eye of a needle': most vivid Quranic absolute-impossibility image for arrogant disbelievers entering Paradise
v.54: أَلَا لَهُ الْخَلْقُ وَالْأَمْرُ تَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِينَ — 'His is the creation AND the command': most comprehensive divine-sovereignty declaration in B

v.96: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرَى آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ بَرَكَاتٍ — 'if only the cities believed and feared → blessings from heaven and earth': Quran's most explicit conditional-prosperity verse, placed at the surah's structural pivot

v.99: أَفَأَمِنُوا مَكْرَ اللَّهِ فَلَا يَأْمَنُ مَكْرَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ — 'no one feels secure from God's plan except the losing people': the surah's most arresting theological warning, within the pivot

v.102: وَمَا وَجَدْنَا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ مِنْ عَهْدٍ — 'We found most of them without any covenant': the pivot's devastating verdict on humanity after five destroyed nations; answered by C′'s mercy declaration

v.143: لَنْ تَرَانِي — Moses asks to see God → mountain leveled by God's تَجَلِّي → Moses falls unconscious → سُبْحَانَكَ → 'I repent → first of believers': Quran's only theophany scene; vision denied becomes faith affirmed

v.156: وَرَحْمَتِي وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ — 'My mercy encompasses ALL THINGS': Quran's most comprehensive mercy declaration; universal scope channeled to the specific (those who follow the unlettered Prophet). Direct answer to v.102's 'no covenant found'

vv.157–158: الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الرَّسُولَ النَّبِيَّ الْأُمِّيَّ الَّذِي يَجِدُونَهُ مَكْتُوبًا عِنْدَهُمْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ → قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعًا: the unlettered Prophet found written in Torah+Gospel; 'O mankind, to you ALL' — placed within Moses narrative, linking Mosaic covenant to universal mission
v.172: أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قَالُوا بَلَى — Quran's primordial covenant: pre-temporal testimony extracted from ALL descendants of Adam before creation; grounds all four يَا بَنِي آدَمَ addresses in B
v.180: وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى فَادْعُوهُ بِهَا — foundational verse for the concept of God's Beautiful Names
v.199: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجَاهِلِينَ — Quran's most concise three-part ethical imperative; traditionally considered the surah's practical summary

v.206: SAJDA verse — 'those near Your Lord not prevented by arrogance from worship → they prostrate': closes the sajda-arc; arrogance (Iblīs, v.11) → prostration (righteous, v.206); اسْتَكْبَرُوا ×9 thread resolved
cross_surah_flags: Q.2:30–39/Q.15:26–44/Q.17:61–65/Q.18:50/Q.20:115–123/Q.38:71–85 — Adam/Iblīs thread. Q.7:11–25 is the most detailed account; only here does Iblīs specify the four-direction strategy (v.17) and predict human ingratitude.
Q.11:25–95 — prophetic-cycle parallel. Q.7:59–93 and Q.11 share the same five-nation sequence; Q.7 is more formulaic, Q.11 adds Noah's son and Abraham's intercession.
Q.20:9–98/Q.26:10–68/Q.28:3–43 — Moses parallel. Q.7:103–171 is the Quran's most comprehensive single-surah Moses narrative.
Q.2:63/Q.4:154 — mountain raised over them as a canopy: Q.7:171 is the most detailed account.
Q.17:110 — الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى: Q.7:180 and Q.17:110 share the foundational formula.
Q.2:65/Q.4:47 — sabbath-breakers → apes: Q.7:163–166 is the most detailed account.
Q.10:109/Q.12:80 — خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ: Q.7:87 (Shuʿayb), Q.10:109, Q.12:80. Three surahs share the formula.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (73/75, 97.3%).

pivot.verse_ar: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا غَنِمْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُ … يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ … لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا

signals.thematic: Four-verse pivot: v.41 (خُمُس legislation: one-fifth of war gains for God/Messenger/near relatives/orphans/needy/traveler → on يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ — Day of Criterion — when the two armies met → God over all قَدِير) → v.42 (Badr's geography: you on near side / they on far / caravan below → if you'd arranged: would have missed the encounter → لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا: God accomplishes what was already destined → perish upon evidence / live upon evidence) → v.43 (God showed them few in your dream → if many: lost courage + disputed → God saved → عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ) → v.44 (when you met: each side saw the other as few → لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ×2 → to God all matters return). The theological key: Badr was not a human achievement but a pre-destined divine accomplishment — positions, dream, mutual perceptions all orchestrated.
signals.lexical: الْفُرْقَان (v.41 — echoes v.29: فُرْقَانًا given to the righteous). مَفْعُولًا ×2 (vv.42/44: 'already destined/accomplished' — the double repetition within four verses emphasizes inevitability). بَيِّنَة ×2 (v.42: 'upon evidence' — both perishing and living happen on evidence). ذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ (v.43 — echoes v.11: hearts steadied before battle).
signals.mirror: D (4v) at offset +0.060. Five-block ring (A/B/D/B′/A′). Flanked by B (21v: pre-battle theology) and B′ (27v: post-battle legislation). The pivot sits between the theological preparation (B) and the institutional consequences (B′) — it is the event itself.
signals.discourse: v.41: legislative (خُمُس) + conditional (if you believed). vv.42–44: narrative-theological (three perspectives on Badr's orchestration: geographic, psychological, perceptual). Two modes in four verses.
signals.rhyme: قَدِيرٌ / مَفْعُولًا / عَلِيمٌ / الصُّدُورِ / الْأُمُورُ — mixed [-iːr/-iːm/-ūr], the surah's most varied cluster at the pivot.

block_map.label: الأَنْفَال for God + Messenger; true believers: heart-fear + faith-increase + reliance (vv.2–4) → الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ×1 (v.4); فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ قَتَلَهُمْ (v.17, most radical divine-agency); God with the believers (v.19)

respond to what gives you LIFE (v.24); God intervenes between man + his heart (v.24); فُرْقَانًا for the righteous (v.29); وَيَمْكُرُ اللَّهُ → خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ (v.30 = Q.3:54); fight until no فِتْنَة → religion entirely for God (v.39); God your مَوْلَى (v.40)

PIVOT (+0.060) — خُمُس legislation (v.41) + يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ; BADR ORCHESTRATION: لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ×2 (vv.42/44); dream-vision (v.43: عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ); mutual perception → God accomplishes what was already destined

stand firm + God with the patient (v.46); God won't change a FAVOR until they change themselves (v.53 → Q.13:11); prepare whatever strength you can (v.60); if incline to peace → incline to it (v.61); God united hearts (v.63, human-impossible); ratio: 1:10 → 1:2 (vv.65–66); captives legislation (v.67)

WALĀYA LEGISLATION: believers = allies of one another (v.72) / disbelievers = allies of one another (v.73); الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ×2 (v.74, echoes v.4); أُولُو الْأَرْحَامِ أَوْلَى (v.75, blood relations in God's decree)

inclusios.note: v.4 (A: true believers = heart-fear + faith-increase + reliance → forgiveness + noble provision) ↔ v.74 (A′: true believers = emigration + striving + sheltering → forgiveness + noble provision). Opens and closes on the identical declaration with different definitions: spiritual (A) → institutional (A′).
Two occurrences within the four-verse pivot — the double repetition emphasizes pre-destination. Badr's outcome was accomplished before it happened.
v.19 (God with believers) → v.46 (God with the patient) → v.66 (God with the steadfast). Thread across A→B′→B′.
v.29 (فُرْقَانًا: criterion given to the righteous) → v.41 (يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ: Badr named the Day of Criterion). The criterion God gives believers (B) is the same word naming the battle (D).

special_features: v.17: فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ قَتَلَهُمْ وَمَا رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ رَمَى — 'you didn't kill → God killed; you didn't throw → God threw': the Quran's most radical divine-agency verse; all human military action at Badr attributed directly to God

v.24: يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِ — 'God intervenes between a man and his heart': the Quran's most intimate divine-intervention declaration; God is closer to the believer than the believer's own heart

v.30: وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ — 'they plan + God plans → God is the best of planners': the Mecca plotters' plan to kill/restrain/exile the Prophet met with God's counter-plan; = Q.3:54 EXACT

v.41: خُمُس — one-fifth of war gains distributed to six categories (God/Messenger/near relatives/orphans/needy/traveler): the foundational war-booty distribution verse; يَوْمَ الْفُرْقَانِ names Badr as the Day of Criterion

vv.42/44: لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ×2 — 'so God might accomplish what was already destined': the double repetition within four verses is unique in the Quran; Badr's geographic positions, the dream-vision, and the mutual perception were all divinely arranged so that the destined outcome could occur on evidence
v.53: God won't change a FAVOR bestowed on a people until they change what is in themselves — the favor-version of Q.13:11's condition-change principle; two surahs, same principle, different object
v.61: وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَهَا — 'if they incline to peace → incline to it': the Quran's most explicit peace-inclination verse; placed inside the war-legislation block

v.63: وَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ لَوْ أَنْفَقْتَ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا مَا أَلَّفْتَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ أَلَّفَ — 'He united their hearts: if you spent all on earth → couldn't have united → but God united': community-building declared humanly impossible, divinely accomplished
vv.65–66: ratio adjustment: 20 overcome 200 (1:10) → 100 overcome 200 (1:2); God lightened the burden with explicit acknowledgment of human weakness
vv.72–75: walāya legislation — the Quran's most concentrated walāya passage: believers = allies of one another / disbelievers = allies of one another; الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ×2 closes the surah
cross_surah_flags: Q.13:11: condition-change parallel. Q.8:53 (God won't change a FAVOR until they change themselves) ↔ Q.13:11 (God won't change a CONDITION). Same principle, different object.
Q.3:54: خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ EXACT. Q.8:30 = Q.3:54. Two surahs sharing the divine-planner formula.
Q.3:123–127: Badr parallel. Q.8 (primary: divine agency, full orchestration) ↔ Q.3:123–127 (secondary: compressed).
Q.9:1–28: consecutive Medinan combat. Q.8 (Badr) → Q.9 (treaty + Tabuk). Traditionally so connected that Q.9 has no بسملة.
Q.59:7: distribution parallel. Q.8:41 (خُمُس: war gains → 6 categories) ↔ Q.59:7 (فَيْء: evacuated property → same 6 categories). Same beneficiaries, different source.
Q.47:4: combat legislation parallel. Q.8:67 (captives only after inflicting massacre) ↔ Q.47:4 (strike necks → bind → ransom or grace). Two phases of the same legislation.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (127/129, 98.4%). No basmalah. الْمُشْرِكِينَ opens → الْعَظِيمِ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جَاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ … فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ يَلْقَوْنَهُ … إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ

signals.thematic: Eight-verse pivot: v.73 (O Prophet, strive against disbelievers + hypocrites + be harsh → Hell) → v.74 (swore they didn't say it → said word of disbelief after Islam → planned without attaining → resented enrichment → repent: better / turn away: punishment in both worlds → no ally) → vv.75–76 (covenanted: 'if given bounty → we'll give charity' → given → stingy → turned away) → v.77 (فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ يَلْقَوْنَهُ: hypocrisy as divine PENALTY for broken promise + lying — permanent until Meeting Day) → v.78 (didn't they know God knows secrets + عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ?) → v.79 (God ridicules those who ridicule generous/poor givers) → v.80 (seventy times won't forgive → disbelieved → فَاسِقُون). The center names the mechanism: broken promises → نِفَاق implanted in hearts by God.
signals.lexical: نِفَاقًا (v.77: hypocrisy as divine penalty — the surah's defining concept at its structural center). عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ (v.78: unique — 'supreme Knower of the unseen'). سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً (v.80: seventy times — hyperbolic impossibility). فَاسِقِينَ (v.80: echoes vv.8/24/53/67/96 — thread closes at pivot). كَذَّبُوا (v.77: 'lied' — the act that triggers the penalty).
signals.mirror: D (8v) at offset +0.089. Six-block ring. Flanked by C (35v: Tabuk + hypocrites during campaign) and C′ (30v: aftermath + sorting). The pivot explains WHY C's hypocrites have sealed hearts and WHY C′'s sorting is permanent: نِفَاق is divine penalty, not mere human choice.
signals.discourse: Seven discourse modes in eight verses: v.73 divine command (يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ) → v.74 narrative exposure → vv.75–76 covenant narrative → v.77 divine declaration → v.78 rhetorical question → v.79 social critique → v.80 finality (seventy times → never). Rapid alternation.
signals.rhyme: Mixed [-iːr/-ūn/-iːn/-ūb/-iːm] — the surah's most varied terminals at the pivot: الْمَصِيرُ / نَصِيرٍ / الصَّالِحِينَ / مُعْرِضُونَ / يَكْذِبُونَ / الْغُيُوبِ / أَلِيمٌ / الْفَاسِقِينَ.

block_map.label: بَرَاءَةٌ (NO بسملة — only surah); four months; fight if don't repent/pray/zakāh → brothers (v.11); Ḥunayn + سَكِينَة (v.26); eight beloved things vs. God (v.24); polytheists: نَجَس (v.28)

Fight People of Book → جِزْيَة; 'son of God' claims; يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُطْفِئُوا نُورَ اللَّهِ → God perfects (v.32); manifest over ALL religion (v.33 = Q.61:9); hoarding → branded; twelve months/four sacred

TABUK + HYPOCRITES ①: 'cling to earth'; CAVE: ثَانِي اثْنَيْن → لَا تَحْزَنْ → كَلِمَة اللَّه العُلْيَا (v.40); pardoned you (v.43); صَدَقَات: 8 categories (v.60); hypocrites: enjoin wrong ↔ believers: enjoin right (vv.67/71 mirror); رِضْوَانٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ (v.72)

PIVOT (+0.089) — strive against disbelievers + hypocrites (v.73); word of disbelief after Islam (v.74); broken covenant → stingy (vv.75–76); نِفَاق as DIVINE PENALTY in hearts until Meeting Day (v.77); عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ (v.78); seventy times → God won't forgive (v.80)

TABUK AFTERMATH + HYPOCRITES ②: 'Hell hotter'; don't pray over dead; السَّابِقُونَ الأَوَّلُونَ: Muhājirīn + Anṣār → الفَوْز (v.100); acknowledged sins → غَفُور (v.102); صَدَقَة purifies (v.103); مَسْجِد الضِّرَار vs. mosque on تَقْوَى (vv.107–108)

God PURCHASED lives/wealth → Paradise (Torah+Gospel+Quran, v.111); nine-attribute portrait (v.112); don't seek forgiveness for polytheists; Abraham: أَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ (v.114); God accepted Prophet/Muhājirīn/Anṣār: رَءُوف رَحِيم (v.117); THREE LEFT BEHIND → تَوَّاب (v.118); كُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ (v.119); Messenger: رَءُوف رَحِيم (v.128); حَسْبِيَ اللَّه → رَبُّ العَرْشِ العَظِيمِ (v.129)

inclusios.note: v.1 (بَرَاءَةٌ: disassociation) → v.118 (God accepted repentance → التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ). The surah named 'Repentance' opens on severing and closes on restoration.
vv.72/89/100/111: 'the great attainment' for believers, the striving community, the forerunners, and those God purchased. The surah's recurring eschatological reward.
v.117 (God to Prophet/Muhājirīn/Anṣār) → v.128 (Prophet to believers). Same attribute pair: God's mercy → Prophet mirrors it.
v.67 (hypocrites: enjoin wrong/forbid right/close hands/forgot God) ↔ v.71 (believers: enjoin right/forbid wrong/prayer/zakāh/obey). Every clause reversed.
v.26 (سَكِينَة at Ḥunayn) + v.40 (سَكِينَة in the cave). Two deployments in one surah — God's tranquility in battle and in flight.

special_features: The ONLY surah without بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيمِ — opens directly with بَرَاءَةٌ (disassociation). The absence of the mercy-formula is the surah's opening statement. The mercy-pair returns at the close (v.128: رَءُوف رَحِيم).

v.40 — the cave of Thawr: ثَانِيَ اثْنَيْنِ إِذْ هُمَا فِي الْغَارِ — 'one of two, when they were in the cave, saying to his companion: Don't grieve, God is with us.' The Quran's only explicit reference to the Hijra cave — placed in a rebuke about Tabuk.
v.60 — eight zakāh categories as فَرِيضَة مِنَ اللَّهِ: the foundational verse of all Islamic zakāh jurisprudence — eight categories legislated in a single verse.

v.67 ↔ v.71 — the hypocrite/believer mirror: the most architecturally precise moral inversion in the surah. Every clause reversed clause-by-clause: enjoin wrong↔right, close hands↔give, forgot God↔obey.

v.77 — نِفَاق as divine penalty: فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ يَلْقَوْنَهُ — 'He penalized them with hypocrisy in their hearts until the Day they meet Him.' The Quran's most theologically radical statement about hypocrisy: it is not merely a human condition but a divine consequence of broken promises.

v.80 — seventy times won't forgive: اسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ أَوْ لَا تَسْتَغْفِرْ … إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ — the absolute impossibility of forgiveness for those whose hypocrisy is divinely penalized.

v.111 — God's purchase: إِنَّ اللَّهَ اشْتَرَى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ — the Quran's most radical transactional metaphor; confirmed in Torah + Gospel + Quran; 'who is truer to his covenant than God?'

v.112 — nine-attribute believer portrait: the Quran's longest single-verse believer-attribute list: repentant / worshipping / praising / traveling / bowing / prostrating / enjoining right / forbidding wrong / guarding God's limits.
v.118 — the three left behind: the most detailed Quranic repentance account — earth confined + souls confined → 'no refuge except TO God' → accepted → التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ.
v.119 — كُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ: 'be with the truthful' — the surah's ethical climax after 119 verses exposing liars.
v.128 — Prophet as رَءُوف رَحِيم: the only verse applying the divine mercy-attribute pair directly to the Prophet; mirrors v.117 (God to community).
cross_surah_flags: Q.8: consecutive combat surahs. Q.8 (Badr) → Q.9 (treaty+Tabuk). Traditional view: Q.9 has no بسملة because it continues Q.8 as one revelation.
Q.61:9: لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ EXACT. Q.9:33 = Q.61:9 — identical 'manifest over all religion' formula in two surahs.
Q.48:4/18/26: سَكِينَة thread. Q.9:26 (Ḥunayn) + Q.9:40 (cave) + Q.48 (Ḥudaybiyyah). Three deployments of divine tranquility across military contexts.
Q.11:75: أَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ thread. Q.9:114 ↔ Q.11:75 — Abraham's rare attribute pair in both surahs.
Q.3:169–170: martyrdom thread. Q.9:111 (fight → Paradise as purchase) extends Q.3:169 (those killed in God's way are alive with their Lord).
Q.63: hypocrite parallel. Q.9 (60+ verses exposing hypocrites) ↔ Q.63 (11-verse concentrated portrait). Q.9 = comprehensive; Q.63 = concentrated.
Q.2:245: divine-loan/purchase thread. Q.9:111 (God purchased) ↔ Q.2:245 (God as loan recipient). Same counterparty metaphor: purchase vs. loan.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (107/109, 98.2%). الْحَكِيمِ opens → الْحَاكِمِينَ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُمْ مَوْعِظَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَشِفَاءٌ لِمَا فِي الصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ … أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ … لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ

signals.thematic: Fourteen-verse pivot: v.57 (يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ — Quran's four-attribute self-description: instruction + healing for what is in the breasts + guidance + mercy for believers) → v.58 ('in God's bounty and mercy — let them rejoice → better than what they accumulate') → v.59 (who permitted the unlawful?) → v.60 (what will inventors think on Day?) → v.61 (no atom's weight absent — all in كِتَاب مُبِين) → v.62 (أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ — God's allies: no fear, no grief) → v.63 (believed + feared God) → v.64 (لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ — no change in God's words = the great attainment) → v.65 ('honor belongs to God entirely') → v.66 (to God belongs all in heavens/earth) → vv.67–68 (night/day signs → 'God has taken a son' → سُبْحَانَهُ → الْغَنِيُّ) → vv.69–70 (inventors won't succeed → worldly enjoyment → to Us their return). Center = what the Quran IS, who benefits, and what cannot change.
signals.lexical: مَوْعِظَة + شِفَاء + هُدًى + رَحْمَة (v.57) — four-attribute Quran self-description; the most complete in a single verse. أَوْلِيَاء اللَّهِ (v.62) — foundational wilāya verse; لَا خَوْفٌ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ is the Quran's standard assurance for believers (×many). لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ (v.64) — unique deployment; echoes الْحَقّ master root (×12+). الْغَنِيُّ (v.68) — 'the Self-Sufficient'; contrast with everything in heavens/earth belonging to Him.
signals.mirror: D (14v) at offset +0.078. Five-block ring (A/B/D/B′/A′). Flanked by B (31v: cosmic axioms + judgment) and B′ (33v: Noah + Moses/Pharaoh + Jonah). Center mediates: B establishes the theoretical framework (truth/error binary) → D declares the Quran's function and its recipients' security → B′ demonstrates through narrative.
signals.discourse: v.57: direct address (يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ) → v.58: qul command → vv.59–60: interrogative → v.61: declarative (God witnesses all) → v.62: أَلَا declaration → v.63: relative clause portrait → v.64: لَا تَبْدِيلَ declaration → v.65: qul command → v.66: declarative → v.67: interrogative sign-pointing → v.68: sana declaration + refutation → vv.69–70: declarative. Nine discourse modes in fourteen verses.
signals.rhyme: The pivot's dominant terminals: يَحْزَنُونَ / يَتَّقُونَ / الْمُؤْمِنِينَ / يَعْلَمُونَ / يَسْمَعُونَ / يَعْلَمُونَ — consistently nasal [-ūn/-iːn], maintaining the surah's dominant pattern while concentrating the densest theological vocabulary in the corpus.

block_map.label: الر + آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْحَكِيمِ; creation in six days → Throne; Paradise liturgy (v.10: سُبْحَانَكَ → سَلَام → الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ, unique); worldly-life parable (v.24, rain → harvest); وَاللَّهُ يَدْعُو إِلَى دَارِ السَّلَامِ (v.25)

الْحُسْنَى وَزِيَادَةٌ (v.26); فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلَالُ (v.32); Quran = confirmation + تَفْصِيلَ (v.37); 'can you cause the deaf to hear?' (v.42); إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ (v.44); every أُمَّة has a messenger; promise + ransom

PIVOT (+0.078) — Quran: مَوْعِظَة + شِفَاء + هُدًى + رَحْمَة (v.57, four-attribute); 'rejoice in God's bounty' (v.58); أَوْلِيَاء اللَّهِ: no fear/grief (v.62); لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ (v.64); عِزَّةٌ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا (v.65); 'God taken a son?' → الْغَنِيُّ (v.68)

NOAH (compressed, v.71); MOSES/PHARAOH (detailed): magicians → Moses' prayer → sea → 'I believe!' → آلْآنَ (v.91) → body preserved as sign (v.92); قَوْمَ يُونُسَ: ONLY saved city (v.98, title reference); 'would you compel people?' (v.99); نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ: obligation upon Us (v.103)

يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَكُمُ الْحَقُّ (v.108, echo of v.57); وَمَا أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِوَكِيلٍ (v.108); اتَّبِعْ مَا يُوحَى + اصْبِرْ حَتَّى يَحْكُمَ اللَّهُ → خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ (v.109, closing)

inclusios.note: v.57 (D pivot: 'instruction + healing + guidance + mercy has come to you') ↔ v.108 (A′: 'the truth has come from your Lord'). Two mankind-addresses: pivot declares the Quran's nature; close declares its consequence (guided for himself / astray against himself).
v.1 (آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْحَكِيمِ: the Wise Book) → v.109 (خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ: best of judges). Opens on divine wisdom → closes on divine judgment. Same root, two attributes.
Surah's master root: v.32 (فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلَالُ) → v.108 (قَدْ جَاءَكُمُ الْحَقُّ). Truth threads every block.
v.3 (A: no intercessor except by permission) ↔ v.108 (A′: 'I am not over you a وَكِيل'). The surah opens denying unauthorized intercession and closes denying the Prophet a managerial role.

special_features: v.10 — the only Quranic description of Paradise's liturgy in sequence: 'their call: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ + their greeting: سَلَام + their last call: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ.' Three acts of worship in Paradise, named in order — the only complete Paradise-worship sequence in the Quran
v.25: وَاللَّهُ يَدْعُو إِلَى دَارِ السَّلَامِ — 'God invites to the Home of Peace': the only use of دَارُ السَّلَام as a name for Paradise (vs. جَنَّة); God Himself described as inviting

v.44: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ النَّاسَ شَيْئًا وَلَكِنَّ النَّاسَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ — 'God doesn't wrong people at all — people wrong themselves': the Quran's most direct divine-justice / human-self-wronging declaration

v.57: Quran described in four attributes simultaneously: مَوْعِظَة (instruction/admonition) + شِفَاء لِمَا فِي الصُّدُور (healing for what is in the breasts) + هُدًى (guidance) + رَحْمَة (mercy) — the most complete single-verse Quran self-description
v.62: أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ — the foundational wilāya verse; أَلَا (unquestionably) marks it as the surah's most emphatic declaration

v.64: لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ — 'no change in God's words': the Quran's most explicit statement of divine-word immutability; ذَلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ — 'that is the great attainment'

vv.90–92: Pharaoh's deathbed declaration — 'I believe! No deity but what Israel believes! I am of the Muslims!' → God: 'NOW? You had disobeyed before!' → 'Today We save you IN BODY as a sign for those after.' The most dramatically timed faith-declaration in the Quran; Pharaoh's body preserved as a historical sign

v.98: فَلَوْلَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ آمَنَتْ فَنَفَعَهَا إِيمَانُهَا إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ — 'has there been a city whose belief benefited it EXCEPT Jonah's people?': the title surah's namesake appears in ONE VERSE as the single exception to the rule of rejected repentance
v.99: أَفَأَنْتَ تُكْرِهُ النَّاسَ — 'would you then compel people?': the Quran's most direct non-compulsion interrogative; framed as rhetorical challenge
v.103: نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ — 'saving believers is an obligation upon Us (حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا)': the Quran's most explicit divine-obligation toward believers
cross_surah_flags: Q.17:82: شِفَاء parallel. Q.10:57 (Quran = شِفَاء لِمَا فِي الصُّدُور) ↔ Q.17:82 (Quran = شِفَاء وَرَحْمَة for believers). Two self-descriptions: Q.10 = healing for what is in the breasts; Q.17 = healing for believers.
Q.39:53: لَا تَقْنَطُوا / Jonah parallel. Q.10:98 (Jonah's people: only saved city) = proof that Q.39:53's 'don't despair of God's mercy' operates. Jonah's people are the living demonstration.
Q.11:96–99: Moses/Pharaoh parallel. Q.10:75–92 (most detailed Pharaoh-drowning account) ↔ Q.11:96–99 (compressed). Q.10 preserves the 'NOW?' and body-preserved details absent from Q.11.
Q.21:87–88: Jonah thread. Q.10:98 (Jonah's PEOPLE saved) ↔ Q.21:87–88 (Jonah the PROPHET saved). Two angles: Q.10 = communal repentance; Q.21 = prophetic prayer.
Q.11:7: six days + Throne. Q.10:3 (six days + Throne + يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ) ↔ Q.11:7 (same creation formula + أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا). Consecutive surahs with near-identical openings.
Q.16:69 / Q.17:82: شِفَاء spectrum. Q.10:57 (Quran = شِفَاء لِلصُّدُور) ↔ Q.16:69 (honey = شِفَاء لِلنَّاسِ) ↔ Q.17:82 (Quran = شِفَاء وَرَحْمَة). Three healings: spiritual / biological / revelatory.

surahs.rhyme_type: Mixed [-iːd/-iːr/-iːn/-ūd] dominant (121/123, 98.4%).

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ … يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ … لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ آوِي إِلَى رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ

signals.thematic: Twenty-three-verse pivot: Ṣāliḥ/Thamūd (vv.61–68): she-camel → three days → shriek → بُعْدًا لِثَمُودَ. Abraham (vv.69–76): guests with good tidings of Isaac/Jacob → wife laughs → 'amazing!' → يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (v.74) → 'Abraham: forbearing, grieving, returning' (v.75) → 'give up this plea → command has come'. Lot (vv.77–83): messengers arrive → Lot anguished → 'trying day' → people hastened → 'my daughters → purer' → لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً (v.80) → 'travel by night → appointment: morning' → rained stones of سِجِّيل. The center places Abraham's hospitality and intercession alongside Lot's helplessness — mercy attempting to mediate between promise (Isaac/Jacob) and punishment (Lot's city). Mercy and judgment physically meet at the same table.
signals.lexical: حَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ (v.75) — the Abrahamic triad: forbearing / grieving / returning. أَوَّاه unique in this combination (also Q.9:114). يُجَادِلُنَا (v.74) — the verb 'argues/disputes with Us' applied to Abraham interceding; one of very few Quranic instances of a prophet debating divine decree. بُعْدًا لِثَمُودَ (v.68) closes N2 portion of the pivot; بُعْدًا formula ×3 total (vv.60/68/95). لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً (v.80) — Lot's vulnerability: the prophet wishing for human strength he doesn't have.
signals.mirror: D (23v) at offset +0.081. Six-block ring (A/N1/N2/D/N3/A′). Flanked by N2 (Hūd/ʿĀd: 11v) and N3 (Shuʿayb/Madyan + Moses: 16v). The pivot is the narrative and theological center of the six-nation cycle — the longest and most emotionally complex unit, containing both the shortest (Thamūd) and most tender (Abraham/Lot) narratives.
signals.discourse: Thamūd (prophetic address → hamstring → command). Abraham (narrative → dialogue → divine speech → divine description of Abraham). Lot (narrative → dialogue → angelic revelation → command → execution). Three consecutive sub-narratives, each with different discourse signatures, unified by the shared theme of divine decision meeting human response.
signals.rhyme: The pivot maintains the surah's mixed rhyme but concentrates the emotionally charged vocabulary: قَرِيبٌ مُجِيبٌ (v.61) / الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيزُ (v.66) / حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ (v.73) / أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ (v.75) / رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ (v.80). The rhyme-words trace an arc from God's nearness through His power through His majesty through Abraham's grief to Lot's vulnerability.

block_map.label: الر + أُحْكِمَتْ آيَاتُهُ ثُمَّ فُصِّلَتْ; worship only God; لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا (v.7, = Q.18:7/Q.67:2); patience → great reward; 'bring ten surahs'; is one on clear evidence = one in Fire?

NOAH (25v, most detailed): 'clear warner'; eminent ridicule; 'build the ship'; بِسْمِ اللَّهِ مَجْرَاهَا (v.41); يَا أَرْضُ ابْلَعِي مَاءَكِ (v.44, literary apex); 'your son is NOT your family — عَمَلٌ غَيْرُ صَالِحٍ' (v.46); فَاصْبِرْ → الْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (v.49)
HŪD/ʿĀD (11v): 'no creature but He holds its forelock' (v.56); saved by mercy; بُعْدًا لِعَادٍ (v.60)

PIVOT (+0.081) — ṢĀLIḤ/THAMŪD: she-camel → three days → shriek → بُعْدًا لِثَمُودَ; ABRAHAM: guests → Isaac/Jacob tidings → wife laughs → يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (v.74) → حَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ (v.75); LOT: anguished → 'trying day' → لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً (v.80) → stones of سِجِّيل. Mercy and judgment at one table.

SHUʿAYB/MADYAN (12v): 'give full measure'; بَقِيَّتُ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ (v.86, unique); إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا الْإِصْلَاحَ (v.88); رَحِيمٌ وَدُودٌ (v.90); بُعْدًا لِمَدْيَنَ (v.95); MOSES/PHARAOH compressed (4v)

شَقِيٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ (v.105); عَطَاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ (v.108); فَاسْتَقِمْ كَمَا أُمِرْتَ (v.112, = Q.42:15); الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ (v.114); وَلِذَلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ (v.119); نُثَبِّتُ بِهِ فُؤَادَكَ (v.120); وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ السَّمَاوَاتِ + فَاعْبُدْهُ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَيْهِ (v.123, closing)

inclusios.note: v.1 (Book: perfected then detailed) ↔ v.120 (Book's purpose: truth + instruction + reminder). A declares the Book's quality; A′ declares its effect.
vv.58/66/94: identical salvation formula for Hūd, Ṣāliḥ, Shuʿayb. The same divine rescue pattern applied three times — prophetic salvation by mercy is a structural law.
vv.60/68/95: 'away with ʿĀd / Thamūd / Madyan.' Three identical dismissal formulas — the destroyed nations are named and expelled with the same phrase.
vv.17/28/63/88: 'on clear evidence from my Lord' — the opening + Noah + Ṣāliḥ + Shuʿayb. The prophetic credential formula threads all four major units.
Three surahs sharing the identical test formula.

special_features: يَا أَرْضُ ابْلَعِي مَاءَكِ وَيَا سَمَاءُ أَقْلِعِي (v.44) — 'O earth, swallow your water! O sky, withhold!': universally recognized as the Quran's literary apex — direct divine address to creation as if creatures; the most compressed narrative command in Arabic literature

'your son is NOT of your family — his deed was unrighteous' (v.46): the Quran's most radical redefinition of family — Noah's son excluded from the promised family because his deeds define him. Kinship is by alignment, not blood

حَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ (v.75) — the Abrahamic triad: forbearing / deeply grieving / constantly returning. The most compressed three-attribute portrait of Abraham in the Quran; أَوَّاه appears only here and Q.9:114

يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (v.74) — 'he began to argue with Us about Lot's people': Abraham as intercessor who debates divine decree; one of the Quran's most remarkable instances of prophetic advocacy

لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ آوِي إِلَى رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ (v.80) — 'if only I had power or could take refuge in a strong support': Lot's expression of helplessness; the Prophet reportedly said: 'God have mercy on Lot — he had a strong support' (i.e., God Himself)
إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا الْإِصْلَاحَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ (v.88) — 'I intend only reform as much as I can': Shuʿayb's mission statement; the most modest prophetic self-description
بَقِيَّتُ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (v.86) — 'what God leaves you is better': the only Quranic deployment of this phrase; Shuʿayb on honest commerce

فَاسْتَقِمْ كَمَا أُمِرْتَ (v.112) — the Prophet reportedly said this verse 'whitened my hair' from its weight: the command to remain on the straight path 'as you have been commanded' — without deviation, without rest
إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ (v.114) — 'good deeds erase misdeeds': among the most practically cited verses in Islamic ethics
وَلِذَلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ (v.119) — 'for that He created them': one of the most debated Quranic phrases; 'that' referring to mercy (in context) or to difference/disagreement; the creation purpose verse
نُثَبِّتُ بِهِ فُؤَادَكَ (v.120) — 'to make firm your heart': the entire prophetic-destruction cycle (Noah through Moses) is explicitly declared to be for the Prophet's psychological strengthening

Six-nation prophetic-destruction cycle: the most sustained multi-prophetic narrative in the Quran (Noah → Hūd → Ṣāliḥ → Abraham/Lot → Shuʿayb → Moses); salvation formula ×3 + dismissal formula ×3 create structural symmetry across nations
cross_surah_flags: Q.18:7 / Q.67:2: أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا EXACT. Q.11:7 (test: best in deed) ↔ Q.18:7 / Q.67:2. Three surahs sharing the identical purpose-of-creation test formula.

Q.7:59–102: parallel prophetic-cycle. Q.11:25–99 ↔ Q.7:59–102 — same sequence (Noah/Hūd/Ṣāliḥ/Lot/Shuʿayb), but Q.11 adds Noah-son episode, Abraham's intercession, Shuʿayb's إِصْلَاح declaration — all absent from Q.7.
Q.26: prophetic-cycle parallel. Q.11 (six nations, emotional depth) ↔ Q.26 (seven nations, formulaic refrain). Q.11 = depth; Q.26 = structure.
Q.9:114: أَوَّاه thread. Q.11:75 (Abraham = أَوَّاه) ↔ Q.9:114 (Abraham = أَوَّاه). Both in Abrahamic intercession contexts.
Q.42:15: فَاسْتَقِمْ EXACT. Q.11:112 ↔ Q.42:15 — same straightness command in two surahs.
Q.12:56/90: الْمُحْسِنِينَ reward. Q.11:115 ↔ Q.12:56/90 EXACT — consecutive surahs sharing the declaration.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (109/111, 98.2%). سَاجِدِينَ opens narrative → يُؤْمِنُونَ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: يَا صَاحِبَيِ السِّجْنِ أَأَرْبَابٌ مُتَفَرِّقُونَ خَيْرٌ أَمِ اللَّهُ الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ … وَمَا أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِي إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ … وَكَذَلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ

signals.thematic: Twenty-two-verse pivot: the surah's narrative turning point. Prison phase (vv.36–42): Joseph's daʿwah to fellow prisoners (Abrahamic مِلَّة, الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ, إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ) → interpretations (wine-presser: survives; bread-carrier: crucified) → 'mention me to your master' → forgotten for several years. King's dream (vv.43–49): seven fat/lean cows + spikes → Joseph's seven-year interpretation. Vindication (vv.50–53): king sends for Joseph → Joseph demands women questioned first → wife confesses ('I sought to seduce') → وَمَا أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِي إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ (v.53). Appointment (vv.54–57): king: 'established, trusted' → Joseph: 'appoint me over storehouses: حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ' → We established Joseph in the land → reward of المُحْسِنِينَ. Prison → power: the lowest point to the highest.
signals.lexical: الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ (v.39) — Joseph's prison daʿwah climax; echoes Q.13:16/Q.14:48/Q.38:65. إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ (v.40) — echoes v.67 (Jacob to sons: same formula). الصِّدِّيق (v.46) — Joseph's unique Quranic title (intensely truthful). النَّفْسُ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ (v.53) — the nafs ammāra concept; foundational Islamic psychology. مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ (vv.21/56: God established Joseph) — the identical phrase bookends the C-D-C′ arc. الْمُحْسِنِينَ (v.56) — appears in every block, marking Joseph's status across the surah.
signals.mirror: D (22v) at offset −0.086. Seven-block ring. Flanked by C (17v: seduction in Egypt) and C′ (25v: brothers' visits + cup stratagem). Both C and C′ involve plots (كَيْد): C = women's plot against Joseph; C′ = Joseph's divinely guided plan for his brother. The pivot sits between two كَيْد episodes: human scheming on both sides, divine plan at center.
signals.discourse: Prison daʿwah (direct theological speech: vv.37–40) → interpretation (narrative + reported speech: vv.41–42) → king's dream (reported narrative: vv.43–44) → Joseph's interpretation (prophetic speech: vv.47–49) → vindication (dialogue: vv.50–52) → النَّفْس declaration (first-person: v.53) → appointment (dialogue + divine declaration: vv.54–57). The pivot contains the surah's only sustained theological discourse — Joseph preaches tawhid in prison.
signals.rhyme: The pivot maintains nasal [-ūn/-iːn] throughout but concentrates the surah's densest theological vocabulary: الْقَهَّارُ / الْحُكْمُ / الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ / الصِّدِّيقُ / الْمُحْسِنِينَ. The pivot's opening (v.36: يُحْسِنُونَ) and close (v.57: الْمُحْسِنِينَ) form an internal inclusio.

block_map.label: الر + أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ (v.3, self-designation); THE DREAM (v.4: eleven stars + sun + moon prostrating); father: 'Lord will choose you + teach تَأْوِيل'; signs for those who ask
Brothers' plot → well; 'we raced → wolf ate him'; false blood shirt (deception); فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ×1 (v.18, Jacob's FIRST patience)

Sold for few dirhams; 'make his stay comfortable'; بُرْهَانَ رَبِّهِ averts evil (v.24); SHIRT TORN FROM BEHIND (evidence); women cut hands: 'noble angel!'; رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ (v.33); imprisoned despite signs

PIVOT (−0.086) — PRISON DAʿWAH: الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ (v.39) + إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ (v.40); interpretations → forgotten years; KING'S DREAM → seven-year plan (vv.47–49); wife confesses; النَّفْسُ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ (v.53); appointed: حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ → مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ (v.56)

Brothers arrive: Joseph recognizes / they don't; 'bring your brother'; merchandise returned; Jacob: اللَّهُ خَيْرٌ حَافِظًا (v.64); إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ (v.67, Jacob); CUP STRATAGEM → extracted from brother's bag; وَفَوْقَ كُلِّ ذِي عِلْمٍ عَلِيمٌ (v.76); eldest remains

فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ×2 (v.83, Jacob's SECOND patience); يَا أَسَفَى عَلَى يُوسُفَ; 'I complain only to God' (v.86); أَإِنَّكَ لَأَنْتَ يُوسُفُ (v.90, THE REVEAL); لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ (v.92, Joseph's forgiveness); SHIRT CAST → فَارْتَدَّ بَصِيرًا (v.96)

Parents + brothers prostrate → يَا أَبَتِ هَذَا تَأْوِيلُ رُؤْيَايَ (v.100, DREAM FULFILLED); إِنَّ رَبِّي لَطِيفٌ; تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِمًا (v.101); invite to God عَلَى بَصِيرَةٍ (v.108); لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي قَصَصِهِمْ عِبْرَةٌ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ (v.111, closing)

inclusios.note: v.4 (dream: 11 stars+sun+moon prostrating) → v.6 (Lord will teach تَأْوِيل) → D (prison dreams + king's dream interpreted) → v.100 ('this is the تَأْوِيل of my dream → my Lord made it reality'). The surah's master arc: dream → taught → practiced → fulfilled.

v.18 (B: Jacob's FIRST — after Joseph thrown in well) ↔ v.83 (B′: Jacob's SECOND — after Benjamin detained). Identical phrase: Jacob loses a son twice and responds identically. The most striking verbal repetition in the surah.
v.18 (B: Jacob accuses) ↔ v.83 (B′: Jacob accuses again). Identical accusation at B and B′ — Jacob's insight into his sons' self-deception is unchanged.
v.40 (D pivot: Joseph to prisoners) ↔ v.67 (C′: Jacob to sons). The same sovereignty declaration by father and son — the surah's theological axiom stated at pivot and in C′.

v.21 (C: 'thus We established Joseph in the land') ↔ v.56 (D: 'thus We established Joseph in the land'). Identical divine declaration: the first establishment (Egyptian household) → the full establishment (treasurer of Egypt). The C-D arc is bracketed by the same phrase.
vv.22/36/56/78/90: 'doer of good' applied to Joseph in every block. His identity across the surah's arc.

Satan's كَيْد (v.5) → brothers' كَيْد (v.5) → women's كَيْد (v.28/33) → Joseph's كَيْد (v.76: 'thus We planned/schemed for Joseph'). The word transforms from human plotting to divine stratagem at the pivot's close.

Shirt 1 (v.18: false blood — deception) → shirt 2 (v.25/28: torn from behind — evidence) → shirt 3 (v.93/96: cast on father → sight restored — healing). Three shirts encode the surah's arc: lie → truth → mercy.

special_features: The Quran's ONLY single-narrative surah — 111 verses telling one continuous story without thematic interruption; أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ (v.3): the Quran's only self-designation as 'the best of stories'

Dream-fulfillment arc: v.4 (Joseph's dream: 11 stars + sun + moon prostrating) → v.100 (parents + brothers prostrate in Egypt: 'this is the تَأْوِيل of my dream from before'). Longest dream-fulfillment arc in Quranic narrative
Three shirts: (1) false blood (v.18: deception/lie), (2) torn from behind (v.25: evidence/truth), (3) cast on Jacob's face (v.96: sight restored/mercy). The shirts encode the surah's moral arc
فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ×2 (vv.18/83): Jacob says the identical phrase after losing each son — the most striking verbal repetition in the surah; same response to different losses
النَّفْسُ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ إِلَّا مَا رَحِمَ رَبِّي (v.53): the nafs ammāra concept — spoken by Joseph after his vindication as a form of humility; foundational to Islamic psychology
الصِّدِّيق (v.46): Joseph's unique Quranic title, meaning 'the intensely truthful one'; given by the king's butler after years in prison
إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ×2 (vv.40/67): stated by Joseph to prisoners and by Jacob to his sons — father and son share the surah's theological axiom
وَفَوْقَ كُلِّ ذِي عِلْمٍ عَلِيمٌ (v.76): 'above every knowledgeable one is the All-Knowing' — the Quran's most compressed epistemological hierarchy

لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ (v.92): 'no blame upon you today' — Joseph's forgiveness of his brothers; among the Quran's most generous human-forgiveness statements; echoes the Prophet's forgiveness at Mecca
إِنَّ رَبِّي لَطِيفٌ لِمَا يَشَاءُ (v.100): 'my Lord is Subtle in what He wills' — Joseph's theological summary of his entire life; اللَّطِيف named by one who lived its meaning
تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ (v.101): at the peak of his power, Joseph prays to die as a Muslim and join the righteous — the prayer of one who has everything

لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي قَصَصِهِمْ عِبْرَةٌ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ (v.111): 'in their stories is a lesson for those of understanding' — closes the surah that opened on أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ; from 'best stories' to 'lesson in stories'
cross_surah_flags: Q.21:87: confinement prayer. Q.12:33 (Joseph: 'prison is dearer' → God responded) ↔ Q.21:87 (Jonah in darkness: 'no deity but You' → responded). Two prophets in confinement, two prayers, two responses.
Q.40:34: compressed narrative. Q.40:34 compresses Q.12's entire story into one verse: 'Joseph came with clear proofs — you doubted — God won't send after him.' Q.40 = footnote to Q.12.
Q.16:125 / Q.20:44: daʿwah methodology. Q.12:37–40 (Joseph's prison daʿwah: gentle, reasoned, Abrahamic) exemplifies Q.16:125 (حِكْمَة + مَوْعِظَة حَسَنَة) and Q.20:44 (قَوْلًا لَيِّنًا).

Q.38:26: prophetic ruler. Q.12:54–55 (Joseph appointed: 'established, trusted' → حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ) ↔ Q.38:26 (David: خَلِيفَة → judge بِالْحَقِّ). Two prophets as rulers: different paths, same divine appointment.
Q.18:7 / Q.67:2: أَحْسَن superlative. Q.12:3 (أَحْسَنَ الْقَصَصِ: best stories) ↔ Q.18:7/Q.67:2 (أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا: best in deed). Same superlative: narrative excellence ↔ ethical excellence.

surahs.rhyme_type: Mixed [-āb/-ār/-ūb/-āl] — most varied in recent corpus.

pivot.verse_ar: أَفَمَنْ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ الْحَقُّ … أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ … طُوبَى لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ

signals.thematic: Eleven-verse pivot: v.19 (seeing vs. blind → إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ) → vv.20–22 (five covenant-keeper attributes: fulfill covenant / join what God ordered / fear Lord + evil account / patient seeking His face, establish prayer, spend, repel evil with good → عُقْبَى الدَّار) → vv.23–24 (Gardens of Eden + righteous family + angels from every gate: 'سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ بِمَا صَبَرْتُمْ — peace for your patience → excellent final home') → v.25 (covenant-breakers: break covenant + cut + corrupt → curse + worst home) → v.26 (God extends/restricts provision → worldly life = enjoyment) → v.27 ('why no sign?' → guides to Himself whoever turns back) → v.28 (أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ) → v.29 (believed + righteous → طُوبَى + good return). Center moves: epistemological divide → ethical portrait → psychological resolution.
signals.lexical: عُقْبَى الدَّار ×3 concentrated at pivot (vv.22/24/25) — the surah's defining concept appears most densely here. أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ (v.19). تَطْمَئِنُّ (v.28 — root appears twice in same verse for emphasis). طُوبَى (v.29 — unique deployment). صَبَرْتُمْ (v.24: 'for your patience' — covenant-keepers' defining quality named at Paradise's gate). الْحَقّ (v.19: knowing the truth) echoes v.1 (A: revealed = الْحَقّ) and v.14 (B: دَعْوَة الْحَقّ).
signals.mirror: C (11v) at offset +0.047. Flanked by B (11v: cosmic signs, thunder, foam parable) and B′ (8v: Arabic legislation, أُمّ الكِتَاب). The cosmic truth (foam vanishes/benefit remains) and the revelatory truth (Arabic legislation) converge on the ethical and psychological truth at center.
signals.discourse: Nine different discourse modes in eleven verses: rhetorical question (v.19) → five-attribute portrait (vv.20–22) → Paradise description (v.23) → angelic direct speech (v.24) → inverse portrait (v.25) → provision statement (v.26) → قُلْ (v.27) → ontological declaration (v.28) → blessing (v.29). Most discourse-varied pivot in the project.
signals.rhyme: [-āb/-ār] dominates (الْأَلْبَاب / الْمِيثَاق / الْحِسَاب / الدَّار ×3) with الْقُلُوب prosodically distinctive at v.28 — the heart-assurance verse's terminal stands apart from the surrounding rhyme scheme.

block_map.label: المر + revealed = الْحَقّ; heavens/earth/fruits → signs; 'dust → new creation?' + shackles → Fire; forgiveness + severe; 'why no sign?' → warner + guide for every people

God knows all → everything by measure; لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا (v.11, most cited); وَيُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ (v.13, title creature glorifies); دَعْوَةُ الْحَقِّ (v.14); all prostrate → shadows; الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ (v.16); FLOOD/FOAM PARABLE (v.17): truth remains, falsehood vanishes

PIVOT (+0.047) — seeing vs. blind → أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ (v.19); COVENANT-KEEPERS (5 attributes vv.20–22) → Gardens → سَلَامٌ بِمَا صَبَرْتُمْ (v.24); COVENANT-BREAKERS (v.25, inverse → curse); أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ (v.28, unique); طُوبَى (v.29, unique)

Sent among community → الرَّحْمَان; if Quran moved mountains → to God the matter; ridiculed → prolonged → seized; Paradise: lasting fruit/shade; Arabic legislation + أَهْوَاء → no protector; أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ (v.39, only deployment)
Show what We promise or take you → بَلَاغ/حِسَاب; land reduced from edges; to God the plan → لِمَنْ عُقْبَى الدَّار (v.42); 'not a messenger' → God شَهِيد + knowledge of Scripture (v.43)

inclusios.note: vv.22/24/25 (concentrated at pivot) → v.35 (B′: righteous consequence) → v.42 (A′: for whom is the final home?). The surah's defining concept — five deployments tracking from the center to the close.
v.1 (A: revealed = truth) → v.14 (B: supplication of truth) → v.17 (B: truth/falsehood parable) → v.19 (C: knowing the truth). Truth threads from revelation through cosmic sign to knowing.
Pivot concentrates عُقْبَى الدَّار ×3; A′ asks 'for whom is the final home?' — the center answers, the close repeats the question.

special_features: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا مَا بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ (v.11) — the Quran's most cited sociological axiom; condition-change is internal before it is external
وَيُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ (v.13) — thunder glorifies with God's praise: the title creature is not a force but a worshipper; the surah's name is a divine attribute

Flood/foam parable (v.17) — two parallel examples (water carrying foam / heated ore carrying slag): truth remains on earth like residue that benefits; falsehood vanishes like foam on water. The Quran's most vivid truth/falsehood parable

أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ (v.28) — 'unquestionably, by the remembrance of God hearts are assured': the most celebrated heart-psychology verse in the corpus; hapax for تَطْمَئِنُّ applied to hearts via ذِكْر specifically
طُوبَى (v.29) — the only Quranic deployment of this word; traditionally: a tree in Paradise; linguistically: intensive بُشْرَى; the pivot's closing blessing

Five covenant-keeper attributes (vv.20–22): fulfill covenant / join what God ordered joined / fear Lord + fear evil account / patient seeking God's face + prayer + spending + repel evil with good — the most structured ethical portrait in the Meccan corpus
Covenant-keepers ↔ covenant-breakers (vv.20–24 vs. v.25) — exact inversion: five attributes → gardens/سَلَام for patience vs. break/cut/corrupt → curse. The binary is absolute.
أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ (v.39) — 'with Him is the Mother of the Book': the divine source-record; one of three deployments with different meanings across the corpus

v.31 — hypothetical Quran (aposiopesis): 'if a Quran moved mountains / broke earth / spoke to the dead' — but God's is the matter entirely; the conditional without consequence, trailing into divine sovereignty
عُقْبَى الدَّار ×5 — concentrated at pivot (×3) then echoed through B′ and A′; the surah's defining question: who inherits the final home?
cross_surah_flags: Q.14:24–26: truth/falsehood parables. Q.13:17 (foam/flood + ore: hydrological/metallurgical) ↔ Q.14:24–26 (good/bad tree: botanical). Consecutive surahs with parallel truth/falsehood parables in different natural registers.
Q.14:48 / Q.38:65 / Q.39:4 / Q.40:16: الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ thread. Q.13:16 — multiple surahs sharing the divine-name pair.
Q.22:18 / Q.16:49: universal prostration. Q.13:15 (all prostrate + shadows) ↔ Q.22:18 (sun/moon/mountains/trees) ↔ Q.16:49 (creatures/angels). Three registers: Q.13 adds shadows.
Q.3:7 / Q.43:4: أُمّ الْكِتَاب. Q.13:39 (divine source-record) ↔ Q.3:7 (clear verses) ↔ Q.43:4 (heavenly archetype). Three deployments, different meanings.
Q.17:44 / Q.24:41: glorification spectrum. Q.13:13 (thunder specifically) ↔ Q.17:44 (everything universally) ↔ Q.24:41 (birds specifically). Three registers from specific to universal to specific.

surahs.rhyme_type: Mixed [-iːd/-iːr/-iːm/-iːn] dominant (96.2%).

pivot.verse_ar: وَقَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ لَمَّا قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ … مَا كَانَ لِيَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطَانٍ إِلَّا أَنْ دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَاسْتَجَبْتُمْ … أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ … يُثَبِّتُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِالْقَوْلِ الثَّابِتِ
