signals.thematic: Seven-verse pivot: v.21 (weak → arrogant: 'no escape, we're all in it') → v.22 (Satan's confession: 'God promised truth; I promised and betrayed; I had NO AUTHORITY except invitation — don't blame me, blame yourselves; I deny your associating me') → v.23 (believers → Gardens → سَلَام) → vv.24–25 (good word = good tree: root firm, branches in sky, fruit all seasons) → v.26 (bad word = bad tree: uprooted, no stability) → v.27 (God keeps firm those who believe with the firm word; sends wrongdoers astray). The center explains the mechanism: Satan had no coercive power — only invitation. Outcome determined by rootedness (firm word) or lack thereof (uprooted).
signals.lexical: سُلْطَان (v.22: 'no authority') echoes v.11 (A: 'authority only by God's permission') — messengers' authority = divine permission; Satan's authority = none. كَلِمَة طَيِّبَة / خَبِيثَة (good/bad word) — only here applied to belief. ثَابِت (vv.24/27: firm root + firm word) — same root bridges parable to doctrinal application. وَعْدَ الْحَقِّ (v.22: God's true promise) ↔ A's mission: from darkness to light.
signals.mirror: C (7v) at offset −0.048. Five-block ring. Flanked by B (8v: tyrant failed, deeds like ashes) and B′ (7v: exchanged favor → home of ruin, can't enumerate favors). Center mediates both: why tyrants fail (no real authority) and why favor is exchanged (invitation accepted, word uprooted).
signals.discourse: v.21: narrative (dialogue of the desperate). v.22: Satan's direct speech — the most psychologically devastating self-indictment in the corpus. v.23: declarative (Paradise). vv.24–26: parable (two trees). v.27: doctrinal declaration. Widest discourse range in the surah: reported dialogue → monologue → description → parable → declaration.
signals.rhyme: مَحِيصٍ / أَلِيمٌ / سَلَامٌ / السَّمَاءِ / يَتَذَكَّرُونَ / قَرَارٍ / يَشَاءُ — most varied terminals in the surah, matching the center's discourse variety.

block_map.label: الر + مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ×2 (Muhammad v.1 + Moses v.5); أَيَّامِ اللَّهِ (v.5, only deployment); لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ (v.7); Noah/ʿĀd/Thamūd; upon God let believers rely
'Drive you out' → Lord: destroy + dwell; every tyrant failed; purulent water; deeds like ashes in stormy wind (v.18); created بِالْحَقِّ; weak → arrogant: 'no escape'

PIVOT (−0.048) — SATAN'S CONFESSION: 'no سُلْطَان except invitation — blame yourselves' (v.22); Gardens → سَلَام (v.23); TWO TREES: good word = rooted tree/fruit (vv.24–25) / bad word = uprooted (v.26); يُثَبِّتُ اللَّهُ بِالْقَوْلِ الثَّابِتِ (v.27)

Exchanged favor for disbelief → دَارَ الْبَوَارِ (home of ruin); establish prayer + spend; rain/ships/rivers/sun/moon; وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ (v.34, ↔ Q.16:18)

ABRAHAM'S PRAYERS (7× رَبّ/رَبَّنَا): city secure + idols + uncultivated valley + Sacred House + Ishmael/Isaac + prayer + forgiveness; earth replaced by another earth (v.48); الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ; هَذَا بَلَاغٌ + one God + أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ (v.52, closing)

inclusios.note: v.1 (to Muhammad: bring people from darknesses to light) ↔ v.5 (to Moses: bring your people from darknesses to light). Same mission formula for two prophets in the surah's opening.

v.11 (A: messengers' authority = God's permission only) ↔ v.22 (C: Satan's authority = none except invitation). Two deployments that together define the limits of all authority — prophetic and Satanic.

v.7 (A: 'if grateful → increased; if deny → punishment is severe') ↔ v.34 (B′: 'can't count favors → man is most unjust, ungrateful'). The surah opens on gratitude's reward and closes the inner frame on humanity's ingratitude.
Q.14:34 (can't enumerate → لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ) ↔ Q.16:18 (can't enumerate → غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ). Same formula: human diagnosis in Q.14 / divine response in Q.16.

special_features: Satan's confession (v.22) — 'I had no authority over you except that I invited you and you responded — so don't blame me, blame yourselves': the most psychologically devastating self-indictment in the Quran; Satan absolves himself by exposing the mechanism of human failure

Two tree parables (vv.24–26) — good word = good tree (roots firm, branches in sky, fruit every season) / bad word = bad tree (uprooted, no stability): the Quran's most celebrated word-as-tree metaphor; يُثَبِّتُ اللَّهُ بِالْقَوْلِ الثَّابِتِ (v.27) names the root as the 'firm word'
مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ×2 (vv.1/5) — the same darknesses-to-light mission formula applied to both Muhammad and Moses in the surah's opening block
أَيَّامِ اللَّهِ (v.5) — 'the days of God': the only Quranic deployment of this phrase; God's historical interventions named as His days
لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ (v.7) — 'if you're grateful I'll increase you': the Quran's clearest statement of gratitude as divine multiplication; stacked lām emphasis

Abraham's Mecca founding prayer (vv.35–41): 7 consecutive رَبّ/رَبَّنَا addresses; city security + idols avoided + uncultivated valley near Sacred House + hearts inclined + fruits + prayer + forgiveness on Day of account — the most architecturally detailed Abrahamic prayer

يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّمَاوَاتُ (v.48) — 'Day the earth replaced by another earth and the heavens': the Quran's most explicit cosmic-replacement verse; الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ
هَذَا بَلَاغٌ لِلنَّاسِ (v.52) — 'this is a notification/communication for people': the surah's self-referential closing; one of the Quran's most direct self-descriptions as a message
Five-block ring (A/B/C/B′/A′) — second confirmed five-block structure in the backfill (alongside Q.15)
cross_surah_flags: Q.16:18: نِعْمَة formula. Q.14:34 (→ لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ) ↔ Q.16:18 (→ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ). Same formula: Q.14 diagnoses human ingratitude; Q.16 reveals divine mercy as the response.
Q.24:35: tree metaphor. Q.14:24–26 (good/bad word = good/bad tree) ↔ Q.24:35 (olive tree in Light Verse). Two botanical metaphors for spiritual reality in the same corpus.
Q.17:65 / Q.15:42: سُلْطَان thread. Q.14:22 (Satan: 'no authority except invitation') ↔ Q.15:42/Q.17:65 ('My servants: no authority over them'). Three surahs defining the limits of Satanic power.
Q.22:26: Abraham and the House. Q.14:35–37 (Abraham's prayer for city + Sacred House) ↔ Q.22:26 (Abraham designated site). Q.14 = prayer; Q.22 = designation/command.

Q.2:126: Abraham's Mecca prayer. Q.14:35 (رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِنًا) ↔ Q.2:126 (رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَذَا بَلَدًا آمِنًا). Near-identical: Q.14 = city already established; Q.2 = being established.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn/-iːm] dominant (97/99, 98.0%). مُبِينٍ opens → الْيَقِينُ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ … نَبِّئْ عِبَادِي أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ • وَأَنَّ عَذَابِي هُوَ الْعَذَابُ الْأَلِيمُ

signals.thematic: Six-verse Paradise + dual-declaration block: v.45 (righteous in gardens and springs) → v.46 (enter in peace, secure) → v.47 (resentment removed from breasts → brothers on thrones facing each other) → v.48 (no fatigue, never expelled) → v.49 (inform My servants: I am the Forgiving, the Merciful) → v.50 (AND My punishment is the painful punishment). The center of a surah dominated by Iblīs's threat (B) and destruction narratives (B′) is the believers' Paradise and God's dual nature. Between the cosmic adversary and the historical consequences, there is rest — and the full theological picture.
signals.lexical: الْمُتَّقِينَ (v.45) echoes الْمُتَوَسِّمِينَ (v.75) and الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (v.77) in B′ — the discerning see the signs. سَلَامٍ (v.46: peace) echoes سَلَامًا (v.52: Abraham's guests). عِبَادِي (v.49) echoes عِبَادِي (v.42: 'My servants: no authority') — the same possessive in Iblīs's restraint and Paradise's recipients. الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ / الْعَذَابُ الْأَلِيمُ (vv.49–50): the dual divine declaration balances mercy and punishment at the structural center.
signals.mirror: C (6v) at offset −0.025. Five-block ring (A/B/C/B′/A′). Flanked by B (19v: Iblīs/Hell's seven gates) and B′ (34v: Abraham/Lot/Thicket/Ḥijr destructions). The center sits between the primordial adversary and the historical consequences — Paradise and the dual declaration are what's at stake on both sides.
signals.discourse: vv.45–48: descriptive (third-person: what Paradise is). v.46: imperative embedded (enter in peace). vv.49–50: command + declaration (نَبِّئْ: inform + dual divine attributes). The center moves from description of reward to declaration of divine nature — from what Paradise IS to what God IS. The dual declaration (Forgiving AND punishing) is the surah's theological answer to both Iblīs's challenge and the destructions.
signals.rhyme: وَعُيُونٍ / آمِنِينَ / مُتَقَابِلِينَ / بِمُخْرَجِينَ / الرَّحِيمُ / الْأَلِيمُ — [-ūn/-iːn] shifts to [-iːm] at vv.49–50. The prosodic transition from nasal [-iːn] to [-iːm] marks the shift from Paradise-description to divine-attribute-declaration.

block_map.label: الر + قُرْآنٍ مُبِينٍ (v.1); perhaps they'll wish they'd been Muslims; إِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ (v.9, preservation guarantee); sects + ridiculed messengers; constellations guarded; earth spread + balanced; treasuries with God → sent down بِقَدَرٍ; We are الْوَارِثُونَ

IBLĪS: صَلْصَال مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُون ×3 (v.26/28/33, narrator/God/Iblīs); مِنْ رُوحِي (v.29); all prostrated except Iblīs: 'never to clay'; reprieved; 'I'll mislead all except الْمُخْلَصِينَ'; صِرَاطٌ عَلَيَّ مُسْتَقِيمٌ (v.41); إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ (v.42 = Q.17:65); seven gates of Hell

PIVOT (−0.025) — PARADISE: gardens/springs (v.45) → enter in peace (v.46) → resentment removed from breasts → brothers on thrones facing each other (v.47) → no fatigue (v.48) → نَبِّئْ عِبَادِي: الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ (v.49) AND الْعَذَابُ الْأَلِيمُ (v.50)

ABRAHAM'S GUESTS + LOT + DESTRUCTIONS: نَبِّئْ عَنْ ضَيْفِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ (v.51); good tidings + Lot's family saved except wife; city people came rejoicing; لَعَمْرُكَ (v.72, only oath by Prophet's life); الصَّيْحَة ×2 (Lot v.73 + Ḥijr v.83); أَصْحَابُ الْأَيْكَةِ (v.78); أَصْحَابُ الْحِجْرِ (v.80, title reference)

Created بِالْحَقِّ; فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ (v.85); الْخَلَّاقُ الْعَلِيمُ; سَبْعًا مِنَ الْمَثَانِي وَالْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ (v.87, only mention); فَاصْدَعْ بِمَا تُؤْمَرُ (v.94); sufficient against mockers; فَسَبِّحْ + كُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ (v.98); وَاعْبُدْ رَبَّكَ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَكَ الْيَقِينُ (v.99, closing)

inclusios.note: v.3 (A: they're going to know) ↔ v.96 (A′: they're going to know). Identical future threat frames the surah's opening and close.
v.1 (قُرْآنٍ مُبِينٍ: clear Quran) → v.87 (الْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ: great Quran) → v.91 (divided the Quran into portions). Opens on clarity → closes on greatness.

v.49 (C: inform My servants of God's dual nature) → v.51 (B′: inform them about Abraham's guests). Two consecutive نَبِّئْ commands at the pivot's hinge: theological declaration → narrative illustration.

vv.26/28/33: same phrase from narrator (v.26), God's command (v.28), Iblīs's refusal (v.33). Adam's material named by three different voices — the only triple-repetition of a creation-material phrase.
v.42 (B: 'My servants: no authority for Iblīs') ↔ v.49 (C: 'inform My servants: I am Forgiving'). The same possessive: restraining the adversary → addressing the recipients of Paradise.

special_features: إِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ (v.9) — the Quran's most explicit self-preservation declaration: 'We sent down the ذِكْر and We will be its guardian'
صَلْصَال مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُون ×3 (vv.26/28/33) — the only Quranic repetition of Adam's creation-material by three different speakers (narrator/God/Iblīs); most detailed pre-Iblīs account
نَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي (v.29) — divine breath into Adam; the Quran's clearest statement of the breath of life as directly from God
صِرَاطٌ عَلَيَّ مُسْتَقِيمٌ (v.41) — 'a path to Me, straight': God declares His own path the straight one; unique inversion of the standard formula
إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ (v.42) = Q.17:65 EXACT — among the Quran's most decisive statements limiting Satan's power
لَعَمْرُكَ (v.72) — 'by your life': the only Quranic oath sworn by the Prophet's own life, by God; occurs mid-narrative in Lot's story

وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِي صُدُورِهِمْ مِنْ غِلٍّ (v.47) — 'We removed all resentment from their breasts': the most psychologically precise Paradise description; غِلّ (deep resentment/grudge) unique in Paradise contexts
سَبْعًا مِنَ الْمَثَانِي وَالْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ (v.87) — the only Quranic naming of the سَبْع مَثَانِي; traditionally identified as Sūrat al-Fātiḥa; links Q.15 to Q.1
فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ (v.85) — 'forgive with gracious forgiveness': the most aestheticized forgiveness command; جَمِيل applied to صَفْح
فَاصْدَعْ بِمَا تُؤْمَرُ (v.94) — 'declare what you are commanded': one of the most direct prophetic-commission imperatives; صَدَع (to split/proclaim openly) is rare
وَاعْبُدْ رَبَّكَ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَكَ الْيَقِينُ (v.99) — 'worship your Lord until certainty (death) comes': the surah's final verse; open-ended worship extending to the last moment of life
Five-block ring (A/B/C/B′/A′) — only five-block structure confirmed in the backfill; all others are seven-block
cross_surah_flags: Q.17:65: إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ EXACT. Q.15:42 (+ 'except deviators') ↔ Q.17:65 (+ 'Lord is sufficient as وَكِيل'). Same declaration, different continuations.
Q.38:71–85: Adam/Iblīs parallel. Q.15:26–44 (most detailed: صَلْصَال ×3 + breath + seven gates) ↔ Q.38:71–85 (I'm better + reprieve + mislead + Hell filled). Q.15 most detailed pre-Iblīs account.
Q.26:176–191: أَصْحَابُ الْأَيْكَةِ. Q.15:78–79 (compressed: retribution) ↔ Q.26:176–191 (expanded Shuʿayb narrative).
Q.1: سَبْع مَثَانِي. Q.15:87 is the only Quranic verse naming the سَبْع مَثَانِي, linking the 1st and 15th surahs.
Q.17:1 / Q.17:60: ascent/vision parallel. Q.15:14–15 (hypothetical heaven-gate → 'bewitched') ↔ Q.17:60 (actual Night Journey disputed). Hypothetical vs. actual.
Q.56:88–89 / Q.89:27–30: Paradise brotherhood. Q.15:47 (resentment removed → brothers on thrones) — most psychologically detailed Paradise scene; غِلّ unique here.

surahs.period: Meccan (with some Medinan verses at close)
surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (126/128, 98.4%). يُشْرِكُونَ opens → مُحْسِنُونَ closes.

pivot.verse_ar: وَمَا أَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ إِلَّا لِتُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً … وَأَوْحَى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ … فِيهِ شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ

signals.thematic: Ten-verse pivot: v.61 (divine forbearance — if God held accountable, no creature left; defers to term) → v.62 (assign to God what they dislike) → v.63 (Satan beautified for former nations) → v.64 (Book revealed to clarify differences + guidance + mercy) → v.65 (rain → life to dead earth: sign for those who listen) → v.66 (livestock: milk pure between excretion and blood: sign for those who reason) → v.67 (palm/grapes: intoxicant and good provision: sign for those who think) → vv.68–69 (وَأَوْحَى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ — the BEE: inspired → houses → eat fruits → follow Lord's ways → from bellies: varying-color drink → شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ: sign for those who think) → v.70 (created → death → decrepit age). The title creature at the surah's center, receiving وَحْي (same verb as prophetic revelation) and producing healing.
signals.lexical: وَأَوْحَى (v.68) — the verb of prophetic revelation applied to the bee; the only Quranic instance of this verb directed at an insect. شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ (v.69) — healing for people; echoes Q.17:82 (Quran = شِفَاء for believers). نِعْمَة ×8+ threads the entire surah as master keyword. لِتُبَيِّنَ (v.64) echoes تِبْيَانًا (v.89: Quran = clarification for everything) — two self-descriptions of the Book as clarifier, at pivot and in B′.
signals.mirror: D (10v) at offset +0.008. Flanked by C (10v: divine ownership + female-infant burial + لِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَى) and C′ (13v: three blessings catalogue + completes favor + إِذَا تُسْلِمُونَ). Three blessings catalogues: A (cosmic) → D (biological: milk/honey) → C′ (domestic: hides/tents/garments). The bee sits at the center of the blessings arc.
signals.discourse: The pivot moves through four sign-types in rapid sequence, each with a distinct audience marker: signs for those who think (v.65: يَسْمَعُونَ) / those who reason (v.66: يَعْقِلُونَ) / those who think (v.67: يَعْقِلُونَ) / those who think (v.69: يَتَفَكَّرُونَ). The bee narrative is the only one with three consecutive signs announced before and after it — the most densely sign-marked passage in the surah.
signals.rhyme: The pivot maintains the surah's nasal [-ūn/-iːn] pattern: يُعَجِّلُونَ / يَعْلَمُونَ / تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَى... → يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (v.69). The bee verse ends on يَتَفَكَّرُونَ — the surah's most reflective audience-address is reserved for the pivot's climax.

block_map.label: أَتَى أَمْرُ اللَّهِ (v.1); BLESSINGS CATALOGUE ① (cosmic): livestock/horses/sea/mountains/stars; أَفَمَنْ يَخْلُقُ كَمَنْ لَا يَخْلُقُ (v.17); وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا (v.18); إِلَهُكُمْ إِلَهٌ وَاحِدٌ; roof fell on plotters

Day: disgraced + partners deny; angels take wrongdoers/good in death; every nation a messenger: worship God, avoid الطَّاغُوت; بَلَى (resurrection true); كُنْ فَيَكُونُ (v.40); أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ (v.43); everything prostrates + humble (vv.49–50)
One deity → fear only Me; نِعْمَة → adversity → cry to God → removes → party associates (vv.53–54); daughters for God + female infanticide; لِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَى (v.60)

PIVOT (+0.008) — divine forbearance (v.61); Book revealed لِتُبَيِّنَ + guidance + mercy (v.64); rain → life (v.65); BLESSINGS ②: milk between excretion/blood (v.66) → palm/grapes (v.67) → وَأَوْحَى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ → شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ (v.69, title creature); decrepit age (v.70)

Provision differences; mates + sons + grandchildren; worship what has no provision; two parables (slave/free; dumb/just); Hour; extracted from wombs knowing nothing; birds in sky; BLESSINGS ③ (domestic): tents/furnishings/garments → كَذَلِكَ يُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ (v.81); recognize God's favor then deny

Day: witness from every nation + Muhammad; وَنَزَّلْنَا الْكِتَابَ تِبْيَانًا لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ (v.89, most comprehensive); إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسَانِ (v.90, compressed code); covenants; حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً for righteous (v.97); Satan no authority over believers

Verse substitution; رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ (v.102); coercion exception (v.106); city parable: لِبَاسَ الْجُوعِ وَالْخَوْفِ (v.112); إِبْرَاهِيم = أُمَّة (v.120, unique); follow مِلَّة إِبْرَاهِيم; ادْعُ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ (v.125); إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ مُحْسِنُونَ (v.128, closing)

inclusios.note: The surah's master keyword: blessings enumerated (A/D/C′) → sourced exclusively to God (C) → denied by those who receive them (C′/A′) → consequences shown (A′: city parable). Every block contains the root.

inclusios.pair: وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا ×2
inclusios.note: Q.16:18 (→ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ) ↔ Q.14:34 (→ لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ). Same formula, different conclusions: Q.16 = mercy; Q.14 = human ingratitude.
A (v.7: livestock → Kind/Merciful) and B (v.47: gradual seizure → Kind/Merciful). Same divine pair at A and B structural positions.

v.64 (D pivot: Book revealed to clarify what they differ in) ↔ v.89 (B′: We sent down the Book as clarification for EVERYTHING). Two self-descriptions of the Quran as clarifier — one at center, one in B′.

Opens: سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ. Closes: إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ مُحْسِنُونَ. From shirk (what God is above) → iḥsān (what God is with). The surah's theological arc.

special_features: وَأَوْحَى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ (v.68) — the verb of prophetic revelation (أَوْحَى) applied to the bee: the only Quranic instance directing this verb at an insect; the bee receives وَحْي as prophets do
شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ (v.69) — honey as healing for people at the exact center; body parallel to Q.17:82 (Quran as شِفَاء for believers): bee/honey :: Prophet/Quran
Three blessings catalogues: A (cosmic: livestock/sea/mountains/stars) → D (biological: milk/honey) → C′ (domestic: hides/tents/garments). The pivot is the biological center of a creation-to-home arc.
v.89: وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ تِبْيَانًا لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ — 'clarification for EVERYTHING': the Quran's most comprehensive self-description

v.90: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسَانِ وَإِيتَاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى وَيَنْهَى عَنِ الْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ وَالْبَغْيِ — 3 commands + 3 prohibitions; Q.17:22–39's Decalogue compressed to one verse
v.97: حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً — 'a good life': gender-explicit reward (ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى) for righteous believers; among the Quran's clearest gender-equal recompense statements
v.106: coercion exception — whoever disbelieves after faith EXCEPT forced while heart secure; the Quran's foundational principle on coerced renunciation
v.112: لِبَاسَ الْجُوعِ وَالْخَوْفِ — 'garment of hunger and fear': the most vivid punishment-as-clothing metaphor; city that denied God's favors clothed in deprivation
v.120: إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً — Abraham called أُمَّة (a comprehensive leader/nation in one person): the only person in the Quran given this singular designation

v.125: ادْعُ إِلَى سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ وَجَادِلْهُمْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ — the Quran's definitive daʿwah methodology: wisdom + good instruction + best argumentation
رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ (v.102) — the Holy Spirit brought the Quran; one of few explicit namings of Gabriel by this title
cross_surah_flags: Q.14:34: نِعْمَة count EXACT formula. Q.16:18 (→ Forgiving/Merciful) ↔ Q.14:34 (→ man is unjust/ungrateful). Same formula, opposite conclusions.
Q.17:82: شِفَاء parallel. Q.16:69 (honey = شِفَاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ) ↔ Q.17:82 (Quran = شِفَاء for believers). Bee/honey :: Prophet/Quran — body analogy across consecutive surahs.
Q.17:22–39: ethical code. Q.16:90 (3 commands + 3 prohibitions, one verse) ↔ Q.17:22–39 (12 commands, 18 verses). Q.16 compresses what Q.17 expands.
Q.21:7: أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ EXACT. Q.16:43 and Q.21:7 share the identical instruction.
Q.22:5: أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ EXACT. Q.16:70 and Q.22:5 share 'returned to most decrepit age so knows nothing after knowledge.'
Q.2:117 / Q.19:35 / Q.36:82: كُنْ فَيَكُونُ thread. Q.16:40 is part of the distributed formula.

surahs.rhyme_type: [-ā] tanwīn accusative dominant (109/111, 98.2%).

pivot.verse_ar: وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْنَا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا … أَجِيبُوا بِحَمْدِهِ

signals.thematic: Eight-verse epistemological crisis: Quran recited → حِجَاب مَسْتُورًا placed between Prophet and Hereafter-deniers (v.45) + أَكِنَّة on hearts + وَقْر (deafness) in ears (v.46) → when Lord mentioned alone they turn in aversion → private talk: 'he is bewitched' (v.47) → look how they strike comparisons → strayed (v.48) → 'when we're bones → resurrected?' (v.49) → قُلْ: 'be stones or iron — He who created you first will restore you' (vv.50–51) → Day: respond with His praise, think you stayed only a little (v.52). The pivot explains WHY wisdom (B) doesn't persuade: a divinely placed veil separates revelation from those who deny the Hereafter.
signals.lexical: حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا (v.45) — the only Quranic instance of this exact compound; a covered/concealed veil. أَكِنَّة (v.46) = coverings; وَقْر = heaviness/deafness. مَسْحُورًا (v.47: 'bewitched') — the accusation Pharaoh made of Moses (Q.17:101) appears here against Muhammad; bookend within A′. وَكِيل threads the pivot: Lord is sufficient (B′, v.65), no disposer from the veil-covered. بِحَمْدِهِ (v.52: you respond with His praise) — echoes the closing v.111: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ.
signals.mirror: D (8v) at offset −0.068. Flanked by C (5v: universal glorification — everything glorifies) and C′ (8v: tell servants to say the best + intercession + signs withheld + accursed tree). The pivot sits between the most universal divine declaration (v.44: not a thing except it glorifies) and the instruction on how to respond (C′: say the best, seek nearness). The gap explained by the veil.
signals.discourse: Shift from imperative (B: Decalogue commands) to descriptive (D: what happens when Quran is recited to disbelievers). The pivot is the only explanatory passage in the surah — it doesn't command or narrate, it diagnoses. Then a question (v.49) and a divine answer (vv.50–52): rapid dialogue ending on the eschatological reversal.
signals.rhyme: مَسْتُورًا / مَوْقُورًا / نُفُورًا / مَنْثُورًا / مَسْحُورًا / مَسْتُورًا… the pivot's [-ūrā] terminals break the dominant [-ā] pattern. The rhyme-shift marks the veil's presence — even the sound of the surah changes at the barrier.

block_map.label: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَى بِعَبْدِهِ (v.1, Night Journey — one verse, no narrative); Moses/Book/Banī Isrāʾīl; two corruptions decreed + two punishments; Quran guides to الْأَقْوَم; fate on neck; no bearer of another's burden; destroy after messenger

WISDOM DECALOGUE (18v): لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ ① (v.22) → 12 commands: worship/parents/relatives/spending/children/zinā/killing/orphans/commitments/measure/knowledge/walking → ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحِكْمَةِ (v.39) → لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ ② (v.39). Decalogue framed by identical tawhid command.
Daughters for God? → تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ السَّمَاوَاتُ السَّبْعُ وَالْأَرْضُ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ (v.44) — NOT A THING except it glorifies. Most absolute universalization. حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا.

PIVOT (−0.068) — حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا (v.45, unique) + أَكِنَّة + وَقْر when Lord mentioned alone → 'bewitched' (v.47) → bones → 'He who created you first' → Day: respond with His praise (v.52). Epistemological veil between wisdom offered and truth refused.
Say الَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ; those they invoke seek وَسِيلَة themselves; signs withheld (former peoples denied); vision + accursed tree = trial → only increases transgression

IBLĪS: prostrate to Adam → refused → 'incite/cavalry/infantry/wealth/children' → إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ (v.65); وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا بَنِي آدَمَ (v.70); tempted almost from revelation; سُنَّةَ مَنْ قَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا (v.77)

Prayer times; tahajjud → مَقَامًا مَحْمُودًا (v.79, unique); جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ (v.81); Quran = شِفَاء + رَحْمَة; الرُّوح → little knowledge (v.85); إِنْس + جِنّ can't produce its like (v.88); five demands → 'human messenger'; Moses/nine signs/Pharaoh: 'bewitched' (v.101 ↔ v.47 bookend); ادْعُوا اللَّهَ أَوِ ادْعُوا الرَّحْمَانَ → الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى (v.110); الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ + تَكْبِيرًا (v.111, closing)

inclusios.note: v.1 (Night Journey) → v.43 (above what they say) → v.93 (Prophet: 'was I but a human?') → v.108 (knowledgeable ones: prostrate/weep). سُبْحَان opens and threads the entire surah.
Opens on سُبْحَانَ (glorification) → closes on الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (praise). The arc: from exalting God above deficiency → praising God for His perfection.

inclusios.pair: لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَهًا آخَرَ ×2
inclusios.note: The identical tawhid command frames the Wisdom Decalogue: v.22 (opens) → v.39 (closes). The entire ethical code is bracketed by monotheism.

v.2 (A: don't take a disposer besides God) → v.54 (C′: not sent as manager) → v.65 (B′: Lord sufficient as protector) → v.68 (B′: no protector). The wali/manager concept threads from Israel's warning to Satan's defeat.
vv.17/30/96: identical divine-attribute pair (Acquainted, Seeing) at A → B → A′. Three structural anchors.
v.47 (D: disbelievers call Prophet 'bewitched') ↔ v.101 (A′: Pharaoh calls Moses 'bewitched'). The same accusation at the center and near the close — two prophets, one slander.

special_features: v.1: Night Journey in ONE verse — سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَى بِعَبْدِهِ; no narrative; the surah's namesake appears and is passed; the focus is entirely on what the journey produced (the ethical code, the Decalogue)
Wisdom Decalogue (B, vv.22–39): 12 ethical commands framed by identical لَا تَجْعَلْ — the only passage explicitly named الْحِكْمَة (v.39) by the Quran itself
v.44: تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ السَّمَاوَاتُ السَّبْعُ … وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِ — 'not a thing except it glorifies': the Quran's most absolute universalization of divine praise
حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا (v.45) — the only Quranic instance; the divinely placed veil between revelation and those who deny the Hereafter; explains why the Decalogue doesn't persuade all
v.65: إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ — 'over My servants you have no authority': the Quran's most unqualified statement of divine protection against Satan
v.70: وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا بَنِي آدَمَ — 'We honored the children of Adam': the Quran's foundational declaration of universal human dignity, without qualification
v.79: مَقَامًا مَحْمُودًا — 'praised station': the Quran's only mention of the مَقَام مَحْمُود (intercession station); connected to tahajjud
v.81: جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ إِنَّ الْبَاطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا — the most celebrated truth/falsehood verse
v.85: الرُّوح → 'the soul is of my Lord's affair → you've been given only a little knowledge': the Quran's most explicit statement of human epistemic limits
v.88: إِنْس + جِنّ gathered couldn't produce the like — the Quran's total inimitability challenge
B↔B′ mirror: Decalogue (12 ethical commands) ↔ Iblīs (attacks wealth/children — the very things the Decalogue governs). Satan targets the code's weak points.
cross_surah_flags: Q.7:205: night/day glorification. Q.17:79 (tahajjud) connects to the glorification thread.
Q.18:57: أَكِنَّة + وَقْر EXACT. Q.17:46 and Q.18:57 — consecutive surahs sharing identical barrier-language for Hereafter-deniers.
Q.33:62 / Q.35:43 / Q.48:23: سُنَّةَ اللَّهِ thread. Q.17:77 (سُنَّةَ مَنْ قَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا + لَا تَجِدُ لِسُنَّتِنَا تَحْوِيلًا) — earliest deployment of the formula with تَحْوِيل.
Q.21:67: أُفّ cross-reference. Q.17:23 (forbidden toward parents) ↔ Q.21:67 (Abraham directs at idols). Same word, reversed targets.
Q.22:62 / Q.31:30: الْحَقّ/الْبَاطِل thread. Q.17:81 (جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ) — most celebrated deployment.
Q.19:88–93: son-denial. Q.17:111 (لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا, calm negation) ↔ Q.19:88–93 (cosmic convulsion). Q.17 = calm; Q.19 = visceral.
Q.20:114: knowledge-limit parallel. Q.17:85 (only a little knowledge given) → Q.20:114 (رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا: pray to expand it). Q.17 declares the ceiling; Q.20 pushes against it.

Q.25:63–76: ethical portrait. Q.17:22–39 (Decalogue: 12 commands, imperative) ↔ Q.25:63–76 (عِبَادُ الرَّحْمَانِ: 12 attributes, descriptive). Two ethical codes — negative (don't) ↔ positive (they do).

surahs.rhyme_type: [-ā] tanwīn accusative dominant (108/110, 98.2%). جَدَلًا (dispute) at center.

pivot.verse_ar: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا فِي هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ جَدَلًا

signals.thematic: Single-verse pivot: 'We have certainly diversified in this Quran for people every kind of example, but man has ever been most prone to dispute.' Sits between the first two narratives (N1: Cave/time; N2: Gardens/wealth) and the last two (N3: Khiḍr/knowledge; N4: Dhul-Qarnayn/power). The Quran reflects on its own instruction method and diagnoses humanity's failure to receive it — at the exact center of a surah built entirely from parables.
signals.lexical: صَرَّفْنَا (diversified/turned) — the Quran describes its own compositional method. الْإِنْسَانُ (man/humanity) — generic, encompassing all four trial-subjects. جَدَلًا (dispute) — the terminal word names the central human pathology. Echoes v.56 (T3: disbelievers dispute with falsehood). Mirrors N2's rich man arguing → N3's Moses questioning Khiḍr.
signals.mirror: D (1v) at offset −0.014. The only single-verse pivot in the project. Four narratives arranged symmetrically around it: N1↔N4 (time/faith ↔ space/power), N2↔N3 (wealth ↔ knowledge). Three transitions (T1/T2/T3) bookend the center.
signals.discourse: The verse is the narrator's meta-commentary — neither dialogue, nor narrative, nor command. It steps outside all four stories to name the pattern. The surah's only self-referential statement about the Quran itself as teacher, mid-surah.
signals.rhyme: جَدَلًا [-alā] at center. The word 'dispute' sits at the mathematical center — the surah's terminal at its pivot names humanity's core problem. The sound [-alā] recurs in the next transition (T3) as a slight prosodic echo.

block_map.label: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ → Book on servant → no crookedness; warn/give tidings; 'God took a son' — grave lie; بَاخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ (v.6, grief); earth's adornment = test: best in deed → then barren

CAVE SLEEPERS (TIME/FAITH): youths → cave → 309 years; cast sleep → woke → 'how long?'; dispute over number; إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ ×1 (command: don't say tomorrow without it); God's promise true + Hour undoubted
Patient with those calling Lord; قُلِ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ; Fire for wrongdoers / Gardens of Eden for believers

TWO GARDENS (WEALTH): rich man: 'I don't think this will perish'; companion: 'why not say مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ?'; garden destroyed; هُنَالِكَ الْوَلَايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِّ (v.44)
World = rain → vegetation → scattered; الْمَالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الدُّنْيَا وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ (v.46); Iblīs of jinn (v.50); Day: record placed; criminals see Fire
PIVOT (−0.014) — SINGLE VERSE: صَرَّفْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ جَدَلًا — Quran's self-reflection on diverse parables + man's argumentativeness
Nothing prevents belief except سُنَّةُ الْأَوَّلِينَ or face-to-face punishment; وَرَبُّكَ الْغَفُورُ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ; destroyed cities + appointed time

MOSES + KHIḌR (KNOWLEDGE): junction of two seas; forgot fish; found servant given mercy + knowledge; three acts: ship torn / boy killed / wall built → three explanations: poor ship / evil boy / orphans' treasure; رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَمْرِي (v.82); إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ ×2 (failure + correct use)
DHUL-QARNAYN (POWER/SPACE): West (dark spring) / East (no shelter) / between mountains: يَأْجُوج وَمَأْجُوج → iron dam; هَذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّي (v.98); dam leveled at Lord's promise

Eyes covered from ذِكْر; greatest losers: effort lost while thinking doing well; جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ (v.107); sea as ink → exhausted before Lord's words (v.109); إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ → one God → righteous work + no shirk (v.110)

inclusios.note: v.7 (A: test = best in deed) → v.30 (T1: reward not lost) → v.110 (A′: do righteous work + no shirk). The surah opens on the test and closes on its conditions.
Q.18:46 and Q.19:76. Consecutive surahs sharing 'enduring good deeds better than wealth/worldly adornment.'

N1 (youths' prayer: grant mercy v.10) → N2 (implicit: garden's loss vs. God's mercy) → N3 (رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ v.82: Khiḍr's acts) → N4 (هَذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّي v.98: Dhul-Qarnayn's dam). Mercy threads all four narratives.
v.23–24 (A: command — never say tomorrow without it) → v.67–68 (N3: Khiḍr says Moses won't be able, Moses disobeys) → v.69 (N3: Moses invokes correctly). Progression: command → failure → correct use.
Q.18:107 and Q.23:11. Two surahs naming الْفِرْدَوْس specifically as believers' lodging/inheritance.

special_features: Only single-verse pivot in the project (v.54) — the Quran's meta-commentary on its own parable-method + humanity's argumentativeness, at exact center
Four-narrative architecture — most unique structure in the project: N1 (time/faith) ↔ N4 (space/power); N2 (wealth) ↔ N3 (knowledge). Four Dajjal-related trials: faith, wealth, knowledge, power
Three إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ deployments as pedagogical sequence: command (v.23) → failure (v.67–68) → correct use (v.69)
Moses as student — the only Quranic episode where a prophet explicitly requests to learn from someone else; the greatest prophet becomes a pupil
Khiḍr's three acts and their hidden mercy: apparent harm → revealed purpose (رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَمْرِي v.82)
Dhul-Qarnayn's declaration (v.98: هَذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّي) mirrors Khiḍr's attribution — both agents of divine mercy, not self-will
Sea-ink (v.109): 'if sea were ink for my Lord's words → exhausted before words'; amplified in Q.31:27 (trees as pens + seven more seas)
الْفِرْدَوْس (v.107) — one of two surahs naming الْفِرْدَوْس specifically (the other: Q.23:11)
الْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ (v.46) = Q.19:76 EXACT — adjacent surahs sharing the phrase
Iblīs identified as of the jinn (v.50) — the Quran's clearest statement of Iblīs's ontological nature
بَاخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ (v.6: killing yourself with grief) = Q.26:3 EXACT — two surahs, same prophetic grief formula
هُنَالِكَ الْوَلَايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِّ (v.44) — 'there the authority belongs to God, the Truth': the Two Gardens' theological conclusion
cross_surah_flags: Q.31:27: sea-ink parallel. Q.18:109 (one sea as ink → exhausted) ↔ Q.31:27 (trees as pens + sea + seven more → not exhausted). Two-surah amplification of divine infinity.
Q.23:11: الْفِرْدَوْس. Q.18:107 and Q.23:11 — two surahs naming الْفِرْدَوْس as the believers' lodging.
Q.19:76: الْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ EXACT. Q.18:46 and Q.19:76 — consecutive surahs.
Q.26:3: بَاخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ EXACT. Q.18:6 and Q.26:3 — same prophetic grief formula.
Q.21:96: يَأْجُوج/مَأْجُوج. Q.18:94–97 (dam built) ↔ Q.21:96 (dam breached). Two stages of the same eschatological event.
Q.20:116–123: Iblīs/Adam. Q.18:50 (Iblīs of jinn, warning) ↔ Q.20:116–123 (Adam falls, recovers). Different emphases: Q.18 = ontological; Q.20 = soteriological.
Q.25:43: هَوَى thread. Q.18:28 (don't follow one whose heart heedless + follows desires) — extends desire-worship thread.

surahs.rhyme_type: [-iyā] dominant (96/98, 98.0%) — most distinctive prosodic signature in the corpus. [-dā] shift at sonship-denial climax (vv.88–98).

pivot.verse_ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ مُوسَى … أُولَئِكَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ … إِذَا تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُ الرَّحْمَانِ خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا

signals.thematic: Eight-verse prophetic genealogy: Moses (مُخْلَص + رَسُول نَبِيّ; brought near God as نَجِيّ — confiding) + Aaron (prophet by mercy); Ishmael (صَادِق الوَعْد + رَسُول نَبِيّ; commanded family prayer/zakāh → مَرْضِيًّا); Idrees (صِدِّيق نَبِيّ; raised to high station). v.58: comprehensive lineage — Adam's descendants + those with Noah + Abraham's + Israel's + those guided and chosen → when الرَّحْمَان's verses recited → fell سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا. The center defines what unites all prophets: prostration and weeping before الرَّحْمَان's revelation.
signals.lexical: الرَّحْمَان ×16 — most saturated divine name in any surah; present in every block. نَجِيًّا (v.52) — the most intimate prophetic-proximity term. وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ ×4 (vv.16/41/51/54/56) introduces each prophet. مَرْضِيًّا (v.55) — Ishmael pleasing to his Lord, unique adjective at pivot. بُكِيًّا (v.58) — weeping; the prophets' defining emotional response to الرَّحْمَان.
signals.mirror: D (8v) at offset +0.051. Flanked by C (10v: Abraham's tender confrontation with father) and C′ (7v: successors who neglected prayer + Paradise). The pivot sits between individual confrontation and communal decline — the prophetic standard against which both are measured.
signals.discourse: Four وَاذْكُرْ commands introduce three prophets (Moses/Ishmael/Idrees) in 2–3 verses each, then v.58 telescopes from individual to collective, naming four generational strata in one sentence. The pivot's discourse compresses from biographical to genealogical.
signals.rhyme: نَبِيًّا / نَجِيًّا / نَبِيًّا / نَبِيًّا / مَرْضِيًّا / نَبِيًّا / عَلِيًّا / وَبُكِيًّا — pure [-iyā] throughout. The pivot sounds like the gentle narratives it summarizes.

block_map.label: كهيعص; ZECHARIAH: private call → bones weakened, head white → 'give me an heir' → يَحْيَى (no namesake before) → sign: three nights of silence → take Book with strength; وَسَلَامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا (v.15, by God)

MARY: withdrew east → spirit as man → 'I seek refuge in الرَّحْمَان' → pure boy; conceived → palm trunk → 'shake it → dates'; brought to people → pointed to infant; JESUS SPEAKS: 'servant of God + Book + prophet + prayer/zakāh + dutiful + not wretched'; وَالسَّلَامُ عَلَيَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدْتُ … (v.33, self-declared); مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا → كُنْ فَيَكُونُ

ABRAHAM: صِدِّيق نَبِيّ; يَا أَبَتِ ×4 (vv.42–45, most tender paternal address sequence): 'why worship deaf/blind idols?' → 'I fear for you from الرَّحْمَان' → father threatens → سَلَامٌ + 'I'll seek forgiveness'; left → Isaac + Jacob → لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا

PIVOT (+0.051) — PROPHETIC GENEALOGY: Moses (مُخْلَص, نَجِيًّا v.52) + Aaron; Ishmael (صَادِق الوَعْد, مَرْضِيًّا); Idrees (عَلِيًّا); v.58: Adam→Noah→Abraham→Israel→guided → آيَات الرَّحْمَان → سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا

Successors neglected prayer + followed desires → غَيّ; EXCEPT repent → Paradise (Gardens of Eden); no ill speech → سَلَام; 'we descend only by your Lord's command' (Gabriel v.64); هَلْ تَعْلَمُ لَهُ سَمِيًّا (v.65)

Man: 'when I die will I be raised?' → وَإِنْ مِنْكُمْ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَا (v.71, all pass through Hell); extract worst → save تَقِيّ; clear verses → 'which of us better?'; الرَّحْمَان extends those in error; الْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ (v.76 = Q.18:46); taken gods for عِزّ → كَلَّا → they'll deny

Righteous delegated to الرَّحْمَان; وَقَالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمَانُ وَلَدًا (v.88); تَكَادُ السَّمَاوَاتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ (v.90, cosmic convulsion); مَا يَنْبَغِي لِلرَّحْمَانِ وَلَدًا; إِنْ كُلُّ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ إِلَّا آتِي الرَّحْمَانِ عَبْدًا; وُدًّا (v.96, God places affection for believers); Quran easy → بَشِّرْ righteous

inclusios.note: v.15 (وَسَلَامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا — by God, 3rd person, about John) ↔ v.33 (وَالسَّلَامُ عَلَيَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدْتُ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّا — by Jesus, 1st person, self-declared). Same three-part formula: born/die/raised alive. One declared by God; one declared by the prophet himself.

A: God GIVES Zechariah a miraculous son (يَحْيَى) — the impossible birth as divine mercy. A′: God REJECTS the claim He took a son — heavens almost rupture (v.90). The surah opens on God granting a son and closes on the horror of attributing one to God.
v.7: 'no one named يَحْيَى before' (human uniqueness). v.65: 'do you know any equal to Him?' (divine incomparability). Same word: unmatched human → unmatched God.

vv.4/32/48: Zechariah ('never unhappy calling You') / Jesus ('not a wretched tyrant') / Abraham ('hope I won't be unhappy calling'). Three prophets, three negations of شَقَاء. Cross-surah: Q.20's master keyword.
The highest concentration of الرَّحْمَان in any surah — present in every block. The surah is named after Mary but structured around الرَّحْمَان.

special_features: الرَّحْمَان ×16 — highest concentration in any surah; present in every block; the surah's true organizing divine name
Twin peace-formulas (vv.15/33): God declares over John (born/death/resurrection) ↔ Jesus declares over himself (same three-part formula) — the only two figures to receive this formula
يَا أَبَتِ ×4 (vv.42–45) — four consecutive uses of the tender paternal address; Abraham's most sustained plea to his father
نَجِيًّا (v.52) — Moses brought near God as a confidant; the most intimate prophetic-proximity term in the Quran
v.58: the Quran's most comprehensive prophetic genealogy in one verse (Adam→Noah→Abraham→Israel→guided) culminating in سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا
تَكَادُ السَّمَاوَاتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ (v.90) — heavens almost rupture, earth splits, mountains collapse from the claim that God has a son: the most violent cosmic-reaction verse
وَإِنْ مِنْكُمْ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَا (v.71) — 'none of you but will come to it (Hell)': the most universally binding eschatological statement
وُدًّا (v.96) — the surah's closing promise: الرَّحْمَان appoints affection in people's hearts for believers
Gabriel speaks (v.64): 'we descend only by your Lord's command' — one of only two Quranic verses where Gabriel addresses the Prophet directly
كُنْ فَيَكُونُ (v.35) — Be and it is; Jesus' creation declared with the same formula as all creation
[-iyā] prosody shifts to [-dā] at the sonship-denial climax (vv.88–98) — the prosodic shift encodes the cosmic convulsion thematically
الْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ (v.76) = Q.18:46 EXACT — consecutive surahs sharing 'enduring good deeds are better'
cross_surah_flags: Q.21:89–90: Zechariah parallel. Q.19 (detailed: private call → three-part peace) ↔ Q.21 (compressed: 'don't leave me alone' → Yahya → khāshi'īn). Q.19 = emotional detail; Q.21 = liturgical summary.
Q.23:50: Jesus/Mary. Q.19 = Quran's most detailed Mary narrative. Q.23:50 = one verse (sign, sheltered on high ground).
Q.20:2/117/123: شَقِيّ thread. Q.19 deploys ×3 for three prophets; Q.20 uses it as master keyword. Consecutive surahs sharing root.
Q.18:46: الْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ EXACT. Q.19:76 = Q.18:46. Consecutive surahs.
Q.21:26–28: الرَّحْمَان took a son denial. Q.19:88–93 (cosmic convulsion) ↔ Q.21:26–28 (theological correction). Q.19 = visceral; Q.21 = doctrinal.
Q.26:84: لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ. Q.19:50 (granted to Abraham) ↔ Q.26:84 (prayed for by Abraham). Same phrase: gift ↔ prayer.
Q.21:91: Mary+Jesus as آيَة. Q.19:21 (آيَة + رَحْمَة) ↔ Q.21:91 (آيَة لِلْعَالَمِينَ). Mercy ↔ universality.

surahs.rhyme_type: Long-vowel [-ā] dominant (133/135, 98.5%) — distinctive among Meccan surahs. Calf pivot disrupts the [-ā] pattern.

pivot.verse_ar: وَمَا أَعْجَلَكَ عَنْ قَوْمِكَ يَا مُوسَى … إِنَّمَا إِلَهُكُمُ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا

signals.thematic: Golden Calf episode (16v): 'What made you hasten from your people?' → Sāmirī led them astray → Moses returns angry/grieved → calf with lowing: 'this is your god and Moses' god, but he forgot' → Aaron warned them: 'your Lord is الرَّحْمَان, follow me' → Moses confronts Aaron + Sāmirī → 'saw what they didn't, took handful from messenger's track, my soul enticed me' → لَا مِسَاسَ → burn it + blow into sea → إِنَّمَا إِلَهُكُمُ اللَّهُ (v.98). The narrative center: idolatry after liberation, tawhid violated and restored.
signals.lexical: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (v.98) — third tawhid declaration (vv.8/14/98): opens → commissions Moses → resolves calf crisis. الرَّحْمَان (v.90: Aaron's instruction mid-crisis). نَفْسُهُ (v.96: 'my soul enticed me') echoes Moses' request: 'expand my breast' (v.25). وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا (v.98) echoes A: 'He knows the secret and what is more hidden' (v.7).
signals.mirror: D (16v) at offset +0.167. Front-loaded surah: B (68v, 50%) occupies first half. The narrative center of a front-loaded surah sits forward of the geometric center. Flanked by C (6v: forgiveness formula) and C′ (14v: Day of Judgment). The calf is the crisis both sections bracket.
signals.discourse: Rapid-fire dialogue: God→Moses → Moses→Aaron → Moses→Sāmirī — three confrontations in sequence, each shorter than the last. The most dialogically compressed section of the surah. Discourse accelerates as the crisis deepens.
signals.rhyme: The Golden Calf section temporarily disrupts the surah's distinctive [-ā] dominance — proper names (Sāmirī, Hārūn, Mūsā used as vocatives) and the urgency of the confrontation create prosodic variation at the surah's most dramatic point.

block_map.label: طه; مَا أَنْزَلْنَا لِتَشْقَى (v.2); الرَّحْمَانُ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ اسْتَوَى (v.5); لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ + الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى (v.8)

MOSES PART 1 (68v): fire → يَا مُوسَى إِنِّي أَنَا رَبُّكَ → sandals → لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنَا (v.14); Moses' prayer → breast/tongue/Aaron; وَاصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِي (v.41); قَوْلًا لَيِّنًا (v.44); magicians → Moses FEARED (v.67) → prostrated → Pharaoh threatens; exodus → أَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُ
Israel delivered from enemy; مَنّ and سَلْوَى; وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا ثُمَّ اهْتَدَى (v.82, perpetual forgiveness formula)

PIVOT (+0.167) — GOLDEN CALF: 'what made you hasten?' → Sāmirī led astray → calf with lowing → Aaron warned → Moses confronts Aaron + Sāmirī → 'my soul enticed me' → لَا مِسَاسَ → burn + blow into sea → إِنَّمَا إِلَهُكُمُ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (v.98)
Day of Judgment: Horn blown; criminals blue-eyed; murmuring; mountains blown away; voices stilled before الرَّحْمَان; no unauthorized intercession; وَعَنَتِ الْوُجُوهُ لِلْحَيِّ الْقَيُّومِ (v.111)

Arabic Quran; رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا (v.114, unique); ADAM: prostration → Iblīs refused → Paradise → فَتَشْقَى (v.117) → tree → ate → ثُمَّ اجْتَبَاهُ رَبُّهُ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ وَهَدَى (v.122) → لَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشْقَى (v.123)

Whoever turns from ذِكْر → مَعِيشَةً ضَنْكًا + blind on Day; destroyed nations → أُولِي النُّهَى (×2: vv.54/128); فَاصْبِرْ → prayer; رِزْقُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَى; وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلتَّقْوَى; كُلٌّ مُتَرَبِّصٌ (closing)

inclusios.note: Master keyword: v.2 (لِتَشْقَى: Quran not for distress) → v.117 (فَتَشْقَى: leaving Paradise = distress) → v.123 (لَا يَشْقَى: following guidance = no distress). Arc: not-distress → distress → not-distress.
vv.8/14/98: A (opening) → B (theophany, God commissions Moses) → D (after destroying calf, tawhid restored). The same declaration opens, commissions, and resolves.
vv.54 and 128 — only surah using this phrase; 'those of sound intelligence' as audience for signs, in B and A′.

vv.73/127/131: 'God is better and more lasting' (magicians) / 'Hereafter's punishment is more intense and lasting' / 'your Lord's provision is better and more lasting'. The comparative formula threads from B through A′.
vv.5/90/108/109: Throne → Aaron's instruction at crisis → voices stilled before → intercession permitted by. الرَّحْمَان threads A → D → C′ (×2).

special_features: شَقَى master thread (vv.2/117/123) — the surah's architectural keyword: distress (Quran not for it) → distress (Paradise lost) → not-distress (guidance followed)
وَاصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِي (v.41) — 'I produced you for Myself': the most intimate divine address in the Quran, God speaking to Moses
قَوْلًا لَيِّنًا (v.44) — 'speak gently to Pharaoh': command to approach the worst tyrant with soft speech
فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ خِيفَةً (v.67) — Moses FEARED before the magicians: the only moment of prophetic fear in the Moses narrative
رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا (v.114) — 'Lord, increase me in knowledge': the only Quranic prayer specifically requesting more knowledge
الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى (v.8) — the beautiful names; the only surah opening on this phrase
الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ (v.111) — earliest Quranic deployment; Q.2:255 (Throne Verse) and Q.3:2 are later
Sāmirī named and condemned (vv.85–97) — the only Quranic naming of the Golden Calf's maker; unique account in all of scripture
لَا مِسَاسَ (v.97) — 'no touching': the Sāmirī's unique punishment; social exile
[-ā] prosody (98.5%) — the most distinctive prosodic profile in the corpus; the surah sounds unlike any other
Adam narrative (B′, 11v) mirrors Moses narrative (B, 68v): both are chosen → tested → fell → forgiven/guided; compressed archetype after historical detail
cross_surah_flags: Q.2:255 / Q.3:2: الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ. Q.20:111 is earliest Quranic deployment. Thread: Q.20→Q.2→Q.3.
Q.7:148–156: Golden Calf parallel. Q.20 (Sāmirī named + burned + لَا مِسَاسَ) ↔ Q.7 (tablets + Moses' anger). Different details, same event.
Q.28:7–13: Moses' birth parallel. Q.20 told by GOD recounting to Moses; Q.28 told by narrator. Different perspectives on the same story.
Q.26:10–68: Moses/Pharaoh/magicians. Q.20 emphasizes Moses' FEAR (v.67); Q.26 emphasizes DEFIANCE at sea. Two emotional registers.
Q.25:58: الْحَيّ. Q.20:111 (الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ: Sustainer) ↔ Q.25:58 (الْحَيِّ الَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ: doesn't die). Two different expansions.
Q.23:96 / Q.41:34: gentle approach. Q.20:44 (قَوْلًا لَيِّنًا to Pharaoh) ↔ Q.23:96/Q.41:34 (ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ). Three surahs on approaching adversaries with gentleness.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (110/112, 98.2%). إِبْرَاهِيمُ breaks prosody at three narrative peaks.

pivot.verse_ar: فَرَجَعُوا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ … قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ … وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا

signals.thematic: Abraham (23v) at center of 'The Prophets' surah: given رُشْد → smashed idols except largest → 'ask them if they speak' → فَرَجَعُوا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (v.64: they returned to themselves — moment of self-recognition, then reversed) → 'burn him' → يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا (v.69) → saved → Isaac/Jacob added → all made righteous → أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا (v.73). The Prophets surah centers on the Prophet of prophets in his defining act.
signals.lexical: فَرَجَعُوا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (v.64) — the only Quranic instance of a crowd experiencing genuine self-reflection, even if momentary. رُشْد (v.51) — rational right-guidance preceding the narrative. الْأَخْسَرِينَ (v.70) — 'the greatest losers' used for those who planned to burn Abraham. أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا (v.73) — Abraham's lineage as leaders-by-divine-command. الْعَالَمِينَ (v.71) echoes v.91 and v.107.
signals.mirror: D (23v) at offset +0.049. Flanked by C (3v: Moses+Quran) and C′ (18v: ten prophetic figures). C gives the textual mandate (Quran = blessed ذِكْر); D gives the Abrahamic model; C′ shows the model enacted by all the prophets after him.
signals.discourse: The pivot shifts from third-person cosmic argument (A/B) into direct narrative dialogue — the most sustained dialogic sequence in the surah. 'Who did this?' → 'the largest' → 'you know they don't speak' → 'burn him' → divine counter-command. The center is pure dramatic confrontation.
signals.rhyme: إِبْرَاهِيمُ breaks the [-ūn/-iːn] pattern at vv.51, 60, 69 — three times, each at a narrative peak: introduction/recognition/fire-miracle. The proper name disrupts prosody to mark the three structural beats of the story.

block_map.label: اقْتَرَبَ لِلنَّاسِ حِسَابُهُمْ (v.1); every ذِكْر → heedless; لَوْ كَانَ فِيهِمَا آلِهَةٌ لَفَسَدَتَا (v.22, cosmic-order proof); every messenger: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاعْبُدُونِ; angels near Him apprehensive
رَتْق/فَتْق: heavens/earth separated (v.30); water = every living thing; كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ (v.35); الرَّحْمَان ridiculed; وَنَضَعُ الْمَوَازِينَ الْقِسْطَ (v.47, scales of justice)
Moses+Aaron: الْفُرْقَان + light + ذِكْر for the righteous; وَهَذَا ذِكْرٌ مُبَارَكٌ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ (v.50, Quran self-reference)

PIVOT (+0.049) — ABRAHAM (23v): رُشْد → smashed idols → 'ask them if they speak' → فَرَجَعُوا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (v.64, unique self-recognition) → 'burn him' → يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا (v.69) → أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا (v.73)

TEN PROPHETS/GROUPS: Lot / Noah / David+Solomon / Job: أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ / Ishmael+Idrees+Dhul-Kifl / Jonah: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ → وَكَذَلِكَ نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (v.88) / Zechariah+Yahya / Mary+Jesus = sign for the worlds; فَاسْتَجَبْنَا ×4
إِنَّ هَذِهِ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً (v.92 = Q.23:52 EXACT); divided → return to Us; Gog/Magog opened; وَاقْتَرَبَ الْوَعْدُ الْحَقُّ (v.97, echoing v.1); الْفَزَعُ الْأَكْبَرُ
Fold sky like scroll (v.104); الزَّبُور: righteous inherit earth (v.105); وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ (v.107); وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمَانُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ (closing v.112)

refrains.note: 'We responded to him' — Noah, Job, Jonah, Zechariah. C′'s structural refrain: four prophets called, four responses.

inclusios.note: v.1: their account has approached (about them, in heedlessness) ↔ v.97: the true promise has approached (confessed by them: 'we were unmindful'). The surah's master inclusio — heedlessness declared becomes heedlessness confessed.
v.1 (وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ) ↔ v.97 (قَدْ كُنَّا فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ هَذَا). Third-person description → first-person confession.

v.71 (Abraham's blessed land for the worlds) → v.91 (Mary+Jesus = sign for the worlds) → v.107 (رَحْمَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ — mercy to the worlds). Three 'for the worlds' deployments mapping the prophetic trajectory from Abraham to Muhammad.
Threads entire surah: new ذِكْر → ذِكْر in Book → Moses given ذِكْر → Quran = blessed ذِكْر → Psalms (الزَّبُور) after الذِّكْرِ. The surah is itself about the ذِكْر and is itself a ذِكْر.
Q.21:92 and Q.23:52 share the identical declaration — one nation, one Lord. Both followed by تَقَطَّعُوا (they divided). Two consecutive surahs (Q.21 and Q.23), same verse, same sequel.

special_features: فَرَجَعُوا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (v.64) — the only Quranic instance of a crowd experiencing genuine self-reflection before reverting; a moment of conscience at the center of the surah
يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ (v.69) — the divine command to fire; the Quran's most dramatic natural-law override
لَوْ كَانَ فِيهِمَا آلِهَةٌ إِلَّا اللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَا (v.22) — the cosmic-order proof for tawhid: multiple gods → corruption; deductive rather than metaphorical
وَكَذَلِكَ نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (v.88) — Jonah's rescue generalized to all believers; the pivot of C′; تسبيح in darkness becomes a universal promise
وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ (v.107) — the most celebrated definition of prophetic mission; placed in A′
اقْتَرَبَ inclusio (vv.1/97): account approached → true promise approached; heedlessness declared (A) → confessed (B′)
رَتْق/فَتْق (v.30): heavens and earth were joined, then We separated them — cosmogony at B's opening
Fold sky like scroll (v.104) — the most vivid eschatological end-of-creation image; parallels Q.39:67
الزَّبُور (v.105): righteous servants inherit the earth — the only Quranic citation of the Psalms as prophetic source
16+ prophets/groups named in one surah — the widest prophetic catalogue in the Quran
فَاسْتَجَبْنَا ×4 (vv.76/84/88/90): Noah/Job/Jonah/Zechariah — C′'s refrain of divine response
cross_surah_flags: Q.23:52: إِنَّ هَذِهِ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً EXACT. Q.21:92 = Q.23:52. Both followed by تَقَطَّعُوا. Two consecutive surahs, identical verse.
Q.37:83–99: Abraham's fire narrative parallel. Q.21 (rhetorical trap + fire-command) ↔ Q.37 (قَلْب سَلِيم + 'I am ill' + sacrifice transition). Different emphases of same story.
Q.18:94/97: Gog/Magog. Q.21:96 (opened → descend from every elevation) ↔ Q.18:94/97 (Dhul-Qarnayn's wall → released). Two frames, same eschatological event.
Q.38:21–25: David/Solomon judging. Q.21:78–79 (civil case: field/sheep) ↔ Q.38:21–25 (test of David). Different judgment episodes.
Q.28:5 / Q.32:24: أَئِمَّة thread. Q.21:73 (Abraham's lineage as أَئِمَّة by lineage) → Q.28:5 (oppressed elevated) → Q.32:24 (through patience). Three routes to prophetic leadership.
Q.22:73 / Q.29:41: insect parables absent from Q.21. Q.21's futility argument is deductive (لَفَسَدَتَا) not metaphorical.

surahs.rhyme_type: Mixed [-iːr/-ūr/-iːz/-iːm/-ūn] — among the most varied in the corpus.

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدَافِعُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا … أُذِنَ لِلَّذِينَ يُقَاتَلُونَ بِأَنَّهُمْ ظُلِمُوا … الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيَارِهِمْ … صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ … وَلَيَنْصُرَنَّ اللَّهُ مَنْ يَنْصُرُهُ … الَّذِينَ إِنْ مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ … وَلِلَّهِ عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ

signals.thematic: Four-verse defense-and-authority block at exact zero offset: v.38 — God defends believers, doesn't like every traitor/ingrate. v.39 — أُذِنَ لِلَّذِينَ يُقَاتَلُونَ: first military permission in the Quran, passive and conditional — granted to those who are FOUGHT because they were WRONGED. v.40 — evicted only for saying 'God is our Lord'; if God didn't check some through others, monasteries/churches/synagogues/mosques where God's name is mentioned would be demolished — protection of ALL worship. v.41 — those given authority: establish prayer/zakāh/good/forbid evil → to God belongs عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ.
signals.lexical: يُدَافِعُ (v.38) — the only Quranic use of the مُفَاعَلَة form of د-ف-ع, implying ongoing active defense. لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ (v.40) echoes v.74 (A′). عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ (v.41) — Q.31:22 EXACT thread. صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ (v.40) — four faith traditions' houses in one verse.
signals.mirror: D (4v) at exact +0.000. Flanked by C (13v) and C′ (7v). v.37 (piety, not meat/blood, reaches God) → vv.38–41 (defend what reaches God) → v.46 (hearts blind, not eyes). The pivot bridges sacrifice (interior piety) to perception (interior sight).
signals.discourse: Shift from Hajj legislation (C: Abraham/Ka'ba/animals/rites) to the doctrinal justification for defensive warfare — the pivot is the hinge between sacred rites and sacred defense. Then C′ returns to historical narration (destroyed nations). The pivot is the surah's most consequential legal moment.
signals.rhyme: ظُلِمُوا/ظُلِمُوا/كَثِيرٍ/لَقَدِيرٌ/لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ/الصَّلَاةَ/الزَّكَاةَ/الْمُنْكَرِ/الْأُمُورِ — mixed terminals, consistent with surah's varied prosody.

block_map.label: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ①②: زَلْزَلَة السَّاعَة; resurrection proof (embryology); worship عَلَى حَرْفٍ → lost; God is الْحَقّ; six communities judged
Everything prostrates: heavens/earth/sun/moon/stars; two adversaries: fire garments + boiling water vs. gardens + silk; guided to صِرَاطِ الْحَمِيدِ

HAJJ legislation: Abraham designated House; proclaim الْحَجّ; sacrificial camels as شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ; لَنْ يَنَالَ اللَّهَ لُحُومُهَا (v.37): piety — not meat/blood — reaches God; الْمُخْبِتِينَ/الْمُحْسِنِينَ

PIVOT (+0.000) — God يُدَافِعُ (v.38); أُذِنَ: FIRST MILITARY PERMISSION (v.39, passive/conditional/defensive); صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ (v.40, four worship-houses); authority → prayer/zakāh/good/evil → عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ (v.41)
Nations who denied before; fallen cities + abandoned wells; لَا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصَارُ وَلَكِنْ تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ (v.46, most definitive blindness statement); God prolonged then seized

يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ③: only a clear warner; Satan's insertion → God abolishes + makes precise; those given knowledge → hearts submit; emigrants/killed → God provides; God is الْحَقّ + invoked = الْبَاطِل → الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ

يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ④: fly parable (v.73, only Quranic fly); مَا قَدَرُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ → لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ; يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا: جَاهِدُوا حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ; مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ → هُوَ سَمَّاكُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ; نِعْمَ الْمَوْلَى وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ

inclusios.note: vv.1/5/49/73: fear Lord → resurrection proof → I am warner → fly parable. Four summons to all humanity across the surah's arc.
D (v.40: God strong enough to protect worship) ↔ A′ (v.74: they didn't appraise God rightly → He is strong). Same divine pair framing the pivot and its echo.
A (v.6: God is الْحَقّ) ↔ B′ (v.62: God is الْحَقّ + what they invoke = الْبَاطِل → الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ). Q.31:30 EXACT.
C (v.34) and A′ (v.67): every nation has a rite. The Hajj block and the closing both establish universal worship-tradition continuity.
C: Abraham designated the House → A′: religion of your father Abraham, He named you Muslims. The surah opens Hajj with Abraham and closes the entire religious project with him.

special_features: أُذِنَ لِلَّذِينَ يُقَاتَلُونَ (v.39) — the Quran's first military permission: passive voice, conditional (because they were wronged), defensive in character; placed at exact geometric center
صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ (v.40) — the only Quranic verse listing four worship-house types from four traditions; criterion = God's name is mentioned
لَنْ يَنَالَ اللَّهَ لُحُومُهَا وَلَا دِمَاؤُهَا وَلَكِنْ يَنَالُهُ التَّقْوَى مِنْكُمْ (v.37) — the Quran's clearest statement that sacrifice's value is spiritual, not material
لَا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصَارُ وَلَكِنْ تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ الَّتِي فِي الصُّدُورِ (v.46) — the definitive Quranic statement on spiritual vs. physical blindness; explains the dead/deaf/blind thread
Fly parable (v.73) — the only Quranic mention of a fly; they cannot create one even gathered; if fly steals they cannot retrieve it — ضَعُفَ الطَّالِبُ وَالْمَطْلُوبُ
هُوَ سَمَّاكُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (v.78) — God named you Muslims, before and in this; closing identity declaration
جَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ (v.78) — the fullest jihād command; paired with مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ and نِعْمَ الْمَوْلَى وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ
Satan's insertion and God's abolishment (v.52) — the Quran's most explicit statement on Satanic interference in prophetic recitation and God's correction
Mixed Meccan-Medinan: eschatological terror (A) + Hajj ritual (C) + fighting permission (D) + theological argument (B′) — the widest thematic range in any single surah
cross_surah_flags: Q.2:190: fighting command escalation. Q.22:39 (أُذِنَ, passive permission) → Q.2:190 (وَقَاتِلُوا, imperative). Q.22 permits; Q.2 commands.
Q.29:41: insect parable parallel. Q.22:73 (fly: can't create) ↔ Q.29:41 (spider: weakest home). Two parables, same idol-futility argument.
Q.31:30: الْحَقّ/الْبَاطِل EXACT. Q.22:62 ↔ Q.31:30 — identical formula + identical divine-name conclusion.
Q.27:80–81 / Q.30:52–53 / Q.35:19–22: dead/deaf/blind thread. Q.22:46 (لَا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصَارُ وَلَكِنْ تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ) provides the explanation for the entire thread.
Q.29:6 / Q.29:69: jihād registers. Q.22:78 (communal imperative) ↔ Q.29:6 (self-benefit) ↔ Q.29:69 (divine guidance earned). Three registers.
Q.24:55: succession/authority parallel. Q.22:41 (authority → prayer/zakāh/good/evil) ↔ Q.24:55 (succession → establish religion + fear→security). Both ground political authority in worship.

surahs.rhyme_type: Nasal-final [-ūn/-iːn] dominant (116/118, 98.3%). [-ūn] at ~70%.

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ هُمْ مِنْ خَشْيَةِ رَبِّهِمْ مُشْفِقُونَ … وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ … وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لَا يُشْرِكُونَ … وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ … أُولَئِكَ يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْرَاتِ وَهُمْ لَهَا سَابِقُونَ

signals.thematic: Five-verse psychological portrait of fearful believers at exact geometric center (offset −0.004). Mirrors the opening's behavioral portrait (vv.1–11) but from the inside: apprehensive from fear (v.57) / believe in Lord's signs (v.58) / don't associate (v.59) / give while hearts are fearful (v.60) / THOSE hasten to good deeds and outstrip (v.61). The opening asks what believers DO; the center reveals what they FEEL. Both portraits use وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ syntax.
signals.lexical: وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ ×4 — same connective as vv.2–9. يُسَارِعُونَ (v.61: hasten to good) directly corrects C's false claim: أَنَّمَا نُمِدُّهُمْ بِهِ (v.55–56: 'We hasten good for them through wealth'). الْخَيْرَات (v.61) — the real acceleration is toward righteousness, not toward wealth. وَجِلَةٌ (v.60: fearful hearts) → unique psychological descriptor.
signals.mirror: D (5v) at offset −0.004. Between C (6v, factions/false confidence) and C′ (16v, confused hearts/arrogance/despair). The pivot is the calm eye: pure believers' interior between two descriptions of failure.
signals.discourse: Five consecutive إِنَّ/وَالَّذِينَ declaratives — no vocative, no imperative, no narrative. Pure characterization. Same وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ structure as vv.2–9. The center quotes its own opening's syntax: the surah's structural center IS its opening's interior.
signals.rhyme: مُشْفِقُونَ/يُؤْمِنُونَ/يُشْرِكُونَ/رَاجِعُونَ/سَابِقُونَ — five consecutive [-ūn], identical to the opening's prosodic pattern. The pivot sounds like the opening because it IS the opening's inner life.

block_map.label: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ; SEVEN ATTRIBUTES (vv.2–9): humble prayer/ill speech/zakāh/chastity/trusts/promises/maintain prayers → الْفِرْدَوْس eternal; EMBRYOLOGY: clay → sperm → clot → lump → bones → flesh → فَتَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخَالِقِينَ; seven heavens; rain → gardens; olive; livestock; ships

NOAH: 'just a man wanting precedence' → رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي → ship → drowned; UNNAMED GENERATION: هَيْهَاتَ هَيْهَاتَ (only doubled, hapax) → الصَّيْحَة; generations → messengers denied → Pharaoh; MOSES/AARON; JESUS/MARY; يَا أَيُّهَا الرُّسُلُ (only collective messenger-vocative); this أُمَّة is one
Divided religion → factions rejoicing; leave them in confusion; do they think wealth/children = نُسَارِعُ لَهُمْ (hasten good for them)? → don't perceive
PIVOT — INTERNAL PORTRAIT: apprehensive from fear (v.57) / believe in signs (v.58) / don't associate (v.59) / give with fearful hearts (v.60) / THOSE hasten يُسَارِعُونَ + outstrip سَابِقُونَ (v.61)

Don't charge soul beyond capacity → record speaks truth; hearts in confusion; haven't they reflected? Messenger unknown → possessed; if truth followed أَهْوَاء → heavens/earth ruined; seized affluent → cry out → despair

Hearing/sight/hearts → little grateful; THREE قُلْ questions (vv.84–89): earth/heavens/مَلَكُوت → 'to God' → remember/fear/deluded; no son, no deity with Him; بَرْزَخ (v.100, most explicit); Horn → no lineage → won't ask each other; ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ (v.96)

Heavy scales → الْمُفْلِحُونَ; light scales → Hell eternal; اخْسَئُوا وَلَا تُكَلِّمُونِ (v.108, harshest divine speech); believers' prayer → خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ → الْفَائِزُونَ; أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ عَبَثًا; فَتَعَالَى اللَّهُ الْمَلِكُ الْحَقُّ; لَا يُفْلِحُ الْكَافِرُونَ; closing duʿāʾ: وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ

inclusios.note: Success arc across the surah: declared (v.1: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ) → confirmed (v.102: الْمُفْلِحُونَ) → denied (v.117: لَا يُفْلِحُ الْكَافِرُونَ). The surah opens on success and closes on its negation.
Believers in A′ pray 'Lord, you are خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ' (v.109) → Prophet closes surah with identical phrase (v.118). The community's prayer becomes the Prophet's closing duʿāʾ.
Seven behavioral attributes (A, vv.2–9) + four psychological attributes (D, vv.57–60). Both use the same connective. Center quotes the opening's syntax.
Noah (v.26) + unnamed prophet (v.39) using the identical prayer. Two prophets, one formula.

C (v.56): 'do they think We نُسَارِعُ good for them through wealth?' → D (v.61): believers are those who يُسَارِعُونَ in good. False hastening (wealth) corrected by true hastening (righteousness) — five verses apart.

special_features: Two believer-portraits: behavioral (A, vv.1–11, 7 attributes) + psychological (D, vv.57–61, 4 attributes) — both using وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ and [-ūn] prosody
Embryology (vv.12–14): clay → sperm → clot → lump → bones → flesh → another creation → فَتَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخَالِقِينَ
هَيْهَاتَ هَيْهَاتَ (v.36) — the only doubled هَيْهَاتَ in the Quran; most emphatic denial of resurrection
يَا أَيُّهَا الرُّسُلُ (v.51) — the only collective vocative addressed to all messengers simultaneously
اخْسَئُوا فِيهَا وَلَا تُكَلِّمُونِ (v.108) — harshest divine speech to Hell's inhabitants; a command to be silent
بَرْزَخ (v.100) — the most explicit statement of the intermediate realm between death and resurrection
Three قُلْ questions (vv.84–89): earth/heavens/مَلَكُوت → 'to God' → remember/fear/deluded — confession sequence leading to logical indictment
وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ (v.60) — giving charity while hearts are fearful; the pivot's most distinctive interior state
أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ عَبَثًا (v.115) — the surah's climactic rhetorical question: purposeless creation negated
خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ ×2 (vv.109/118): the community's prayer answered by making it the Prophet's closing words
cross_surah_flags: Q.25:63–76: عِبَادُ الرَّحْمَنِ parallel. Q.23:1–11 (7 attributes → الْفِرْدَوْس) ↔ Q.25:63–76 (12 attributes → الْغُرْفَة). Q.23 adds internal portrait at center.
Q.18:107: الْفِرْدَوْس. Q.23:11 and Q.18:107 — the two surahs naming الْفِرْدَوْس as inheritance.
Q.37:125: أَحْسَنَ الْخَالِقِينَ. Q.23:14 ↔ Q.37:125. Same phrase, two surahs.
Q.25:1/10/61: تَبَارَكَ thread. Q.23:14 (فَتَبَارَكَ) extends thread to Q.23 as earlier deployment.
Q.26:89: قَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ vs. وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ. Q.26 names the ideal heart; Q.23 describes its interior feeling. Two approaches to the same phenomenon.

surahs.rhyme_type: Mixed [-iːm/-iːn/-ūn/-ūr/-āb] without single-zone dominance. Most prosodically varied pivot in corpus (6 different terminals in 6 verses).

pivot.verse_ar: اللَّهُ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ مَثَلُ نُورِهِ كَمِشْكَاةٍ … نُورٌ عَلَى نُورٍ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ لِنُورِهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ … وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللَّهُ لَهُ نُورًا فَمَا لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ

signals.thematic: Six-verse triptych: v.35 — آيَة النُّور: God IS the Light of the heavens and earth; five-nested metaphor (niche→lamp→glass→olive tree→oil) → نُورٌ عَلَى نُورٍ → God guides to His light whom He wills. vv.36–38 — men not distracted by trade from dhikr/prayer/zakāh, fearing Day. v.39 — disbelievers' deeds like سَرَاب (mirage): thirsty man finds nothing but God. v.40 — or like ظُلُمَات in deep sea: waves upon waves upon clouds, can't see hand → whoever God doesn't give light has NONE. Light → devotion → illusion → absolute darkness.
signals.lexical: نُور ×7 — root appears ONLY in the pivot (5× in v.35, 2× in v.40). نُورٌ عَلَى نُورٍ hapax. سَرَاب (v.39) most vivid false-hope metaphor. بَيْع/تِجَارَة (v.37) echoes the ifk: those who spread slander were 'distracted' from truth. مَثَل (v.35) echoes v.34.
signals.mirror: D (6v) at offset +0.078. Flanked by C (8v) and C′ (6v). Bridges: C (social legislation → 'distinct verses') → D (metaphysical Light/darkness) → C′ (cosmic glorification → 'distinct verses → straight path').
signals.discourse: Entirely declarative — no vocative, no imperative, no قُلْ. The legislative urgency of the first half and obedience demands of second half are SUSPENDED for pure metaphysical contemplation. Third-person declaration → description → parable → parable.
signals.rhyme: عَلِيمٌ [-iːm] / الْآصَالِ [-āl] / الْأَبْصَارِ [-ār] / حِسَابٍ [-āb] / الْحِسَابِ [-āb] / نُورٍ [-ūr]. Six different terminals in six verses — most prosodically varied pivot in corpus. Each terminal matches its verse's content.

block_map.label: سُورَةٌ أَنْزَلْنَاهَا وَفَرَضْنَاهَا (unique self-reference); zinā: 100 lashes; accuse without 4 witnesses → 80 lashes; لِعَان; if not for God's favor → تَوَّابٌ حَكِيمٌ

الْإِفْك (slander): وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ ×4 (most concentrated); 'great slander'; Day: tongues/hands/feet testify; accuse chaste believing women → cursed; evil words ↔ evil men / good words ↔ good men → مُبَرَّءُونَ

يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ②: don't enter houses without permission; believing men lower gaze; خُمُرِهِنَّ عَلَى جُيُوبِهِنَّ (most specific modesty instruction); marry the unmarried; sent distinct verses

PIVOT — آيَة النُّور (v.35): اللَّهُ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ; five-nested metaphor → نُورٌ عَلَى نُورٍ; men not distracted by trade (vv.36–38); MIRAGE (v.39): disbelievers' deeds = thirsty man finds nothing; DARKNESS (v.40): waves/clouds layered → whoever God doesn't give light has NONE

Everything glorifies God + birds; مُلْك of heavens/earth; rain from sky-mountains; God alternates night/day → lesson for أُولِي الْأَبْصَارِ; every creature from water; sent distinct verses → guides to صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ

'We obey' → party turns away → disease in hearts; 'سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا' → الْمُفْلِحُونَ; وَعَدَ اللَّهُ: لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ (v.55, most explicit succession promise) + establish religion + substitute fear with security

يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ③: permission at three times; puberty → full permission; post-menopausal modesty; don't treat Messenger's call like calling each other; to God belongs all → بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ

inclusios.note: Most concentrated deployment in the Quran — all four within the ifk passage (B). Each time trails off (vv.10/20) or names the consequence (vv.14/21). The Quran withholds completing the sentence to make the mercy feel more immediate.
vv.5/22/33/62 — transition points A/B/C/A′. Forgiveness and mercy bracket every major section.
ifk close (B) → permission legislation ×2 (A′). Knowing, Wise.
The نُور root appears ONLY in the pivot (vv.35–40). The surah's defining concept — its very name — exists solely at its center.
A′'s recurring formula. Echoes C′ (v.46: أَنْزَلْنَا آيَاتٍ مُبَيِّنَاتٍ) and A (v.1: فَرَضْنَاهَا).

special_features: v.1: سُورَةٌ أَنْزَلْنَاهَا وَفَرَضْنَاهَا — the only surah that names itself سُورَة and declares its own obligation (فَرَضْنَاهَا)
آيَة النُّور (v.35): the Quran's most elaborate five-nested metaphor; نُورٌ عَلَى نُورٍ hapax; نُور root ×7 exists ONLY at the pivot
وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ ×4 in ifk passage (B) — most concentrated divine-mercy-saves formula in the Quran; sentences twice left incomplete
لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ (v.55) — the Quran's most explicit conditional succession promise to believers
Twin counter-parables: سَرَاب (mirage, v.39) + ظُلُمَات layered (v.40) — the most vivid false-hope and total-darkness metaphors in the corpus
Six different rhyme terminals in six pivot verses — most prosodically varied pivot in the entire corpus
Pivot is entirely declarative: no vocative, no imperative, no قُلْ — legislative urgency suspended for metaphysical contemplation
C↔C′: social legislation (what humans must do consciously) ↔ cosmic glorification (what creation does by nature) — same obedience, different levels
B↔B′: speech violations (slander, ifk) ↔ obedience violations (hypocrisy, 'we obey' then turn away)
A↔A′: sexual boundary legislation ↔ domestic boundary legislation (permission)
cross_surah_flags: Q.33:53: house etiquette parallel. Q.24:27–29 (all houses) ↔ Q.33:53 (Prophet's houses specifically). Q.24 general; Q.33 restricted.
Q.33:35: gender-paired virtues. Q.24:30–31 (lower gaze, خُمُر) ↔ Q.33:35 (ten paired spiritual qualities). Legislation (Q.24) → qualities (Q.33).
Q.35:39 / Q.38:26 / Q.27:62: خَلَائِف/succession thread. Q.24:55 (لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ) most explicit. Thread: Q.24→Q.27→Q.35→Q.38.
Q.36:65 / Q.41:20–21: body-parts testify. Q.24:24 (tongues/hands/feet). Three surahs, different limbs.
Q.33:33: أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ. Q.24:11–20 (juridical clearing via ifk) ↔ Q.33:33 (divine purification declaration). Both protect Prophet's household.

surahs.rhyme_type: Tanwīn accusative [-iːrā/-ūrā/-āmā] dominant (75/77, 97.4%). No Muqaṭṭaʿāt.

pivot.verse_ar: أَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَهَهُ هَوَاهُ أَفَأَنْتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وَكِيلًا … أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَالْأَنْعَامِ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا

signals.thematic: Compressed destruction-catalogue (Moses+Aaron → destroyed; Noah → drowned → sign; ʿĀd/Thamūd/Rass/many → examples → destroyed; evil-rained town) followed by the mockery and the surah's most diagnostic verse: اتَّخَذَ إِلَهَهُ هَوَاهُ (v.43) — takes desire as god. Closes: like livestock, more astray. Pivot moves from historical evidence to psychological diagnosis.
signals.lexical: أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا ×3 (vv.34/42/44) concentrated at/near pivot. هَوَاهُ (v.43) — أَهْوَاء thread's most explicit deployment, earliest in corpus. نُشُورًا (v.40) echoes v.3. الْأَنْعَامِ (v.44) unique livestock comparison at this position.
signals.mirror: D (10v) at offset +0.006. Flanked by C (14v) and C′ (16v) — near-balanced. Bridges: C (eschatological + gradual revelation) → D (historical destruction + desire-worship) → C′ (cosmological evidence + creation signs).
signals.discourse: Rapid narrative compression (Moses/Noah/ʿĀd/Thamūd/Rass in 5v) → 2sg address: 'Have you seen?' (v.43) → 3rd-person diagnosis (v.44). Third-person narrative → prophetic address → universal diagnosis.
signals.rhyme: وَزِيرًا/تَدْمِيرًا/أَلِيمًا/نُشُورًا/رَسُولًا/سَبِيلًا/وَكِيلًا — tanwīn accusative [-ā]. Destruction and diagnosis share the surah's flowing prosody.
