structure_summary.note: 26-verse inverted structure with primary pivot at v.17 (5/5). Two secondary convergence zones: v.8 (4 signals — وُجُوهٌ exact bridge) and vv.25–26 (5 signals — divine reckoning close). Seven rhyme zones in 26 verses — highest zone density in dataset. Two morphological rhyme zones: مَفْعُولَة passive ptcp ×4 (vv.13–16) and passive perfect [-at] ×4 (vv.17–20). عَيْن antithetical bridge vv.5/12: same noun, opposite qualities, condemned↔rewarded. هَلْ أَتَاكَ opening: most intimate first verse in dataset — presupposes ongoing dialogue. Two direct Prophet addresses (v.1/v.21) frame vv.2–20. ر-ف-ع root cross-unit bridge: مَرْفُوعَة (v.13)↔رُفِعَت (v.18). Closing acceleration: zones 4/5/6 each exactly 2 verses (2+2+2).

verses.ar: هَلْ فِي ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٌ لِّذِي حِجْرٍ
أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ
الَّتِي لَمْ يُخْلَقْ مِثْلُهَا فِي الْبِلَادِ
فَأَمَّا الْإِنسَانُ إِذَا مَا ابْتَلَاهُ رَبُّهُ فَأَكْرَمَهُ وَنَعَّمَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَكْرَمَنِ
وَأَمَّا إِذَا مَا ابْتَلَاهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَهَانَنِ
كَلَّا إِذَا دُكَّتِ الْأَرْضُ دَكًّا دَكًّا
وَجِيءَ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِجَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَذَكَّرُ الْإِنسَانُ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُ الذِّكْرَىٰ

pivot.verse_ar: كَلَّا بَل لَّا تُكْرِمُونَ الْيَتِيمَ

secondary_convergence.note: كَلَّا second pillar. Theme: moral indictment → eschatological events. Mirror: opens second half of inverted structure. Discourse: second-person collective drops, إِذَا temporal opens the Day, cosmic passive verbs. Lexical: كَلَّا ×2, دَكًّا دَكًّا masdar reduplication. Prosodic: rhyme break [-akk], eschatological zone opens.

pivot.notes: كَلَّا first pillar: negates human moral inference (vv.15–16) AND opens moral indictment (vv.17–20) — faces backward and forward simultaneously. ك-ر-م root exhaustion completes here: root ×2 at v.15 (claimed honor) / antithetical هـ-و-ن gap at v.16 / negated at v.17 (لَا تُكْرِمُونَ) / absent from all subsequent verses. Second-person PLURAL enters for first time in surah. Rhyme break: [-an] ends, [-iːm] opens. Geometric center 15.5, v.17 offset +1.5, norm 0.050.
pivot.center_logic_note: single-verse primary v.17 (5/5, كَلَّا pillar) + 5/5 secondary at v.21 (second كَلَّا pillar); surah has two independent 5/5 kalla anchors.

double_kalla_architecture.v17: كَلَّا بَل لَّا تُكْرِمُونَ — negates vv.15–16 + opens indictment
double_kalla_architecture.v21: كَلَّا إِذَا دُكَّتِ — closes indictment + opens eschatology
double_kalla_architecture.note: كَلَّا appears exactly twice at structurally non-equivalent positions. Each instance faces both backward and forward: v.17 refutes the false inference AND opens the charge list; v.21 closes the charge list AND opens the Day. Together they bracket the moral indictment block [كَلَّا]+[four charges vv.17–20]+[كَلَّا]. Compare Q.96 triple كَلَّا (vv.6/15/19) — distributed as three pillars. Q.89's double deployment creates a tighter binary frame around a single moral unit.

karam_root_exhaustion_corrected.v15: أَكْرَمَهُ/أَكْرَمَنِ — root ك-ر-م ×2; human falsely claims divine honor
karam_root_exhaustion_corrected.v16: أَهَانَنِ — root هـ-و-ن; antithetical gap: ك-ر-م absent at parallel position
karam_root_exhaustion_corrected.v17: لَا تُكْرِمُونَ — root ك-ر-م negated; correction uses same root falsely invoked in v.15
karam_root_exhaustion_corrected.note: Root gap at v.16 is structural: the absence of ك-ر-م at the parallel position is what creates the space for the antithetical هـ-و-ن. The correction in v.17 uses the same root that was falsely claimed — 'you do not honor the orphan' directly contradicts 'He honored me.' Root exhaustion confirmed after v.17: ك-ر-م absent from vv.18–30.

insan_distal_frame.v15: فَأَمَّا الْإِنسَانُ — deluded man; falsely interprets divine testing
insan_distal_frame.v23: يَتَذَكَّرُ الْإِنسَانُ — regretful man; remembers on the Day too late
insan_distal_frame.note: الْإِنسَانُ appears exactly twice in the surah at structurally symmetric positions (v.15 opens the moral argument; v.23 closes its eschatological consequence). Between the two appearances, discourse shifts to collective second-person plural 'you' (vv.17–20) — the individual الْإِنسَانُ frames the collective indictment. Structurally equivalent to: Al-Balad's الْبَلَد ×2 intra-unit frame; Al-Layl's الْأَشْقَى/الْأَتْقَى distal pair. A content noun marking the boundaries of the surah's moral argument.

reflexive_interrogative_apodosis.v5: هَلْ فِي ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٌ لِّذِي حِجْرٍ
reflexive_interrogative_apodosis.note: Unusual oath resolution: every other oath surah in dataset delivers an apodosis (declaration, verdict, or creation statement). Q.89 v.5 reflects the oath chain back at the listener as a question: 'Is there not in that an oath for one of perception?' The oath is evaluated by challenging the audience to assess its sufficiency. ذَٰلِكَ anaphoric to the entire four-verse oath chain. لِذِي حِجْرٍ implies the rational audience as addressee. The apodosis IS the challenge to respond.

zones.note: Oath unit unified by final /r/ across five distinct vowel environments ([-ajr]/[-aʃr]/[-atr]/[-asr]/[-ijr]). The consonant holds, the vowel varies.

[-aːd] ×7 (vv.6–12), [-aːb] break at v.13 (punishment poured sounds different from transgression), [-aːd] return at v.14 (المِرْصَادِ closes البِلَادِ chain). الْبِلَادِ exact return v.8↔v.11 within zone.
أَكْرَمَنِ/أَهَانَنِ — first-person object suffix -نِ creates the terminal in both verses. The human voice claiming divine judgment is phonetically marked as a unit.
كَلَّا opens; [-iːm] v.17 (الْيَتِيمَ), [-iːn] v.18 (الْمِسْكِينِ), [-amm] ×2 vv.19–20 (masdar terminals). حُبًّا جَمًّا (v.20) intra-verse masdar+elative pair seals the indictment block.

[-akk]/[-aff]/[-a]/[-aːtiː]/[-ahad]×2. Eschatological unit resists terminal uniformity — identical pattern to Al-Balad Zone 5 (vv.17–20) and Al-Layl varied close. أَحَدٌ ×2 consecutive (vv.25–26) seals the unit.

Four verses, four distinct terminals. Righteous soul unit phonetically unique — no zone rhyme. Intimacy encoded in phonetic singularity. د-خ-ل imperative ×2 (vv.29–30) provides aural parallelism without terminal identity.

nafs_mutmainna_close.note: Surah closes with direct vocative address to the reassured soul. رَبِّكِ at v.28 is the sole feminine divine possessive in the surah. Three imperatives (ارْجِعِي/فَادْخُلِي/وَادْخُلِي) close the surah on invitation rather than warning — contrast with Q.90's مُؤْصَدَة sealed-fire close and Q.92's Fire close. The د-خ-ل imperative pair (vv.29–30) mirrors the construction of Q.93's فَحَدِّثْ sole affirmative command close but as a paired entry sequence.

Reflexive interrogative apodosis — oath challenged back at the listener

structure_summary.note: 30-verse inverted structure with primary pivot at v.17 (كَلَّا first pillar; 5/5) and secondary 5-signal convergence at v.21 (كَلَّا second pillar). First 30-verse surah in dataset. Six rhyme zones of decreasing regularity: /-r/ consonant cluster (oath) → [-aːd] dominant (history) → [-an] first-person suffix (human voice) → [-iːm/-iːn/-amm] (indictment) → varied rapid shifts (eschatology) → intimate variety (soul address). Double كَلَّا brackets moral indictment block. ك-ر-م root exhaustion at v.17. الْإِنسَانُ distal frame (vv.15/23). Reflexive interrogative apodosis (v.5): unique oath resolution in dataset. Eschatological zone phonetically unstable — confirmed pattern across Q.89/Q.90/Q.92. Closes on جَنَّتِي intimate possessive.

verses.ar: أَيَحْسَبُ أَن لَّن يَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَحَدٌ
أَوْ إِطْعَامٌ فِي يَوْمٍ ذِي مَسْغَبَةٍ
ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْمَرْحَمَةِ
وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِنَا هُمْ أَصْحَابُ الْمَشْأَمَةِ

pivot.verse_ar: فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ الْعَقَبَةَ ۞ وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا الْعَقَبَةُ

secondary_convergence.note: Divine gift enumeration: eyes/tongue-lips/two-paths. Dual [-ayn] zone: grammar and rhyme fused. Divine We returns (أَلَمْ نَجْعَل). 4-signal boundary: theme + mirror + discourse + prosody.

pivot.notes: Compound seam: v.11 declares negated failure (فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ الْعَقَبَةَ — he has not broken through the difficult pass), v.12 interrogates the pass directly (وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا الْعَقَبَةُ). Root ع-ق-ب ×2 across consecutive verses — sole such pair in surah. Rhyme break at v.11: [-ayn] zone ends, [-qabah] opens. فَلَا negated past + وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ direct address = two distinct discourse modes in consecutive verses. The العَقَبَة root then extends to رَقَبَةٍ (v.13) — the first act of breaking through the pass phonetically sounds like the pass itself. Seam midpoint 11.5, geometric center 10.5, offset +1.0, norm 0.050.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.11–12, 5/5); max secondary 4/5 (divine gift enumeration vv.8–10). Single resolved compound center.

la_uqsimu_direct_oath.note: Every other oath surah in this dataset opens with waw al-qasam (وَ) — impersonal, speaker invisible. Q.90 opens with divine first-person declaring the oath directly: 'I swear by this city.' Speaker present from the first word. Moreover, v.2 inserts the Prophet as addressee within the oath unit itself ('and you are free within it'). No other oath unit in this sequence places the addressee inside the oath chain. The oath is both uniquely voiced and uniquely personal.

zones.note: Seals oath+apodosis+self-delusion together. The البلد/ولد/كبد/أحد cluster: consonant-final [-d] with varying preceding vowel. Rhyme unifies three thematic units.

Dual morphological suffix. Grammar and rhyme identical: the two eyes/two lips/two paths are grammatically dual AND phonetically dual. Only case in dataset where a grammatical category generates a rhyme zone.
العَقَبَة ×2 then رَقَبَة: the -ق-ب cluster fuses the obstacle and the first act of breaking through it at the level of sound. The pass and the slave's neck are phonetically the same.
Three consecutive مَفْعَلَة pattern circumstantial nouns: مَسْغَبَة/مَقْرَبَة/مَتْرَبَة. Grammar and sound uniform across all three mercy objects.
Rapid terminal shifts: [-amah]/[-anah]/[-amah]/[-adah]. No single sound dominates. Destiny bifurcation is phonetically unstable. مُؤْصَدَة closes on the most stopped terminal in the surah.

dual_morphology_generates_rhyme_zone.note: The dual morphological suffix of Arabic (-ayn) produces a terminal rhyme zone where grammar and rhyme are identical. The two eyes / two lips / two paths are each grammatically dual (a pair) and phonetically dual (each terminal ends in -ayn). The morphological form of the gift IS the sound of the gift's terminal. Only case in dataset where a single grammatical category (dual number) generates and exhausts a complete rhyme zone.

aqabah_raqabah_phonetic_fusion.note: Root ع-ق-ب (pass) and root ر-ق-ب (neck) both carry the -ق-ب consonant cluster and produce the same [-qabah] terminal sound. The first act of breaking through the difficult pass (freeing a slave's neck) phonetically sounds like the pass itself. The structural seam (vv.11–12) and its first definition (v.13) are phonetically fused into a three-verse sonic unit.

maf3alah_morphological_echo.note: The مَفْعَلَة (maf'ala) pattern noun appears twice in the surah at structurally distinct positions: (1) vv.14–16: three circumstantial nouns of condition — مَسْغَبَة (hunger-day)/مَقْرَبَة (near-orphan)/مَتْرَبَة (dust-destitution) — defining the mercy acts of the pass. (2) vv.18–19: الْمَيْمَنَة (right companions)/الْمَشْأَمَة (left companions) — the binary destiny declaration. The same grammatical form used for the mercy-conditions reappears in the destiny-conditions. A morphological echo connecting the acts of mercy to their recompense.

balad_intra_unit_inclusio.v2: الْبَلَدِ — this city (Prophet free within it)
balad_intra_unit_inclusio.note: Exact repeat of الْبَلَد in consecutive verses (vv.1–2) within the oath unit itself — the only verbatim consecutive terminal pair in the surah. The city is sworn by (v.1) and then characterized (v.2) in immediate succession. The inclusio is intra-unit rather than across the surah: it frames the oath's first two objects rather than the surah's opening/closing.

[-ayn]×3 sealed; dual zone complete; two paths moral duality in rhyme

رَقَبَة [-qabah]×3; phonetic fusion: pass=neck; most compressed verse

ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْمَرْحَمَةِ

Apodosis — man created into hardship (كَبَد)
Self-deluded man — أَيَحْسَبُ brackets boast

Culmination — ثُمَّ; faith + patience + compassion
Binary destiny — companions of right ↔ left; sealed fire

structure_summary.note: 20-verse inverted structure with compound seam at vv.11–12. Five rhyme zones of decreasing regularity (7+3+3+3+4-varied): [-ad] seals oath+apodosis+self-delusion; [-ayn] dual morphology generates rhyme zone (grammar=sound); [-qabah] fuses العَقَبَة×2 + رَقَبَة; [-abah] marks three مَفْعَلَة mercy objects; Zone 5 varied as destiny bifurcates. لَا أُقْسِمُ: first-person divine oath with addressee within oath unit — unique in dataset. مَفْعَلَة morphological echo: mercy-conditions (vv.14–16) reappear as destiny-conditions (vv.18–19). Structural movement: oath establishes hardship → self-delusion exposed → gifts enumerated → failure declared → pass defined → completion condition → binary destiny.

verses.ar: فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ نَاقَةَ اللَّهِ وَسُقْيَاهَا
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِذَنبِهِمْ فَسَوَّاهَا

pivot.verse_ar: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن زَكَّاهَا ۞ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَن دَسَّاهَا

secondary_convergence.note: Four-فَ compression; فَسَوَّاهَا closes سَوَّاهَا bridge from v.7 (creation↔destruction); ك-ذ-ب ×2 narrative frame closes; دَمْدَمَ onomatopoeic quadrilateral; رَبُّهُمْ punishment agent named; most rhythmically dense verse in surah.

pivot.notes: Compound apodosis delivering the resolution of the entire 8-verse oath sequence (vv.1–8). Two-verse antithetical couplet: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن زَكَّاهَا (succeeded / purified) ↔ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَن دَسَّاهَا (doomed / corrupted). قَدْ emphatic ×2 — sole consecutive قَدْ pair in surah. زَكَّاهَا/دَسَّاهَا antithetical gemination pair (heavy كّ ↔ heavy سّ). The 8-verse oath builds to this moral verdict; the 5-verse Thamud narrative (vv.11–15) is the historical instantiation of the 'doomed' verdict. Full-surah uniform [-aha] terminal makes rhythm breaks (at v.8 فَ, v.9 قَدْ) the only prosodic differentiation available.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.9–10, apodosis couplet, 5/5) + 5/5 secondary at v.14 (four-فَ compression, فَسَوَّاهَا bridge closes); primary confirmed by position.

seven_oath_sequence.note: Longest oath sequence in dataset (7 verses). Progressive movement: cosmic exterior → human interior. Each group introduced by وَ. Oath culminates in the human soul (v.7) as the object of divine fashioning — making the soul the apodosis target. The oath's structural logic: cosmic phenomena are invoked in order to direct attention inward to the human moral interior.

sawwaha_creation_destruction_bridge.v7: وَنَفْسٍ وَمَا سَوَّاهَا — the One Who fashioned the soul
sawwaha_creation_destruction_bridge.v14: فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِذَنبِهِمْ فَسَوَّاهَا — levelled them to the ground
sawwaha_creation_destruction_bridge.note: Root س-و-ي ×2 at the precise junction of two major structural units: oath culmination (v.7) and narrative punishment climax (v.14). Semantic deployment is antithetical: divine creation-fashioning ↔ divine destruction-levelling. Same root, same terminal sound (-هَا), opposite valences. In a full-surah uniform [-aha] environment, this is the only verbatim root-terminal pair crossing a major thematic boundary — equivalent in structural function to الْحُسْنَى ×2 in Al-Layl. Fourth confirmed cross-unit structural bridge in sequence (Q.91–Q.94), extending the cluster.

full_surah_uniform_aha_terminal.note: All 15 verse terminals carry the [-aha] suffix without exception. Second full-surah uniform terminal in dataset (Q.92 holds first instance at 21 verses). The [-aha] suffix is structurally distinct from the alif maqsura [-a] of Q.92/Q.93 — it carries an additional consonantal element (-ها) — but its structural function is identical: it cannot mark thematic boundaries because it never varies. All structural detection via passes 1–4. Only prosodic differentiation available: rhythm breaks (v.8 فَ, v.9 قَدْ) and intra-verse intensity markers (دَمْدَمَ v.14, geminated زَكَّاهَا/دَسَّاهَا vv.9–10).

damdama_climax_marker.note: Quadrilateral reduplicated root دَمْدَمَ (rumbling/crushing/overwhelming) is phonetically unique in the surah. In a uniform [-aha] terminal environment, the onomatopoeic interior of v.14 carries the prosodic weight that the terminal cannot. Parallel function to زُلْزِلَتِ in Q.99 v.1 and أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ in Q.95 v.5 — intra-verse root reduplication marking point of maximum thematic intensity.

cross_surah_laylaghshaha_echo.q91_v4: وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَاهَا — the night when it covers [the sun]
cross_surah_laylaghshaha_echo.q92_v1: وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَى — the night when it covers
cross_surah_laylaghshaha_echo.note: Near-verbatim oath construction shared across Q.91 v.4 and Q.92 v.1. In Q.92, the night-covering opens the surah as its first oath object. In Q.91, the same construction is the fourth oath in the solar sequence, with the feminine pronoun suffix (-hā) referring back to the sun. The construction is otherwise identical. Q.91 and Q.92 may form a structural pair: Q.91's solar sequence contains Q.92's opening oath as one element within a larger sequence.

phonetic_frame_v1_v15.note: Phonetic frame confirmed, lexical inclusio absent. v.1 وَضُحَاهَا and v.15 عُقْبَاهَا share the [-aha] terminal suffix at surah opening and closing but carry no shared root. The frame is sonic, not semantic — it closes the uniform [-aha] environment on the same sound it opened with. Distinct from the سَوَّاهَا/فَسَوَّاهَا root-bridge (vv.7/14) which carries both phonetic identity AND semantic antithesis.

signal_table.prosody_note: [-aha]×7; oath sealed; سَوَّاهَا bridge v.7→v.14 opens
فَ replaces وَ; intra-verse [-aha] doubling; unique in surah
دَسَّاهَا geminated; antithetical gemination pair vv.9–10 sealed

signal_table.verse_ar: فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ نَاقَةَ اللَّهِ وَسُقْيَاهَا
signal_table.prosody_note: [-aha]×13; reported speech; فَقَالَ; sole direct speech

signal_table.verse_ar: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِذَنبِهِمْ فَسَوَّاهَا
signal_table.lexical_signal: كَذَّبُوهُ ×2↓ sealed; سَوَّاهَا ×2↓ bridge sealed; رَبُّهُمْ; دَمْدَمَ
signal_table.prosody_note: دَمْدَمَ quadrilateral onomatopoeic; four-فَ compression; فَسَوَّاهَا exact root echo v.7; most dense verse

signal_table.lexical_signal: وَلَا يَخَافُ; عُقْبَاهَا echoes وَضُحَاهَا v.1
signal_table.prosody_note: وَلَا isolated; surah close; [-aha] frame sealed

block_map.label: Oath group 1 — solar/temporal (sun/moon/day/night)
Oath group 2 — cosmic structural (heaven/earth + builders)
Oath group 3 — human soul; سَوَّاهَا bridge opens
Hinge — فَ opens; inspiration declared; فُجُور/تَقْوَى pair
PIVOT — قَدْ apodosis; purified↔corrupted antithetical couplet
Thamud narrative — denial/wicked/warning/punishment
Frame close — divine sovereignty; عُقْبَاهَا echoes v.1

structure_summary.note: 15-verse linear-progressive structure (7+1+2+4+1). Second full-surah uniform terminal in dataset ([-aha] suffix, distinct from Q.92 alif maqsura [-a] but structurally equivalent). Pivot at compound apodosis vv.9–10: entire 8-verse oath sequence (longest oath unit in dataset) resolves into two-verse moral verdict. Thamud narrative (vv.11–14) instantiates the 'doomed' verdict historically. سَوَّاهَا root bridge (vv.7/14) is the only cross-unit prosodic signal in the uniform terminal environment: creation-fashioning ↔ destruction-levelling from same root. دَمْدَمَ onomatopoeic quadrilateral marks punishment climax. Q.92 v.1 echo at Q.91 v.4 (وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَاهَا / يَغْشَى). Dominant architecture: linear-progressive rather than inverted ring — Q.91 is the first surah in dataset where the structural movement is directional rather than mirrored.

verses.ar: وَمَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مَالُهُ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰ
وَمَا لِأَحَدٍ عِندَهُ مِن نِّعْمَةٍ تُجْزَىٰ

pivot.verse_ar: وَمَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مَالُهُ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰ

secondary_convergence.note: Divine plural → sole first-person singular (فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ); most intimate threat mode; نَارًا تَلَظَّى Fire named; geminated ظّ onomatopoeic; direct second-person re-engaged.

pivot.notes: Sole rhetorical question in surah; functional hinge between human-case block (vv.5–10) and divine-authority/destiny block (vv.12–21). مَالَهُ introduced here (v.11) and crosses to righteous giver (v.18) — identical noun encoding the moral contrast: wealth hoarded avails nothing at death; wealth given produces purification. Longest verse in surah by syllable weight. In a surah where all 21 terminals are [-a], rhythm and verse-length are the only prosodic differentiation; v.11 is the heaviest rhythmic unit. تَرَدَّى (falling/ruin) unique in surah — the withholder's fate named metaphorically at the precise pivot.
pivot.center_logic_note: single-verse primary v.11 (5/5, functional hinge) + 5/5 secondary at v.14 (divine singular first-person declaration); primary confirmed by geometric center.

full_surah_uniform_terminal.note: First surah in dataset where alif maqsura covers every single verse without exception across a text of substantial length. Compare Q.93 (8-verse zone then break) and Q.96 (9-verse zone then break). In Al-Layl, prosodic architecture is entirely non-functional as a boundary marker — every thematic cut and discourse shift is phonetically invisible at the terminal level. Structural boundaries detectable only through passes 1–4. The only prosodic differentiation available is through internal aural markers (gemination, onomatopoeia), verse length/rhythm breaks, and lexical echo within the terminal word itself (الْحُسْنَى ×2; لِلْيُسْرَى/لِلْعُسْرَى near-pair; الْأَشْقَى/الْأَتْقَى antithetical pair).

three_layer_antithetical_architecture.note: Three nested levels of binary opposition each encoded by antithetical near-identical sound at the terminal. The surah's argument is constructed as a single descending antithesis: cosmic → behavioral → eschatological. Each layer uses phonemic pairing to mark the opposition even within a uniform [-a] environment.

mal_cross_unit_bridge.v11: وَمَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مَالُهُ إِذَا تَرَدَّى — withholder's hoarded wealth avails nothing
mal_cross_unit_bridge.v18: الَّذِي يُؤْتِي مَالَهُ يَتَزَكَّى — righteous one's given wealth produces purification
mal_cross_unit_bridge.note: Identical noun مَالَهُ crossing the pivot (v.11) to mark the moral contrast at vv.11/18. Third confirmed cross-unit lexical bridge in dataset (Q.93 يَتِيم lexical-identity bridge; Q.94 semantic-opposition bridge; Q.92 مَالَهُ identical-noun antithetical bridge). Pattern: a single noun bearing opposite moral valences on each side of the structural seam.

husna_binary_frame.v6: وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنَى — believed in the best
husna_binary_frame.note: Exact terminal word ×2 across non-consecutive verses; sole verbatim cross-verse terminal match in surah. In a 21-verse uniform [-a] environment, the الْحُسْنَى repeat is the only prosodic differentiator available and carries disproportionate structural weight. The belief/denial axis is marked by identical sound — the same word names what the giver accepts and the withholder rejects.

yusra_usra_antithetical_apodosis.note: Near-verbatim apodosis pair; exact verbal phrase + prepositional antithesis. لِلْيُسْرَى / لِلْعُسْرَى share [-usra] vowel and consonant structure with only initial [y/ʕ] differing. Strongest aural antithesis in the case unit; the divine response to each camp is phonetically near-identical, making the contrast depend entirely on a single consonant.

lasawfa_yardhaa_cross_surah.note: وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَى (v.21) closes Al-Layl on the identical construction as Ad-Duha v.5 (وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضَى). Both surahs close the righteous trajectory with لَسَوْفَ + يَرْضَى satisfaction. Cross-surah signal: Q.92 and Q.93 are structurally paired companion surahs, and their shared terminal construction confirms thematic unity across the pair.

divine_person_shift_at_warning.v14: فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ — I have warned you (first-person singular)
divine_person_shift_at_warning.note: Sole first-person singular in surah. All preceding divine speech uses first-person plural نَا (vv.7, 10, 12, 13). The shift from 'We' to 'I' at the warning verse is the most intimate and direct address mode in the surah. Divine plural announces sovereignty (v.12–13); divine singular delivers the personal warning (v.14). Pattern not yet confirmed in other surahs in dataset.

signal_table.prosody_note: لِلْيُسْرَى antithetical pair opens; نَا enters
الْحُسْنَى exact repeat v.6↔v.9 sealed; sole verbatim match

signal_table.verse_ar: وَمَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مَالُهُ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰ
signal_table.prosody_note: longest verse; تَرَدَّى phonetic weight; sole rhetorical question
[-a]×12; لَلْهُدَى emphatic لَ; divine sovereignty declared
تَلَظَّى geminated ظّ; onomatopoeic; sole singular أَنَا; most intense verse

signal_table.verse_ar: وَمَا لِأَحَدٍ عِندَهُ مِن نِّعْمَةٍ تُجْزَىٰ

signal_table.prosody_note: الْأَعْلَى أَفْعَل superlative; divine title echoes human pair vv.15/17
shortest verse; double future marker; maximum prosodic stillness; cross-surah echo Q.93v5

block_map.label: Oath — night/day/creation; antithetical triplet
Apodosis — your efforts are diverse; إِنَّ verdict
Case 1 — giver/fearer/believer; ease toward ease
Case 2 — withholder/self-sufficient/denier; ease toward difficulty
PIVOT — مَالَهُ bridge; sole question; falling; hinge
Divine authority — guidance/Hereafter/first life; إِنَّ
Warning + الْأَشْقَى — Fire; sole singular I; mirrors D
الْأَتْقَى — righteous; مَالَهُ given; satisfaction; mirrors C

structure_summary.note: 21-verse inverted structure (3+1+3+3+1+2+1+2+5). First surah in dataset with full-surah uniform [-a] terminal — prosodic architecture entirely non-functional as boundary marker; all structural detection via passes 1–4. Three-layer antithetical argument: cosmic (vv.1–3) → behavioral (vv.5–10) → eschatological (vv.15–17), each encoded in phonemic pairing. v.11 is the functional hinge between human-case block and divine-authority/destiny block: sole rhetorical question, longest verse, مَالَهُ bridge, تَرَدَّى unique. Two 5-signal zones (v.11 primary, v.14 secondary). الْحُسْنَى ×2 (vv.6/9) is the only prosodic differentiator in the uniform terminal environment. وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَى (v.21) cross-surah echo of Q.93 v.5 — companion pair confirmed.

pivot.verse_ar: وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلًا فَأَغْنَىٰ ۞ فَأَمَّا الْيَتِيمَ فَلَا تَقْهَرْ

secondary_convergence.note: Oath resolves; رَبُّكَ named for first time; double مَا negation unique in surah; inner mirror B opens; tonal break within sustained [-a] zone. Opens consolation unit and رَبُّكَ triple frame.
Sole affirmative command; نِعْمَة sole occurrence; رَبِّكَ triple frame closes; [-ith] prosodically isolated terminal — geminated دّ unique in surah; surah close.

pivot.notes: Primary seam: benefaction unit (vv.6–8) ends; command unit (vv.9–11) opens. يَتِيم crosses the boundary (v.6 object of divine care → v.9 object of human obligation) — only content word confirmed across both units; the logic of the command is encoded in the word's placement. Rhyme break [-a]→[-ar] at v.9 is the most structurally loaded single rhyme shift in the surah: the 8-verse [-a] zone ends and the command unit opens simultaneously. Discourse flips prophet from passive recipient to obligated agent. Three 5-signal convergence zones (vv.8–9, v.3, v.11) — distributed architecture; no single unmirrored center. رَبُّكَ triple frame (vv.3/5/11) frames the direct-address units while absent from oath and benefaction units.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.8–9, 5/5) + two independent 5/5 secondaries: v.3 (oath resolves) and v.11 (sole affirmative command); surah has three 5/5 zones.

eight_verse_alif_maqsura_zone.note: 8 consecutive verses on the same terminal spanning oath, consolation, and benefaction units without deviation. Second-longest uniform terminal zone in dataset (Q.96 holds record at 9 verses). Within this zone, the terminal is prosodically inert as a boundary marker — thematic boundaries in vv.1–8 are detectable only through passes 1–4, not pass 5. The break at v.9 is consequently the most architecturally loaded single rhyme shift in the surah.

yatim_cross_unit_bridge.v6: أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيمًا فَآوَى — orphan: object of divine care
yatim_cross_unit_bridge.v9: فَأَمَّا الْيَتِيمَ فَلَا تَقْهَرْ — orphan: object of human obligation
yatim_cross_unit_bridge.note: Only content word confirmed to cross the benefaction/command thematic boundary. The causal logic (because you were sheltered as an orphan, do not oppress the orphan) is encoded in the word's placement rather than stated explicitly. Compare Q.94's verbal bridge (burden removed → now take up labor) which operates by semantic opposition; Q.93's bridge operates by lexical identity across units.

rabbuka_triple_frame.v3: رَبُّكَ — opens consolation unit; oath resolved
rabbuka_triple_frame.v5: رَبُّكَ — closes consolation unit; future gift
rabbuka_triple_frame.v11: رَبِّكَ — closes command unit; favor to be declared
rabbuka_triple_frame.note: Divine relational title at three structurally symmetric positions: opens consolation (v.3) / closes consolation (v.5) / closes command (v.11). Absent from oath unit (vv.1–2) and benefaction unit (vv.6–8) where divine agency is implied but not named. رَبُّكَ thus frames both direct-address units (consolation, command) while absent from both impersonal units (oath, benefaction narration). Most precise positional leitwort deployment in dataset where a term marks opening and closing of non-consecutive units.

wajada_triple_chain.note: Root و-ج-د ×3 across consecutive verses; triple discovery formula (orphan / lost / poor); confined entirely to benefaction unit; absent before and after. Most metrically regular unit in surah. Mirrors the فَأَمَّا/وَأَمَّا triple of vv.9–11 as structural parallel across units.

fa_amma_triple_command.note: Triple conditional partition frame mirroring the triple وَجَدَكَ of vv.6–8. Two prohibitions + one affirmative command. The affirmative close (فَحَدِّثْ) is the sole positive imperative in the surah and stands phonetically isolated by its [-ith] terminal break.

signal_table.prosody_note: [-a] anchor opens; oath register; no addressee
[-a]×2; oath pair sealed; light↔dark phonetic parallel
[-a]×3; tonal shift within zone; مَا double negation rhythmic emphasis
[-a]×5; لَسَوْفَ emphatic lām + future; consolation peaks
[-a]×6; rhetorical question opens; فَ chain opens; past tense enters
[-a]×7; فَهَدَى فَ chain ×2; question form drops
[-a]×8 closes; فَ chain ×3 closes; و-ج-د triple complete

signal_table.lexical_signal: فَأَمَّا; يَتِيم ×2 bridge v.6↔v.9↑; فَلَا تَقْهَرْ
signal_table.prosody_note: rhyme break [-ar]; [-a] zone ends; فَلَا تَ- opens; prophet→obligated agent
[-ar]×2; فَلَا تَنْهَرْ near-rhyme pair with v.9

signal_table.lexical_signal: رَبِّكَ ×3↓ frame closes; نِعْمَة sole↑; فَحَدِّثْ
signal_table.prosody_note: [-ith] break; geminated دّ; فَحَدِّثْ phonetically isolated; sole affirmative command

block_map.label: Oath — الضُّحَى/اللَّيْل antithetical pair
Consolation — رَبُّكَ opens; future gift promised
Benefaction — و-ج-د triple; past gifts enumerated
SEAM — يَتِيم bridge; rhyme break; recipient→agent
Command — فَأَمَّا/وَأَمَّا triple; mirrors consolation
Frame close — رَبِّكَ ×3; نِعْمَة; فَحَدِّثْ

structure_summary.note: 11-verse inverted structure (2+3+3+3). No single unmirrored center — three 5-signal convergence zones distributed across the surah (vv.8–9 seam, v.3, v.11). 8-verse alif maqsura zone (vv.1–8) makes the rhyme prosodically inert within the first three units; the break at v.9 is the surah's single most loaded structural marker. يَتِيم bridge (vv.6/9) is the only cross-unit content word and encodes the surah's causal logic implicitly. رَبُّكَ triple frame (vv.3/5/11) precisely marks both direct-address units while absent from both impersonal units. فَحَدِّثْ closes as sole affirmative command, prosodically and semantically isolated.

pivot.verse_ar: فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا ۞ إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا
pivot.notes: Only compound pivot in dataset where both verses are nearly verbatim identical (فَ deleted v.6 only). Thematic shift at v.5: divine past-action enumeration → impersonal timeless promise. Divine 'We' (نَا) disappears entirely at v.5 — most radical register change in surah. Rhyme break [-ak]→[-an] at v.5 co-extensive with thematic cut. The deletion of فَ is the sole phonetic change: فَإِنَّ (consequence) → إِنَّ (bare absolute assertion) — second iteration strips the conditional and makes the promise categorical. First surah in dataset where compound pivot is constituted by verbatim self-repetition rather than a structural seam.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.5–6, 5/5, verbatim doubled verse); no secondaries registered. Smallest compound seam in dataset (vc=8).

three_zone_rhyme_coextensive.note: Only surah in dataset where every rhyme-zone boundary is simultaneously a thematic-unit boundary with zero lag or offset. The rhyme zones ARE the thematic structure. Compare Q.100 (4 zones, 11 verses) and Q.107 (2 zones, 7 verses) — both have lag; Q.94 has none. Tightest rhyme-to-theme ratio in dataset.

r_kaf_cluster_gift_unit.note: Four consecutive second-person possessive nouns, each carrying medial /r/ before the -كَ suffix (صَدْر- / وِزْر- / ظَهْر- / ذِكْر-). The /r/-كَ cluster is sustained across all four gift verses without exception. Tightest 4-verse terminal architecture of any gift-unit in this sequence. Prosodic density phonetically marks the unit of divine benefaction.

verbatim_central_doubling.note: Only instance in dataset of a full verse repeated at the structural center of its surah in consecutive verses. Compare: Q.109 vv.3/5 — non-adjacent exact duplication; Q.82 vv.17–18 — consecutive exact duplication at end; Q.102 vv.3–4 — consecutive modified (ثُمَّ prefix). Q.94 is the only case where consecutive exact repetition IS the structural center. The فَ-to-bare-إِنَّ shift intensifies assertion: conditional consequence becomes absolute affirmation.

kaf_suffix_frame.opening: لَكَ v.1 ("did We not expand for you")
kaf_suffix_frame.closing: رَبِّكَ v.8 ("to your Lord direct your longing")
kaf_suffix_frame.note: Second-person relational suffix -كَ frames entire surah. Seven occurrences across vv.1–4 and 7–8; absent only from pivot unit vv.5–6 where the discourse becomes impersonal and universal. The absence of -كَ from the pivot is itself a structural marker: the promise is not personal but categorical.

anqada_onomatopoeic.note: Sole sound-mimetic word in surah. The cracking/creaking quality of أَنْقَضَ (انقضاض — to crack under weight) marks the burden's physical reality before its removal. Unique in this sequence as onomatopoeic in a gift/benefaction unit rather than a threat unit.

structure_summary.note: 8-verse inverted ring (1+2+1+2+1+1). Three-zone rhyme architecture ([-ak]×4 / [-an]×2 / [-ab]×2) perfectly co-extensive with three thematic units — no lag at any boundary. Only such case in dataset. Center hinge vv.5–6 is the only near-verbatim consecutive repetition in the dataset where the repeated verse constitutes the structural center. -كَ suffix frames entire surah (vv.1–4, 7–8) but is absent from pivot unit (vv.5–6), prosodically marking the universal impersonal register of the promise. Divine 'We' (نَا) confined to vv.1–4 and disappears at the pivot — the most architecturally precise agent-withdrawal in this sequence.

pivot.verse_ar: ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَاهُ أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ
pivot.notes: Theme inversion: best stature (v.4) → lowest of low; ثُمَّ temporal escalation; أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ intra-verse root doubling (س-ف-ل); antithetical superlative to أَحْسَنِ v.4; [-iːm] of v.4 ends, [-iːn] returns; nadir around which surah argument turns
pivot.center_logic_note: functional nadir v.5 (5/5); no secondaries. أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ as the surah's lowest point resolves all five signals independently.

superlative_pair_frame.v4: أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ — best of stature (apodosis open)
superlative_pair_frame.v8: أَحْكَمِ الْحَاكِمِينَ — most just of judges (surah close)
superlative_pair_frame.note: Only two superlative constructions in surah; both أَفْعَل form; structurally symmetric positions: apodosis open ↔ surah close. Frame: what man was made as ↔ who will judge what he became. Most semantically charged morphological frame signal in dataset. Compare Q.108 إِنَّا/إِنَّ envelope (vv.1/3) and Q.112 أَحَدٌ inclusio (vv.1/4).

uun_distal_echo.note: [-uːn] distal echo in a surah otherwise dominated by [-iːn]. Two positive content zones share the same terminal: sanctified oath objects ↔ reward for believers. The [-iːm] of v.4 stands as sole deviation — prosodically isolating the creation-in-best-stature verse as the apex before descent.

intra_verse_root_doubling_at_nadir.note: Only intra-verse root doubling in surah, placed at structural nadir. Compare Q.99 زُلْزِلَتِ/زِلْزَالَهَا (v.1 opening event) and Q.96 خَلَقَ/خَلَقَ (vv.1–2 consecutive). Q.95 places its doubling at thematic low point rather than opening — phonetic density signals semantic extremity.

four_oath_resolution.note: Four oath objects across three verses (v.1: fig+olive; v.2: Mt Sinai; v.3: secure city). Apodosis at v.4: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الإِنسَانَ. Oath-pivot pattern confirmed: resolution at v.4 is the primary structural boundary. 4-verse oath unit is the longest oath block in dataset (Q.100 has 5-verse chain but Q.95's vv.1–3 have four objects in three verses).

ahsan_ahkam_arc.note: أَحْسَنِ (v.4) describes man's created state; أَسْفَلَ (v.5) describes his fall; أَحْكَمِ (v.8) describes the judge who will adjudicate. The superlative form moves: creation superlative → descent superlative → judicial superlative. Three superlative positions marking the surah's three stages.

[-iːn]; أَحْكَمِ echoes أَحْسَنِ v.4; superlative pair sealed; surah closes unanswered

PIVOT — ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَاهُ; descent nadir

structure_summary.note: 8-verse inverted ring (3+1+1+1+2). Four-oath resolution at v.4 (pattern confirmed). Pivot v.5 = nadir; أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ intra-verse root doubling. أَحْسَنِ/أَحْكَمِ superlative pair frames apodosis-to-close. [-uːn] distal echo vv.1/6 links sanctified oath objects to believers' reward. [-iːm] sole deviation isolates apex verse (v.4) prosodically.

verses.ar: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ
إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ

pivot.verse_ar: كَلَّا إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَيَطْغَىٰ
pivot.notes: كَلَّا ×1; revelation unit ends; الإِنسَان ×3 arc completes (created v.2 / taught v.5 / transgresses v.6); رَبِّكَ disappears from surah; 9-verse [-a] alif maqsura zone opens (longest single-terminal zone in dataset); tonal transformation from crisp consonant-close to open vowel
pivot.center_logic_note: functional seam v.6 (5/5); no secondaries. كَلَّا at v.6 is the confirmed pivot marker; all signals concentrate at the single verse.

secondary_pivots.note: كَلَّا ×2; divine first-person plural 'We' re-enters for first time since v.1; لَنَسْفَعًا emphatic tanwin unique in surah; [-a] zone ends; [-iya] opens; النَّاصِيَة named
كَلَّا ×3 closes; triple imperative; وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ inverts اقْرَأْ v.1; second-person prophet re-addressed; [-ib] abrupt double consonant stop — surah's sharpest prosodic close

occurrences.function: confrontation unit ends → divine threat issued

three_kalla_pillars.note: Three كَلَّا distributed as architectural pillars across 19 verses — each marks a major structural turn, dividing the surah into four blocks (vv.1–5 / vv.6–14 / vv.15–18 / v.19). كَلَّا verses function as hinges rather than belonging to adjacent blocks. Compare: Q.102 كَلَّا ×3 consecutive (vv.3–5); Q.82 كَلَّا ×1 (v.9 center). Q.96 is the first surah in dataset where كَلَّا is distributed as a structural pillar system across a long text. كَلَّا-as-pivot confirmed at n=4 across Q.82/Q.104/Q.102/Q.96.

triple_consecutive_root_doubling.note: Three consecutive-verse root doublings within the revelation unit (vv.1–5) — no other surah in dataset produces three such doublings within a single unit. The creation pair (vv.1–2) is structurally replicated by the teaching pair (vv.4–5), with اقْرَأْ (v.3) as the hinge. Parallel to Q.99's consecutive root doubling (زُلْزِلَتِ/زِلْزَالَهَا) but here three pairs in one unit.

command_inversion.v19: وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ — prostrate and draw near (physical, inward)
command_inversion.note: Opening command ↔ closing command: both direct second-person imperatives to same addressee. Recitation → prostration. The surah's entire arc encoded in this pair. Only surah in dataset with an opening/closing imperative pair with this structural inversion relationship.

nine_verse_open_vowel_zone.note: Longest single-terminal rhyme zone in dataset — 9 consecutive verses with open terminal vowel. Maps precisely onto the transgression + confrontation block. Extended tonal suspension frames the surah's central dramatic confrontation.

occurrences.context: عَلَّمَ الإِنسَانَ مَا لَمْ يَعْلَمْ — taught

insan_arc.note: الإِنسَان traces a three-stage arc: created → taught → transgresses. Confined to vv.2–6. Each occurrence marks a structural unit boundary. Absent from vv.7–19. The arc is completed exactly at the structural pivot (v.6), then the text shifts to confrontation without naming mankind again.

ra_ay_vision_axis.note: Root ر-أ-ي runs through the confrontation block: man's self-satisfied seeing (v.7) → triple challenge to see (vv.9/11/13) → Allah sees (v.14). Vision axis: human arrogance in sight → divine omniscient sight. Root disappears after v.14.

signal_table.prosody_note: rhyme break [-am]; 2nd command; اقْرَأْ exact repeat
ZONE BREAK [-a] alif maqsura opens; 9-verse zone; 5-sig pivot

signal_table.verse_ar: كَلَّا لَئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ لَنَسْفَعًا بِالنَّاصِيَةِ
signal_table.prosody_note: ZONE BREAK [-iya]; لَنَسْفَعًا emphatic tanwin; divine 'We' re-enters; 5-sig pivot2
[-ib] abrupt double stop; triple imperative; command inversion; 5-sig pivot3

block_map.label: اقْرَأْ — revelation; creation; teaching
كَلَّا×1 — transgression; return
أَرَأَيْتَ ×3 — confrontation unit
كَلَّا×2 — divine threat; forelock; d-aw pair
كَلَّا×3 — وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ; command inverse

structure_summary.note: 19-verse surah with three كَلَّا architectural pillars (vv.6/15/19) dividing four blocks. Triple consecutive-verse root doubling in revelation unit. الإِنسَان three-stage arc (created/taught/transgresses) completes at pivot v.6. 9-verse alif-maqsura zone (longest in dataset) frames confrontation block. Opening/closing command inversion (اقْرَأْ ↔ وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ). Triple أَرَأَيْتَ confrontation unit. ر-أ-ي vision axis (human sight → divine sight).

verses.ar: كَلَّا لَئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ لَنَسْفَعًا بِالنَّاصِيَةِ

pivot.verse_ar: لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ
pivot.notes: Geometric center; لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر ×3 exhausted here — declarative subject (prev: prepositional object v.1, interrogative subject v.2); sole comparative in surah; impersonal — no speaker/addressee; [-adr]→[-ahr] rhyme break; /r/ chain opens; شَهْر sole numerical quantity
pivot.center_logic_note: geometric center v.3 (5/5); no secondaries. 5-verse surah — center verse is the only possible pivot; all signals confirm it.

secondary_convergence.note: ن-ز-ل root closes at v.4; سَلَامٌ maximum phonetic stillness vs. تَنَزَّلُ maximum density; هِيَ pronoun; temporal boundary مَطْلَعِ الْفَجْرِ; inner B closes + frame A closes

occurrences.function: interrogative subject — question (what is the Night?)
declarative subject — definition (the Night is better than...)

triple_leitwort_exhaustion.note: Three consecutive verses; three distinct grammatical functions: location → question → definition. Epistemological sequence exhausts naming possibilities before surah moves to content without naming the Night again (vv.4–5). Densest triple-exhaustion pattern in short surah corpus. Compare: الْقَارِعَة ×3 in Q.101 (same triple consecutive structure). Q.97 and Q.101 are the only two surahs in dataset with triple consecutive leitwort opening.

r_terminal_chain_full_surah.note: Terminal /r/ across all 5 verses with zero deviation — most consistent full-surah terminal consonant chain in dataset relative to verse count. Medial consonant varies across all 5 positions (d/d/h/m/j). Structurally analogous to Q.105 [-iːl]×4+[-uːl] (consonant maintained, vowel shifts) and Q.103 /r/ ×3, but Q.97 maintains /r/ across all verses with maximum medial variation. vv.1–2 share exact [-adr] before chain opens.

inna_to_rabbihim_divine_name_shift.note: Divine self-reference opens surah (إِنَّا, v.1 — only occurrence) then shifts to third-person relational title at v.4 (رَبِّهِم). By v.4 the angels are acting under His permission, not narrated by Him. The surah's divine subject transitions from speaker to referent across the pivot. Parallel to Q.100 singular→plural shift, but here a first-person→third-person mode shift.

nzl_root_frame.v1: أَنْزَلْنَاهُ — We sent it down (Quran's descent)
nzl_root_frame.note: Root ن-ز-ل frames the surah: divine act of sending down (v.1) ↔ continuous angelic descending (v.4). Same root, two referents, two grammatical voices (past transitive ↔ present intransitive). The Night is the common container. Bridge across the pivot.

salam_phonetic_resolution.note: سَلَامٌ as opening word of v.5 — maximally open phonetics (long vowel, unmarked nasalized stop) immediately following تَنَزَّلُ v.4 (geminated زّ, heaviest consonant cluster in surah). Sharpest aural contrast across consecutive verses in dataset. The peace-declaration is prosodically enacted by the sound.

signal_table.verse_ar: لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ
signal_table.prosody_note: [-adr]→[-ahr] break; /r/ chain opens; شَهْر sole quantity; 5-sig pivot

signal_table.verse_ar: تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمْرٍ

signal_table.prosody_note: [-ajr]; سَلَامٌ max phonetic stillness vs تَنَزَّلُ; most compressed; /r/ chain closes

block_map.label: إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ — revelation; Night named
وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ — Night questioned
PIVOT — لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر ×3; better than 1000 months
تَنَزَّلُ — angels descend; content disclosed
سَلَامٌ — peace; until dawn

structure_summary.note: 5-verse inverted ring (1+1+1+1+1). لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر ×3 exhausted at pivot — three grammatical functions: location/question/definition. /r/ terminal across all 5 verses with maximum medial variation. إِنَّا (v.1) → رَبِّهِم (v.4) divine name shift. ن-ز-ل frames vv.1↔4. سَلَامٌ closes with sharpest aural contrast in dataset (v.4 gemination → v.5 open stillness).

verses.ar: لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ
تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمْرٍ

pivot.verse_ar: وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ... وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ
pivot.notes: Geometric center (v.5 of 8); sole verse stating what was actually commanded; passive وَمَا أُمِرُوا; دِين ×2 intra-verse; الْقَيِّمَة closes its pair (v.3→v.5); ق-و-م bridges الْكُتُبُ الْقَيِّمَة (v.3) ↔ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَة (v.5); pivot between البَيِّنَة unit and verdict unit.
pivot.center_logic_note: geometric center v.5 (5/5); no secondaries. إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ×2 antithetical pair at center; all signals confirm single concentration.

paired_terminal_architecture.note: Unique in dataset: three distinct terminal words each appearing exactly twice in paired positions across the surah. Terminal rhyme and thematic structure are co-extensive — the lexical pairs ARE the structural boundaries. Each pair corresponds to a distinct thematic unit. The [-iyya] family is a lexical architecture, not just a sound pattern.

qayyima_bridge.note: Root ق-و-م closes البَيِّنَة definition (v.3) and command statement (v.5) — connecting what was revealed to what was required across the structural center. The bridge is both lexical (same adjective) and semantic (scripture → religion). First case in dataset where the same root-form closes two verses flanking the pivot from both sides.

khalidina_antithetical_deployment.v6: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا — eternal abiding in Hellfire
khalidina_antithetical_deployment.v8: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا — eternal abiding in gardens
khalidina_antithetical_deployment.note: Identical phrase deployed in antithetical eschatological contexts across the surah close. Only surah in dataset where the same exact phrase describes both punishment and reward within one text. Parallel to Q.99 خَيْرًا↔شَرًّا substitution couplet — but here the frame phrase is identical, not the content word.

near_verbatim_distal_frame.v1: الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ
near_verbatim_distal_frame.v6: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ
near_verbatim_distal_frame.note: Longest near-verbatim distal phrase in dataset — a full multi-word clause repeated with only إِنَّ prefixed. Opens the surah as its grammatical subject (v.1) and opens the verdict unit as its condemned object (v.6). Strongest cross-unit lexical frame detected in dataset.

iyya_v_ahu_terminal_break.note: [-iyya] family dominant vv.1–7 (with v.2 deviation مُطَهَّرَة). V.8 breaks to [-ahu] — رَبَّهُ with geminated بّ. Surah closes outside its own rhyme family. The terminal word is the most intimate divine relational form in the surah (his Lord). Parallel to Q.107's [-iːn]/[-uːn] bipartite but here a single-verse terminal exception rather than a full zone shift.

وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ... وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ
signal_table.prosody_note: [-iyya]; الْقَيِّمَة echoes v.3; 2nd longest; دِين doubled; 5-sig pivot

block_map.label: البَيِّنَة defined — scripture chain

structure_summary.note: Three paired terminal words (الْبَيِّنَة, الْقَيِّمَة, الْبَرِيَّة) each appearing ×2 — terminal rhyme IS structural boundary. الْقَيِّمَة bridges scripture (v.3) to religion (v.5) across pivot. خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا identical phrase for both punishment (v.6) and reward (v.8). Near-verbatim distal frame الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (v.1↔v.6) — longest cross-unit lexical frame in dataset. Single terminal break [-ahu] at v.8 closes outside [-iyya] family.

verses.ar: لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنفَكِّينَ حَتَّىٰ تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ
رَسُولٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ يَتْلُو صُحُفًا مُّطَهَّرَةً
وَمَا تَفَرَّقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ إِلَّا مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ
وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ حُنَفَاءَ وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أُولَٰئِكَ هُمْ شَرُّ الْبَرِيَّةِ
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ
جَزَاؤُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا رَّضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِيَ رَبَّهُ

pivot.verse_ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا
pivot.notes: Compound center vv.4–5: يَوْمَئِذٍ first appearance (v.4); رَبَّكَ أَوْحَى — sole divine agency + sole 2nd-person address (v.5). Geometric center of 8-verse surah. Pivot between cosmic event unit (vv.1–3) and human accountability unit (vv.6–8). [-aːraha] near-variant marks center.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.4–5, 5/5); no secondaries. Suffix-declension device generates rhyme zone co-extensive with pivot.

compound_center.note: Geometric center is a 2-verse unit (3+2+3 distribution). v.4: يَوْمَئِذٍ anchor + earth discloses. v.5: رَبَّكَ أَوْحَى — only divine agent + only 2nd-person suffix in surah. Combined 5-signal center unit.

secondary_convergence_zones.note: Theme shift divine explanation→human dispersal; يَوْمَئِذٍ closes; singular الإِنسَان→plural النَّاس; passive لِيُرَوا; rhyme break [-ahum]; longest verse; suffix shifts fem→masc plural.
مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّة ×2; خَيْرًا/شَرًّا antithetical; يَرَهُ verbatim ×2; collective→individual فَمَن; rhyme break [-ahu]; frame A closes.

suffix_declension_as_structure.note: Unique in dataset: suffix declension tracks thematic subject-scale. Earth acts (-هَا) → people disperse (-هُم) → individual judged (-هُ). The rhyme is not broken — it is grammatically transformed. Cosmic → collective → individual scale encoded in morphology. The phoneme family /a+h/ persists throughout; only person and number shift.

zilzal_root_reduplication.note: Quadrilateral root ز-ل-ز-ل appears as both verb and verbal noun within a single verse. Only quadrilateral root reduplication in analyzed corpus. Parallel to Q.103 تَوَاصَوا ×2 intra-verse (same root twice) but here achieved through verb+verbal-noun within one root rather than verb repetition.

near_verbatim_verdict_couplet.v7: مَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ
near_verbatim_verdict_couplet.v8: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ
near_verbatim_verdict_couplet.note: Tightest antithetical pair in dataset — only a single word substituted across an otherwise verbatim couplet. More constrained than Q.102 vv.3–4 (ثُمَّ added) or Q.82 vv.17–18 (exact consecutive). يَرَهُ exact terminal ×2. Fourth verbatim/near-verbatim duplication type in dataset: antithetical-substitution couplet.

aha_phonetic_family.note: All 8 terminals share /a+h/ phoneme cluster — most sustained phonetic unity in any surah analyzed. The surah never truly breaks its sound family: it only transforms the person of the suffix. Distinct from uniform rhyme (Q.114 [-aːs] ×6) which is unchanging — here the rhyme evolves through grammatical transformation rather than remaining static.

rabbaka_isolated_address.note: Sole second-person address in surah. The كَ suffix is phonetically isolated within the [-aha] zone — the entire surah addresses no one except at this single verse, where divine agency is named. Compare Q.105 'أَلَم تَرَ' (2nd-person in double opening) and Q.102 'سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ' (2nd-person plural). Q.99 is the only surah where 2nd-person address is confined to the geometric center.

رَبَّكَ — sole 2nd-person in surah; كَ isolated

النَّاس — dispersal; أَعْمَال
مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّة ×2 — verdict couplet

structure_summary.note: 8-verse inverted ring (3+2+3 distribution). Suffix declension tracks subject-scale: -هَا (earth) → -هُم (people) → -هُ (individual). Phoneme family /a+h/ unified across all 8 terminals. رَبَّكَ sole 2nd-person address at geometric center only. Near-verbatim antithetical verdict couplet (vv.7–8) with single-word substitution — tightest pair in dataset. زِلْزَال root reduplication unique.

verses.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِّيُرَوْا أَعْمَالَهُمْ
فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ
pivot.notes: Oath unit ends; إِنَّ emphatic opens 4-verse chain; الإِنسَان named as new subject; لِرَبِّهِ divine relational frame opens (closed at v.11); rhyme break [-uːd]; tanwin-nunation ends; long vowel zone opens. Highest multi-signal convergence point.
pivot.center_logic_note: functional seam v.6 (5/5); no secondaries. Oath chain exhausted before v.6; all five signals resolve at the participial-to-consequence seam.

secondary_convergence.note: Triple إِنَّ indictment → interrogative أَفَلَا يَعْلَمُ; second suspension resumes (إِذَا ×2); مَا فِي opens; الْقُبُور; rhyme break [-uːr]; longest verse. Second 5-signal pivot.

double_suspension_resolution.shared_feature: Both resolutions introduced by إِنَّ emphatic; both name رَبّ relational axis
double_suspension_resolution.note: Double syntactic suspension — longest oath chain in dataset (5 verses), resolved at v.6; then second إِذَا suspension (2 verses), resolved at v.11. Both resolutions: إِنَّ + رَبّ. Most complex suspension architecture in dataset. Q.103 had 1-verse oath suspension; Q.106 had 2-verse لِ suspension; Q.82 had 4-verse إِذَا suspension. Q.100 is the first surah with TWO suspension units in the same text.

zones.note: ضَبْحًا/قَدْحًا/صُبْحًا — verbal nouns
نَقْعًا/جَمْعًا — verbal nouns; oath action sequence
لَكَنُودٌ/لَشَهِيدٌ/لَشَدِيدٌ — emphatic lam predicates
الْقُبُورِ/الصُّدُورِ/لَخَبِيرٌ — r-echo closes

quadruple_rhyme_zones.note: Four distinct rhyme zones — most segmented prosodic architecture in dataset. Zones 1–2 (tanwin-nunation) unify oath unit; Zones 3–4 (long vowel + stop/liquid) unify verdict/consequence units. Zone-break at v.6 coincides precisely with all other structural boundaries. Previously, Q.107 had bipartite architecture (2 zones); Q.102 had 4 zones across 8 verses. Q.100 produces 4 zones across 11 verses with cleaner zone-boundary alignment.

fa_consecutive_chain.note: Four-verse فَ consecutive chain confined to oath unit — longest sustained فَ chain in dataset. Both فَ-initial participles (vv.2–3) and فَ-initial past verbs (vv.4–5). Oath's sequential action visible in the morphological progression: description → event.

inna_lam_predicate_chain.note: Four إِنَّ + emphatic لَ predicate nominals across verdict and consequence units. All four share lam-emphasis structure; vv.6–8 form continuous triple indictment; v.11 closes as divine counter-assertion. إِنَّ+لَ chain frames the entire non-oath section.

quburr_sudur_sealed_pair.note: Outer/inner container pair: graves (outer) ↔ breasts (inner). Exact terminal rhyme ×2. مَا فِي repeated across both — 'what is in the graves' ↔ 'what is in the breasts'. Only semantic-antithesis pair in dataset where the antithesis is spatial (external↔internal) rather than evaluative (reward↔punishment).

singular_to_plural_shift.v11: إِنَّ رَبَّهُم (their Lord — plural subject implied)
singular_to_plural_shift.note: Mankind indicted as singular (v.6: الإِنسَان) → divine verdict delivered to plural (v.11: رَبَّهُم). Shift from individual moral portrait to collective eschatological accounting. Same device as Q.82 v.9 (singular → plural) and Q.104 v.8 (هِم plural enters), but here marks the structural close rather than a mid-surah shift.

إِذَا suspension — graves/breasts
إِنَّ رَبَّهُم — divine apodosis closes

structure_summary.note: Double syntactic suspension: oath (vv.1–5, resolved v.6) and interrogative-conditional (vv.9–10, resolved v.11). Both resolutions: إِنَّ + رَبّ. Four distinct rhyme zones with zone-boundary at v.6 coinciding with all other structural signals. فَ chain (×4) confined to oath unit. إِنَّ+لَ predicate chain (×4) unifies verdict/consequence units. Singular الإِنسَان (v.6) → plural رَبَّهُم (v.11).

verses.ar: أَفَلَا يَعْلَمُ إِذَا بُعْثِرَ مَا فِي الْقُبُورِ

pivot.verse_ar: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا الْقَارِعَةُ
pivot.notes: Frame A closes; الْقَارِعَة ×3 exhausted; وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا inclusio opens (links to v.10); second-person challenge; [-iʕa] ×3 closes. 4 signals — boundary vv.3–4 registers 5 combined.
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.3–4, 4/5); no secondaries. Earliest-lean compound in dataset (norm −0.318); pivot precedes dominant content by verse count.

compound_boundary.note: Strongest convergence zone: الْقَارِعَة unit closes at v.3 (4 signals) + cosmic scene opens at v.4 (4 signals). Combined 5-signal boundary across 2-verse seam. Parallel to Q.104 compound pivot (word-span) but here a unit-boundary seam rather than a single pivot word.

secondary_convergence_zones.note: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا inclusio closes (v.10); هَاوِيَة defined as نَارٌ حَامِيَة (v.11); participle triplet closes; 2nd-person re-engaged then bare declarative; [-iːa] closes; most compressed terminal.

boundaries.label: Triple-naming unit
boundaries.notes: الْقَارِعَة ×3; [-iʕa] ×3; nominal→interrogative→escalated-challenge; وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ opens at boundary

boundaries.label: Cosmic scene
boundaries.notes: يَكُونُ/تَكُونُ pair; simile ×2; [-uːθ/-uːʃ] near-rhyme; third-person cosmic narration

boundaries.label: Binary verdict
boundaries.notes: فَأَمَّا/وَأَمَّا pair; مَوَازِينُهُ exact ×2; [-uːhu/-iːa] alternation; رَاضِيَة/هَاوِيَة antithetical

boundaries.label: Terminal interrogative-definition
boundaries.notes: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا closes inclusio; نَارٌ حَامِيَة defines هَاوِيَة; [-iːa] triplet closes; bare 2-word nominal

triple_qariah_opening.note: Exact terminal repetition across three consecutive opening verses — heaviest consecutive opening repetition in dataset. الْقَارِعَة ×3 then permanently absent. Named → questioned → challenged. Compare: Q.104 الْحُطَمَة ×2 consecutive (compound pivot); Q.109 ×2 non-adjacent; Q.82 ×2 consecutive exact. Q.101 is the only triple consecutive opening repetition.

double_ma_adraka_frame.note: Exact phrase وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا used as structural bracket — first instance at opening close (v.3) suspends definition for 7 verses; second at body close (v.10) resolves in 1 verse. Only surah in dataset where the phrase appears at both structural boundaries. First v.3 suspension-span = 7 verses (longest deferred answer). Compare: Q.82 vv.17–18 (verbatim phrase doubled consecutively); Q.104 v.5 (once, single use). Q.101 uses it architecturally as a double frame.

feminine_participle_triplet_distributed.note: Second feminine participial triplet in dataset (first: Q.104 الْمُوقَدَة/مُؤْصَدَة/مُمَدَّدَة). Q.104 concentrates triplet at surah close (vv.6/8/9); Q.101 distributes across consequence structure (vv.7/9/11). Both share [-iːa]-class terminal. Positive ↔ negative ↔ definition — distributed rather than concentrated triplet is new variant.

mawazin_antithetical_repeat.note: Exact nominal repeat across non-consecutive verses — only cross-verse verbatim nominal in surah body. Heavy ↔ light: same word, opposite result. Parallel to Q.109 vv.3/5 non-adjacent repetition, but here antithetical rather than semantic mirror.

nested_internal_mirror.note: Three-level nested ring: outer (vv.1–3 ↔ vv.10–11), inner (vv.4–5 ↔ vv.6–9), innermost (vv.6–7 ↔ vv.8–9). Second surah with explicitly nested ring structure in dataset (first: Q.82). No single unmirrored geometric center — structural seam falls between units rather than at a verse.

block_map.label: الْقَارِعَة ×3 — naming + challenge
Cosmic scene — moths + wool
فَأَمَّا — heavy scales → reward
وَأَمَّا — light scales → هَاوِيَة
وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ ↔ نَارٌ حَامِيَة

structure_summary.note: Three-level nested ring: outer (vv.1–3 ↔ vv.10–11 via وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا), inner (vv.4–5 ↔ vv.6–9), innermost (vv.6–7 ↔ vv.8–9). No single geometric center — structural seam at vv.3–4. Triple الْقَارِعَة opening ×3 unique in dataset. وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا used as double structural bracket (first suspension 7 verses; second resolves in 1). Distributed feminine participle triplet (vv.7/9/11) — second such triplet, first distributed variant.

pivot.verse_ar: كَلَّا لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ
pivot.notes: Third كَلَّا (closes كَلَّا block); sole conditional verse; apodosis withheld — unique syntactic incompletion in dataset; ع-ل-م doubled intra-verse; الْيَقِين introduced here, resolved at v.7; rhyme break [-iːn]; hinge between warning block and vision block
pivot.center_logic_note: functional center v.5 (5/5); no secondaries. كَلَّا ×2 double pillar with withheld apodosis structure; single concentrated pivot.

secondary_convergence_zones.note: Theme shift distraction→rebuke; كَلَّا ×2; تَعْلَمُونَ verbatim consecutive; ثُمَّ escalation; rhyme break [-uːn]; exact phonetic duplication vv.3–4
Vision block + accountability close; لَتَرَوُنَّ ×2; الْيَقِين closes; النَّعِيم terminal antithesis; [-iːm/-iːn] alternation; emphatic lām+نَّ triplet

boundaries.notes: التَّكَاثُر named; graveyard close; [-aːθur/-aːbir] zone; second-person address opens

boundaries.label: كَلَّا warning block
boundaries.notes: كَلَّا ×3; ع-ل-م ×3; تَعْلَمُونَ verbatim vv.3–4; conditional suspension v.5; الْيَقِين introduced

boundaries.label: Vision + accountability close
boundaries.notes: لَتَرَوُنَّ ×2; ثُمَّ ×2; الْيَقِين closes; الْجَحِيمَ/النَّعِيمِ antithetical pair; emphatic lām+نَّ ×3

triple_kalla_graduated.note: Most كَلَّا occurrences in any analyzed surah (previous max: 1 each in Q.82 and Q.104). Three-كَلَّا graduated sequence: warning → escalated warning → pivot-into-conditional. Third كَلَّا does not repeat — it pivots. كَلَّا-as-pivot-marker pattern now at n=3 across Q.82/Q.104/Q.102.

yaqin_lexical_bridge.note: الْيَقِين introduced at pivot (v.5) as the condition for knowledge withheld, then resolved at v.7 as direct sight. Mode-shift ع-ل-م → ر-أ-ي across the pivot: the conditional 'if you knew' is answered not with knowledge but with seeing. First lexical bridge of this type in dataset — term introduced at pivot, resolved in mirror-frame.

withheld_apodosis.note: Sole syntactically incomplete verse in dataset — conditional clause with no apodosis. The pivot verse is the only verse in the analyzed corpus that deliberately withholds its grammatical completion. Different from the syntactic suspension device (Q.106/Q.82/Q.103) which suspends and then resolves — here the resolution is permanently deferred.

verbatim_consecutive_vv3_4.note: Exact verbatim phrase repeated across consecutive verses with ثُمَّ escalation prefix only addition. Third instance of verbatim verse-related duplication in dataset (Q.82 vv.17–18 exact full-verse; Q.109 vv.3/5 non-adjacent; Q.102 vv.3–4 consecutive with ثُمَّ addition). Q.102 is the only instance where the repetition is modified — ثُمَّ is added, making it a graduated rather than exact duplicate.

jahim_naim_antithetical_pair.note: Hellfire ↔ Pleasure: antithetical pair sharing terminal rhyme [-iːm] at structural close. Both introduced by emphatic lām+نَّ oath constructions. الْجَحِيمَ/النَّعِيمِ are phonetically near-mirror words (shared /iːm/ + contrasting initial consonants). Parallel to مَال v.2/3 (behavioral pair) and تَوَاصَوا ×2 Q.103 (mutual pair) but first antithetical-rhyme-pair close in dataset.

ilm_ray_mode_shift.note: Pivot shifts from knowledge register (ع-ل-م ×3 in vv.3–5) to sight register (ر-أ-ي ×2 in vv.6–7). The conditional 'if you knew' is never answered with knowledge — it is answered with sight of Hellfire. Root-substitution at structural pivot — parallel to Q.111's لَهَب disappearance and Q.110's اللَّهِ→رَبِّكَ shift, but here a full root-register change rather than single-term substitution.

structure_summary.note: Triple كَلَّا block (vv.3–5) graduated: warning → escalated warning → conditional pivot. Withheld apodosis at pivot — only syntactically incomplete verse in dataset. الْيَقِين lexical bridge v.5→v.7. Root-register shift ع-ل-م → ر-أ-ي across pivot. الْجَحِيمَ/النَّعِيمِ antithetical [-iːm] close. كَلَّا-as-pivot-marker now n=3.

pivot.verse_ar: إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ
pivot.notes: Geometric and functional center; oath suspension resolves; إِنَّ + lām emphatic; الإِنسَان sole occurrence; load-bearing assertion flanked by qualifying frames vv.1 and 3; [-usr] near-rhyme between compressed units
pivot.center_logic_note: geometric center v.2 (5/5); no secondaries. 3-verse surah — v.2 is the only possible center; all signals confirm.

secondary_convergence.note: Theme shift verdict→exception; إِلَّا concatenation; تَوَاصَوا ×2 intra-verse; fourfold وَ chain; rhythm break — longest verse by far; بِالصَّبْرِ partial phonetic echo of وَالْعَصْرِ v.1

oath_suspension_device.note: Third syntactic suspension in dataset (Q.106: لِإِيلافِ; Q.82: إِذَا ×4). Oath frame suspends predicate; resolved by إِنَّ emphatic at v.2. Shortest suspension span: single verse.

asymmetric_weight_distribution.note: Most extreme syllabic asymmetry in dataset. Prosodic weight increases monotonically: 2→5→17. Unlike every other analyzed surah where pivot carries maximum compression, here maximum weight falls on the terminal verse. The four-part exception structurally inverts the single universal verdict: one noun condemned universally ↔ four conditions required for exemption.

r_consonant_thread.note: No full terminal rhyme — vowel nucleus varies throughout. Terminal /r/ is the sole sonic thread connecting units of radically unequal length. New rhyme type in dataset: consonantal terminal thread with maximum vowel variation.

intra_verse_verbal_repetition.note: Exact verbal repetition within a single verse — only intra-verse duplication in dataset. Parallel to Q.82 vv.17–18 (consecutive inter-verse) and Q.109 vv.3/5 (non-adjacent inter-verse), but unique as intra-verse.

envelope_pattern_no_lexical_inclusio.note: Q.103 produces an envelope structure (v.1 frame / v.2 center / v.3 frame) without a lexical inclusio — the two framing verses share grammatical function (qualifying v.2) but no shared root or terminal word. Previous envelope-like patterns in dataset always carried a lexical anchor. Functional-only envelope is new in dataset.

signal_table.discourse_detail: oath register; waw al-qasam; predicate suspended; no addressee
signal_table.prosody_note: [-asr] anchor; 2 words — lightest unit in dataset

signal_table.discourse_detail: oath resolves; إِنَّ+lām emphatic; universal 3rd-person verdict

إِلَّا — exception; 4-part qualification

structure_summary.note: Three-verse envelope with monotonic weight increase (2→5→17 words). Terminal /r/ threads all three verses without full rhyme. Functional-only envelope — no lexical inclusio. Oath suspension (v.1) resolved at pivot (v.2) then universality suspended again by إِلَّا exception (v.3). Most asymmetric syllabic distribution in dataset.

verses.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ

pivot.verse_ar: كَلَّا لَيُنبَذَنَّ فِي الْحُطَمَةِ
pivot.notes: Compound pivot vv.4–5: الْحُطَمَة named v.4 ↔ interrogated v.5. كَلَّا sole adversative; emphatic future passive; (-adahu) ends; [-ama] opens; phonetic stop
pivot.center_logic_note: compound_seam primary (vv.4–5, 5/5); no secondaries. كَلَّا at v.4 is the heuristic marker; compound seam bridges moral indictment to Hell description.

compound_pivot.note: Only compound pivot in dataset. الْحُطَمَة spans verse boundary: named at v.4-end (كَلَّا + punishment) then immediately interrogated at v.5 (وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ). The pivot word IS the hinge.

compound_pivot_humazah.note: Only word in dataset simultaneously at verse-end (v.4) and verse-start frame (v.5 interrogative). Structural hinge is the lexical item, not the verse position.

participial_triplet.note: Three feminine active participles describing the Fire with escalating gemination (none→ص→دّ). Fueled→sealed→columned. Unique closing prosodic architecture in dataset.

humazah_lumazah_internal_rhyme.note: Both epithets share [-aza] within single verse — only instance of in-verse end-rhyme in dataset.

adahu_couplet.note: Identical terminal (-adahu) across consecutive verses; both geminated دّ. Acoustic parallelism marks behavioral-profile couplet.

kalla_second_instance.dataset_note: Second كَلَّا in dataset (first: Q.82 v.9). Both mark structural pivots. Pattern: كَلَّا = pivot marker.

signal_table.prosody_note: triplet 3/3; مُمَدَّدَة دّ heaviest; confinement closes

structure_summary.note: Compound pivot vv.4–5 — only distributed hinge in dataset. Participial triplet vv.6/8/9 with escalating gemination — unique closing architecture. كَلَّا second instance in dataset, both marking structural pivots. Internal rhyme v.1 (هُمَزَة/لُمَزَة) — only in-verse rhyme in dataset.

pivot.verse_ar: وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا أَبَابِيلَ
pivot.notes: Geometric center; first affirmative past-tense verb in surah (وَأَرْسَلَ); interrogative mode drops, pure 3rd-person narration opens; sole instrument-introduction verse; أَبَابِيل reduplicative morphology unique in surah; [-iːl] sustained but aural texture shifts
pivot.center_logic_note: geometric center v.3 (5/5) + 5/5 secondary at v.5 (terminal simile close); short surah distributes convergence across pivot and terminal simultaneously.

secondary_convergence.note: Theme shift narration→simile verdict; فَجَعَلَهُم echoes فَعَلَ v.1; -هُم chain closes; frame A closes; rhyme break [-uːl]; /l/ preserved — deliberate vowel resolution; most compressed verse. Second concurrent 5-signal candidate.

boundaries.label: Double interrogative unit
boundaries.notes: أَلَم ×2 — double interrogative; -هُم chain opens; frame A + outer B open; [-iːl] anchor ×2. أَلَم absent vv.3–5.

terminal_consonant_preservation_rhyme.cross_dataset_note: Al-Fil is unique in the dataset in preserving the terminal consonant while shifting the vowel nucleus at the final verse — producing phonetic resolution rather than break. Compare: Q.113 disperses terminals; Q.111 breaks entirely; Q.107 switches zones; Q.105 closes the same consonant on a different vowel. New rhyme strategy: terminal-consonant-echo.

alam_double_interrogative.note: Double أَلَم interrogative confined to opening unit — absent vv.3–5. Parallel to Q.107 double أَرَأَيْتَ pattern (single-verse challenge). Q.105 extends the challenge across two verses before narrative pivot.

hum_suffix_object_chain.note: Companions tracked as grammatical object across vv.2–5: كَيْدَهُم (their plan) / عَلَيْهِم (upon them) / تَرْمِيهِم (striking them) / فَجَعَلَهُم (making them). Continuous object chain — Quraysh's agency collapses from active planner to passive object over 4 verses.

fil_feel_faala_frame.note: Divine action verb opens (how your Lord dealt) and closes (He made them) the surah. Two different but cognate roots of divine agency bracket the narrative.

ababiil_reduplication.note: Reduplicative morphology — unique sound texture in surah; no parallel elsewhere. Marks the pivot verse with an auditory signal independent of rhyme class.

simile_close.note: Most compressed close of any 5-verse surah in dataset. كَ comparative particle introduces the sole simile in the surah — figurative rather than declarative close. New closing strategy: comparison-as-verdict.

signal_table.verse_ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصْحَابِ الْفِيلِ

narration → simile verdict; كَ comparative closes

structure_summary.note: [-iːl] ×4 followed by [-uːl] echo — terminal consonant preserved, vowel resolved. Most sustained pre-terminal rhyme block in any 5-verse surah in dataset. Simile close (كَعَصْفٍ مَأْكُولٍ) is new closing strategy in dataset. -هُم suffix tracks Quraysh agency collapsing over 4 verses.

pivot.verse_ar: فَلْيَعْبُدُوا رَبَّ هَٰذَا الْبَيْتِ
pivot.notes: Analysis doc frames highest convergence zone as vv.2–3 boundary (5 signals). Confirmed pivot at v.3 — grammatical resolution point of لِ suspension: فَ consequential resolves لِإِيلافِ; jussive فَلْيَعْبُدُوا first finite command; إِيلاف absent for first time; رَبَّ هَٰذَا الْبَيْتِ unique title introduced. v.4 is concurrent 5-signal candidate (rhyme break; longest verse; مِن antithesis; الَّذِي clause).
pivot.center_logic_note: grammatical resolution v.3 (5/5) + 5/5 secondary at v.4 (imperative-resolution close); v.3 and v.4 are structurally co-equal in a 4-verse surah.

secondary_convergence.note: Theme shift command→causal elaboration; الَّذِي relative clause; Quraysh return to object/beneficiary; هُم suffix closes; مِن antithetical pair جُوع/خَوْف; rhyme break [-awf]; bipartite parallel structure; longest verse. First surah in dataset with two concurrent 5-signal candidates.

boundaries.label: Protasis unit

suspended_lam.note: The opening لِإِيلافِ is grammatically dependent — its purpose clause is not resolved until فَلْيَعْبُدُوا (v.3). The entire surah is syntactically a single suspended-then-resolved construction across 4 verses. The vv.2–3 seam is therefore the grammatical resolution point of the surah's sole main clause. No equivalent syntactic suspension detected in any other surah in the dataset.

iilaf_inclusio.note: Sole instance of verbatim root repetition across consecutive verses in this surah; absent vv.3–4. Opens and defines the protasis unit.

juun_khawf_antithesis.note: Paired antithetical nouns within single verse: hunger ↔ fear; material ↔ psychological. Both introduced by مِن — parallel construction. Only verse with repeated preposition as structural device.

dual_five_signal_candidates.note: First surah in dataset with two concurrent 5-signal candidates. v.3 is the grammatical pivot (لِ resolution); v.4 is the semantic terminal (divine characterization). The 4-verse surah has no geometric center — the structural seam falls between vv.2–3, producing equal-weight halves rather than a single unmirrored center.

pairs.shared: divine benefaction framing — gift named opens ↔ gifts enumerated closes
human activity/response — caravan practice ↔ worship commanded

pure_2x2_mirror.note: Unlike every other surah in dataset, Q.106 has no isolated center verse. Pure 2+2 inverted mirror with the structural seam between vv.2–3. Both halves carry equal unit weight.

hum_suffix_leitwort.note: هُم suffix marks Quraysh as beneficiary in both even verses (2,4); absent in both odd verses (1,3). Creates alternating beneficiary/non-beneficiary pattern across the 4-verse surah.

ay_diphthong_cluster.note: Vv.1–3 share [-ay-] diphthong nucleus with varying terminal consonants — a vowel-nucleus rhyme cluster rather than exact terminal rhyme. V.4 breaks the diphthong entirely to [-aw-]. Distinct from any rhyme type previously recorded in dataset.

bipartite_prosodic_architecture.cross_dataset_note: Clearest bipartite prosodic division in dataset. Unlike Q.113 (dispersing rhyme) or Q.109 (fragmented terminals), Q.107 produces two sustained, coherent zones divided precisely at the structural pivot.

wayl_isolation.note: Sole exclamatory verdict form in surah; فَ consequence particle opens new block; phonetically isolated — no equivalent elsewhere. Marks the structural seam prosodically.

salat_concatenation.note: Same root bridges the pivot: woe pronounced on pray-ers (v.4) ↔ their prayer characterized as heedless (v.5). Concatenation across the structural seam.

gemination_pair_vv23.note: Geminated عّ / ضّ — near-rhyme pair within denier-unit; dense consonant cluster signals behavioral intensity before pivot.

yaf_imperfect_chain.note: يَ- prefix imperfect verbs across both units unify behavioral descriptions; absent only at vv.4–5 (verdict + heedlessness characterization).

inna_inclusio.note: إِنَّا (divine plural) opens — gift assertion. إِنَّ closes — verdict assertion. Same emphatic particle, different agent-orientation: divine gift (v.1) ↔ enemy's state (v.3).

kawthar_abtar_phoneme_pair.note: Both carry definite article; both are إِنَّ predicate nominals; shared [-tar] terminal cluster. Phonetic and syntactic parallelism across envelope verses — gift named ↔ enemy named with phonemic echo.

ka_suffix_chain.note: Possessive suffix كَ sustained across vv.2–3 (your Lord / your enemy) — binds command and verdict as addressed to the same recipient.

tightest_rhyme_in_dataset.note: No rhyme break at any verse. Tightest rhyme architecture in dataset. Compare: Q.114 uniform [-aːs] across 6 verses is the only other no-break case. Q.108 achieves it in 3 verses where every structural signal type is active simultaneously.

huw_isolation.note: Pronoun of restriction — 'he [and not you] is the severed one.' Sole occurrence in surah. Reverses the accusation implied against the Prophet and redirects it onto the enemy. Discourse-level inversion device.
