cross_surah_pivot_positions.note: Supplementary observation from analysis document — detected pivot positions across dataset:
cross_surah_pivot_positions.observation: In every case the detector identifies the geometric or functional center as the highest convergence zone. Pattern recorded without interpretation.

pivot.verse_ar: لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِينِ
pivot.notes: Denial chain → bilateral declaration; first simultaneous address of both parties; ع-ب-د root absent entirely for first time; دِين introduced — only new root in final verse; [-iini] unique terminal; shortest verse; nominal-only construction
pivot.center_logic_note: terminal functional v.6 (5/5); the surah's structural argument completes only at its final verse — the repeated لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ / وَلِيَ دِينِ reversal resolves at the last verse. No pivot precedes it; the surah builds linearly to its close.

secondary_convergence.notes: Tense frame shifts present→future; sole past tense form عَبَدتُّمْ; participial construction replaces finite verb; [-tum] rhyme break with geminated تّ. Structural seam within the denial block — inner axis of the 4-verse negation unit.

boundaries.label: Negation block opens

boundaries.label: Verbatim mirror vv.3/5
boundaries.notes: وَلا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ — exact phonetic and lexical duplication across a non-adjacent verse pair. Only instance of verbatim verse-repetition in dataset. Noted separately for analytical weight — this signal is independent of all other passes and confirms structural symmetry without inference.

boundaries.label: Denial block
boundaries.notes: Root ع-ب-د 8 occurrences; مَا ×4; وَلا ×3; verbatim duplication vv.3/5; tense pivot at v.4

verbatim_duplication.note: Exact phonetic and lexical duplication — only instance of verbatim verse-repetition in dataset. v.3 present tense ↔ v.5 future tense context; identical wording, different temporal frames.
verbatim_duplication.analytical_weight: exceptional — signal is self-confirming across passes (lexical + prosodic duplication simultaneously); no inference required
verbatim_duplication.cross_dataset_note: Strongest single lexical mirror signal in dataset. All other mirrors are confirmed by root repetition with semantic variation; this is verbatim identical text across two non-adjacent verses.

leitwort_abd.note: Dominant root across vv.2–5; completely absent at v.6 (the pivot). Absence at final verse is the structural signal — parallel to اللَّهِ absence at pivot in Q.112/Q.110.

din_terminal.note: Sole occurrence in surah; sole new root introduced at final verse; [-iini] terminal isolated. The worship-root disappears and the religion-root replaces it — semantic pivot matching structural pivot.

chiastic_possessives_v6.note: Paired possessive pronouns in terminal verse: 'for you' / 'for me'. Chiastic distribution mirrors the entire surah's alternating you/I structure compressed into a single bipartite clause.

tense_grid.note: 2×2 tense/person grid across vv.2–5: speaker-present / addressee-present / speaker-future / addressee-future. Most systematic internal structure in dataset.

pass_3_mirror.notes: All correspondences are thematic inference only. Every Quranic mirror was confirmed by verbatim or root-identical repetition at symmetric positions. Pattern not falsifiable from lexical evidence. Structural confidence: LOW.

pass_4_discourse.notes: 4 distinct discourse states across 5 units. Genuine independently verifiable signals — but narrative prose naturally generates discourse mobility. Genre feature, not structural marker.

signal_table.note: divine act; narrator disappears; Q.74:1–5 embedded

apparent_pivot.label: S3
apparent_pivot.reason: S3 is geometric center and accumulates 5 signals — all at LOW independent weight. None confirmed by cross-pass lexical anchoring. Geometric centrality in a 5-unit sequence is necessary but not sufficient.

theme_shifts.note: high density — characteristic of narrative prose, not design

lexical_signals.quality: all local or weak; zero verbatim framing

mirror_signals.quality: all thematic inference; zero lexical anchor

discourse_shifts.quality: strongest category; independently verifiable but genre-expected

prosodic_signals.quality: local features only; zero terminal rhyme architecture

key_differentiators_vs_quran.quran: Consistent terminal rhyme across all verses
key_differentiators_vs_quran.control: Zero terminal rhyme; all 5 terminals phonetically distinct

key_differentiators_vs_quran.quran: Mirrors confirmed by verbatim/root-identical repetition at symmetric positions
key_differentiators_vs_quran.control: All mirror candidates thematic inference only; zero lexical anchor

key_differentiators_vs_quran.quran: Every surah shows confirmed lexical inclusio spanning full text
key_differentiators_vs_quran.control: No opening/closing frame; السَّمَاء clusters S1–S2 only

key_differentiators_vs_quran.quran: Concentrated at pivot; once per surah
key_differentiators_vs_quran.control: 4 shifts across 5 units — fully distributed; genre feature

experimental_report.conclusion: The five-pass detector produces surface-level outputs when applied to hadith prose. Signals lack cross-pass confirmation, lexical anchoring, and prosodic architecture. Apparent convergence at S3 is a function of geometric centrality, not independent structural design. CRITICAL FINDING: the protocol distinguishes hadith prose from Quranic verse at the level of signal QUALITY, not merely signal PRESENCE.

control_verdict.verdict: WEAK — 5 apparent signals at S3, 0 confirmed. No rhyme architecture, no verbatim inclusio, no lexically-anchored mirror. Discourse shifts genuine but genre-expected.
control_verdict.methodological_note: Embedded Q.74 citation carries Quranic structural features belonging to the Quran layer only. The زَمِّلُونِي/الْمُدَّثِّر bridge is a Quranic allusion, not a hadith compositional device. Layers must be analyzed separately.

control_text_structure_tests.source: Nawawi 40 Hadith / Sahih al-Bukhari / Sahih Muslim
control_text_structure_tests.title: Hadith 1 — Actions by Intentions
control_text_structure_tests.pattern_note: [U1–U2] principle block // [U3–U4] application block. NOT inverted, NOT envelope, NOT centered. U3↔U4 is sequential parallel couplet, not chiastic/inverted.
control_text_structure_tests.pivot_note: No unit reaches ≥4 independent signal convergence. U3 scores 5 surface signals but mirror is PARALLEL not INVERTED; U4 replicates all signals at equal strength. U3 is first half of parallel couplet, not pivot.

key_differentiators_vs_quran.rhyme: Zero terminal rhyme architecture — all four terminals phonetically unrelated.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.mirror_type: U3↔U4 sequential parallel couplet only — not chiastic/inverted. No cross-text root anchoring at symmetric positions.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.lexical_partition: ن-و-ي (U1–U2) and هـ-ج-ر (U3–U4) cleanly separated, zero cross-block linkage. Roots partition text rather than weave through it.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.discourse_mobility: Only 2 genuine discourse shifts across 4 units vs. 3–5 in Quranic surahs of comparable length.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.structural_pattern: Linear bipartite — no Quranic surah in dataset is purely linear; all produced inverted or envelope patterns.

comparison_vs_CT001.verdict: CT-002 scores higher than CT-001 in lexical signal quality and mirror confidence. Still lacks: rhyme architecture, cross-block threading, inverted mirror, discourse mobility, any ≥4-signal pivot convergence.
comparison_vs_CT001.conclusion: Protocol distinguishes hadith from Quran at level of signal TYPE, INTERACTION, and structural PATTERN — not merely signal presence. Sequential bipartite parallelism is a feature of Arabic legal/wisdom prose, not multi-axis convergence architecture.

control_text_structure_tests.title: Psalm 23

units.en: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want

units.he: בִּנְאוֹת דֶּשֶׁא יַרְבִּיצֵנִי / עַל־מֵי מְנֻחוֹת יְנַהֲלֵנִי
units.en: In green pastures He makes me lie down; beside still waters He leads me

units.he: נַפְשִׁי יְשׁוֹבֵב / יַנְחֵנִי בְּמַעְגְּלֵי־צֶדֶק לְמַעַן שְׁמוֹ
units.en: My soul He restores; He leads me in paths of righteousness for His name's sake

units.he: גַּם כִּי־אֵלֵךְ בְּגֵיא צַלְמָוֶת / לֹא־אִירָא רָע כִּי־אַתָּה עִמָּדִי / שִׁבְטְךָ וּמִשְׁעַנְתֶּךָ הֵמָּה יְנַחֲמֻנִי
units.en: Even through the valley of deep darkness, I will fear no evil, for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff — they comfort me

units.he: תַּעֲרֹךְ לְפָנַי שֻׁלְחָן נֶגֶד צֹרְרָי / דִּשַּׁנְתָּ בַשֶּׁמֶן רֹאשִׁי כּוֹסִי רְוָיָה
units.en: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows

units.he: אַךְ טוֹב וָחֶסֶד יִרְדְּפוּנִי כָּל־יְמֵי חַיַּי / וְשַׁבְתִּי בְּבֵית־יְהוָה לְאֹרֶךְ יָמִים
units.en: Surely goodness and steadfast love will pursue me all the days of my life; and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever

pivot.note: Geometric center; sole danger verse; unmirrored in content; נ-ח-מ root unique here; third-person→second-person אַתָּה shift (sharpest discourse shift in text); longest verse (6 cola); [-uni] echoes [-eni] of v.2 across valley boundary

control_text_structure_tests.pattern_note: A (v.1 יְהוָה frame) → B (vv.2–3 pastoral provision) → X (v.4 valley pivot) → B′ (v.5 banquet provision) → A′ (v.6 יְהוָה house frame). Confirmed inverted mirror with lexical inclusio.

yhwh_inclusio.note: First confirmed verbatim lexical inclusio in any non-Quranic control text. Identical pattern to Quranic inclusio devices (e.g. النَّاسِ in Q.114, أَحَدٌ in Q.112).

third_to_second_person_pivot.v4: אַתָּה עִמָּדִי — You are with me (direct address opens)
third_to_second_person_pivot.note: Person shift at geometric center mirrors Q.1 Al-Fatiha v.5 (إِيَّاكَ — You we worship) — both texts pivot from declarative divine description to direct address at their structural center.

lo_negation_pair.v1: לֹא אֶחְסָר — I shall not want
lo_negation_pair.v4: לֹא אִירָא — I will fear no evil
lo_negation_pair.note: Distal negation pair at frame and pivot positions — structurally analogous to Quranic distal concatenation devices.

first_person_suffix_chain.note: Suffix -י (first-person singular) sustained across 14+ positions throughout text — most sustained single-suffix chain in any text analyzed. Structurally analogous to Q.99 suffix declension but here static (person unchanged) rather than evolving.

pastoral_banquet_provision_mirror.B: vv.2–3 — pastures, still waters, restored soul (third-person provision)
pastoral_banquet_provision_mirror.B_prime: v.5 — table, anointed head, overflowing cup (second-person provision)
pastoral_banquet_provision_mirror.note: Pastoral abundance ↔ banquet abundance. Both positive; both marked by surplus imagery. Strongest thematic mirror pair in text.

signal_table.lexical: יְהוָה inclusio opens; לֹא אֶחְסָר; רֹעִי

signal_table.prosody: [-eni]×2 exact terminal pair — only verbatim rhyme in text

signal_table.lexical: לֹא אִירָא distal pair closes; אַתָּה↑; נ-ח-מ↑; שִׁבְטְךָ/מִשְׁעַנְתֶּךָ
signal_table.prosody: rhythm break — longest verse 6 cola; [-uni] echoes [-eni]; אַתָּה phonetically isolated; 5-sig pivot

signal_table.lexical: יְהוָה inclusio closes↓; יָמִים×2; חֶסֶד; וְשַׁבְתִּי
signal_table.prosody: [-ayai/-yamim] temporal expansion; יָמִים sole masc. pl. absolute terminal; most varied close

block_map.label: יְהוָה רֹעִי — shepherd declared
Pastoral provision — pastures, waters, restoration
PIVOT — valley; אַתָּה; danger; comfort
Banquet provision — table, oil, cup
בֵּית־יְהוָה — house vow; יָמִים

control_text_structure_tests.tier_note: Tier 2 (of 4 detected): partial multi-axis convergence + confirmed inclusio + inverted mirror + 5-signal pivot. Lacks Quranic-level rhyme architecture and prosodic zone system.

key_differentiators_vs_quran.rhyme: No sustained terminal rhyme scheme — Hebrew psalm verse operates on synonymous/antithetical/synthetic parallelism, not terminal rhyme. Only terminal pair ([-eni]×2, v.2) is couplet-internal. Rhyme architecture remains the single most reliable Quran differentiator.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.leitwort_threading: First-person suffix -י ubiquitous but grammatically required by POV genre convention, not architecturally selected as in Quranic leitwörter (e.g. النَّاسِ ×5 in Q.114 as structurally chosen content word).
key_differentiators_vs_quran.prosodic_zones: No bipartite or tripartite rhyme-zone division corresponding to thematic units. Quranic surahs consistently produced clean zone architecture (e.g. Q.107, Q.100, Q.96).

features_shared_with_quran.verbatim_inclusio: יְהוָה v.1↔v.6 — first non-Quranic text to produce confirmed verbatim frame inclusio
features_shared_with_quran.unmirrored_center: v.4 as sole danger verse at geometric center — matches Quranic inverted pattern exactly
features_shared_with_quran.discourse_pivot: Third-person→second-person shift at center — matches Q.1 Al-Fatiha v.5 pivot pattern
features_shared_with_quran.five_signal_convergence: v.4 reaches 5-signal convergence — matches signal count of Al-Falaq, Al-Masad, Al-Kawthar, Al-Fatiha

four_tier_protocol_ranking.tier1_quran: Full multi-axis convergence + rhyme architecture + inverted mirror
four_tier_protocol_ranking.tier2_psalm23: Partial convergence + inclusio + inverted mirror candidate — NO rhyme architecture
four_tier_protocol_ranking.tier3_nawawi_hadith: Sequential parallelism only — NO rhyme, NO chiasm, NO cross-block threading
four_tier_protocol_ranking.tier4_bukhari_prose: Local features only — NO architecture at any level

control_text_structure_tests.title: Imru' al-Qays — Mu'allaqah (Opening 8 Verses)
control_text_structure_tests.tier_note: Between Tier 3 (Nawawi) and Tier 2 (Psalm 23). Arabic verse with terminal rhyme — highest surface similarity to Quran in dataset; lowest structural equivalence ratio relative to that surface similarity.
control_text_structure_tests.pattern_note: Elegiac movement: location → ruin-trace → visual observation → interior emotion. Genre convention of wuqūf 'alā al-aṭlāl (standing at ruins). Directional, not reflexive. No inverted mirror detected. Structural confidence for any cross-unit mirror: LOW.
control_text_structure_tests.pivot_note: No single verse reaches ≥4 independent signal convergence. Only candidate zone: vv.6–7 boundary (4 signals: theme shift + كَأَنَّ leitwort + discourse shift + فُلْفُلِ prosodic density). This is a seam, not a pivot.

convergence_zones.note: كَأَنَّ root bridges thematic cut; فُلْفُلِ reduplication at terminal; discourse shift (تَرَى→كَأَنِّي); theme shift (external→internal). Only zone reaching ≥4 threshold.

signal_table.lexical: رَسْمُهَا -هَا chain opens; فَ chain closes

key_differentiators_vs_quran.rhyme_type: Qaṣīda monorhyme (rawī = لِ) sustained uniformly across ALL 8 verses — carries ZERO structural information; cannot mark pivots or boundaries because it never changes. CONTRAST: Quranic variable rhyme shifts at structural boundaries and IS the structural signal.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.mirror_type: Linear ekphrasis (place→ruin→trace→emotion) — genre convention of aṭlāl poetry. No inverted/envelope mirror detected. Every Quranic surah produced an inverted mirror candidate.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.inclusio: No verbatim lexical inclusio confirmed. مَنزِلِ/حَنْظَلِ consonantal similarity is phonetic coincidence within monorhyme, not lexical design.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.pivot_convergence: No single-verse 5-signal pivot. Maximum: 4-signal 2-verse boundary zone (vv.6–7). Every Quranic surah produced ≥5 signals at a single pivot verse.
key_differentiators_vs_quran.arabic_surface_similarity: Most superficially similar corpus to Quran (Arabic, rhyming, comparable length) — yet lowest structural equivalence ratio relative to that surface similarity. Monorhyme is structurally inert vs. Quranic variable rhyme as device.

features_shared_with_quran.discourse_mobility: 4 genuine discourse shifts across 8 verses (dual imperative → third-person narration → second-person observation → first-person subject) — comparable to Quranic short surahs.
features_shared_with_quran.arabic_terminal_rhyme: Terminal rhyme present throughout — but structurally inert monorhyme, not architecturally variable.

control_text_structure_tests.critical_finding: The five-pass protocol successfully distinguishes pre-Islamic Arabic monorhyme poetry from Quranic verse despite both being: (1) Arabic, (2) terminal-rhyming, (3) comparable unit-count. Differentiator is not the PRESENCE of rhyme but its STRUCTURAL BEHAVIOR — whether rhyme varies to mark structure (Quran) or remains fixed as genre convention (qaṣīda).

pivot_position_survey.observation: In every surah analyzed (n=17), the five-pass detector identifies the geometric or functional center as the highest convergence zone. No surah has been analyzed where the highest-signal verse is peripheral without functional justification.

entries.name: Al-Qamar
entries.note: 55v. Offset -0.073. Thamūd at structural center; narrative arc 9+5+[10]+8+2. Quran-refrain ×4 absent after Pharaoh — structural-theological signal. السَّاعَة bracket (vv.1/46). مُقْتَدِرٍ bracket (vv.42/55). مُسْتَمِرّ inversion. ضلال وسعر reversal. Near-100% rā' unity. Three consecutive surahs 54-56 without يَا أَيُّهَا.

entries.name: Al-Raḥmān
entries.note: 78v. Offset -0.154 (early pivot at vv.26-27; geometric midpoint v.39). Sequential dual architecture. Refrain ×31. الرَّحْمَٰنُ sole opening verse. الْمِيزَانَ ×3 consecutive. الْمُجْرِمُونَ sole [-uūna] — most extreme terminal isolation in corpus. ذُو الْجَلَالِ ×2 (vv.27/78) pivot-and-close. Dual paradise hierarchy unique. تَبَارَكَ cross-67:1.

entries.name: Al-Wāqiʿah
entries.note: 96v. Offset +0.031 near-exact. Unique bipartite tripartite architecture. No يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا — يَا أَيُّهَا delay pattern reaches total elimination. Tripartite announcement ×3 consecutive vv.8–10. [-uūna] 100% ×16 vv.57–72 — longest uniform rhyme in corpus. فَسَبِّحْ EXACT ×2 bracket (vv.74/96). مَكْنُونٍ ×2 (vv.23/78). حَقُّ الْيَقِينِ cross-69:51. [-uūd] triple unique. ثُلَّة/قَلِيل theology.

entries.name: Al-Ḥadīd
entries.note: 29v. Offset +0.023 near-exact. Musabbiḥāt corpus opener. Latest يَا أَيُّهَا in sequence v.28/29. قَرْضًا حَسَنًا bracket vv.11/18 seals center. Four divine names v.3 unique. نُور ×6. Zone 1 ×12 — longest in sequence.

entries.name: Al-Mujādilah
entries.note: 22v. Offset +0.023 near-exact. Five-block inverted ring. Unique opening: قَدْ سَمِعَ. Party binary: حِزْبُ الشَّيْطَانِ ×2 / حِزْبُ اللَّهِ ×2. يُحَادُّونَ EXACT frame ×2 (vv.5/20). بِالْإِثْمِ EXACT TRIAD ×2 at pivot. خَبِير ×3 within surah. Mutual-pleasure unique.

entries.name: Quraysh
entries.note: First surah with two concurrent 5-signal candidates (v.3 + v.4)

entries.name: Al-Infitar
entries.note: Three concurrent 5-signal pivots (v.5 apodosis, v.9 كَلَّا center, v.19 terminal sovereignty). First multi-pivot surah in dataset. Secondary 5/5: vv.17–19 (Day interrogated + نَفْسٌ inclusio close). نَعِيمٍ/جَحِيمٍ exact terminal couplet vv.13–14. يَوْمَ الدِّينِ ×3 final five verses. Twin architecture with Q.84 (5 structural parallels, highest in sequence).

entries.name: Al-Takathur
entries.note: Third كَلَّا closes كَلَّا block; withheld apodosis; الْيَقِين introduced.

entries.name: Al-Qariah
entries.note: No single geometric center; compound boundary vv.3–4 registers 5 signals. Terminal vv.10–11 also 5 signals.

entries.name: Al-Adiyat
entries.note: Oath-verdict boundary; highest multi-signal convergence; 4-zone rhyme architecture breaks here.

entries.name: Al-Alaq
entries.note: Three كَلَّا pillars (vv.6/15/19) each register 5 signals. v.6 is primary (first pillar; الإِنسَان arc completes; longest rhyme zone opens). First 19-verse surah in dataset.

entries.name: Al-Inshirah
entries.note: Compound pivot constituted by consecutive near-verbatim self-repetition. First surah where the pivot IS the repeated verse. Midpoint 5.5; distance from geometric center 4.5 = 1.0.

entries.name: Ad-Duha
entries.note: Three 5-signal zones (vv.8–9, v.3, v.11). Primary seam at vv.8–9 (benefaction→command; يَتِيم bridge; rhyme break). Geometric center v.6; seam midpoint 8.5; distance 2.5.

entries.name: Al-Layl
entries.note: v.11 = geometric midpoint of 21-verse surah. Sole rhetorical question; longest verse; مَالَهُ bridge; تَرَدَّى unique. Perfect geometric center (11 of 21).

entries.name: Ash-Shams
entries.note: Compound apodosis at vv.9–10. Midpoint 9.5, geometric center 8.0, offset +1.5, norm 0.10. 8-verse oath sequence resolves into 2-verse moral verdict. Secondary at v.14 (5 signals). First linear-progressive architecture in dataset.

entries.name: Al-Balad
entries.note: Compound seam vv.11–12. Seam midpoint 11.5, geometric center 10.5, offset +1.0, norm 0.050. Failure declared (v.11) + interrogative definition (v.12). [-ayn] zone breaks at v.11. العَقَبَة ×2. Secondary at vv.8–10 (4 signals). First 20-verse surah in dataset.

entries.name: Al-Fajr
entries.note: كَلَّا first pillar v.17. Center 15.5, offset +1.5, norm 0.050. Second 5-signal zone at v.21 (كَلَّا second pillar). First 30-verse surah in dataset. Six rhyme zones. الْإِنسَانُ distal frame vv.15/23.

entries.name: Al-Ghashiyah
entries.note: Garden furnishings→creation contemplation. Center 13.5, v.17 offset +3.5, norm 0.135. Secondary: v.8 (4 signals, وُجُوهٌ exact bridge), vv.25–26 (5 signals, divine reckoning). 7 rhyme zones — highest density. عَيْن antithetical bridge. Two morphological rhyme zones.

entries.name: Al-A'la
entries.note: Compound seam vv.9–10. Center 10, seam midpoint 9.5, offset −0.5, norm −0.026. Near-exact. Secondary: vv.1/15 inclusio (4), vv.18–19 close (5). Full-surah [-a] ×19. ذ-ك-ر three-unit thread. سَبِّحِ sole imperative opening.

dominant_word_withheld_at_pivot.note: Q.112: اللَّهُ absent vv.3–4. Q.114: النَّاسِ absent v.4. Q.110: اللَّهِ→رَبِّكَ substitution at v.3. Three variants: full absence, cognate substitution, relational-form substitution.

leitwort_root_disappears_at_pivot.note: Q.109 v.6: ع-ب-د (×8 vv.2–5) absent at pivot. Q.111 v.3: لَهَب shifts from proper name to common noun at pivot (variant). Root-disappearance at terminal verse is an emerging pattern.

verbatim_verse_duplication.note: Q.109 vv.3/5 (non-adjacent exact duplication). Q.82 vv.17–18 (consecutive exact duplication at terminal). Q.102 vv.3–4 (consecutive modified — ثُمَّ prefix added). Q.99 vv.7–8: near-verbatim antithetical-substitution couplet. Q.94 vv.5–6: consecutive near-exact at structural center (فَ deleted only) — first instance in dataset where verbatim repetition IS the pivot. Q.78 vv.4-5: سَيَعْلَمُونَ verbatim ×2 consecutive with only ثُمَّ كَلَّا added. Only full-verb terminal pair in sequence. Q.75 vv.34-35: أَوْلَى لَكَ فَأَوْلَى / ثُمَّ أَوْلَى لَكَ فَأَوْلَى — woe verdict verbatim ×2 with ثُمَّ escalation. Same pattern as Q.78 vv.4-5 (سَيَعْلَمُونَ ×2 with ثُمَّ) and Q.82 (ثُمَّ وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ ×2). Q.75 vv.17-18: قُرْآنَهُ exact terminal ×2 consecutive (divine guarantee ↔ human instruction — both close with the Quran's name); also Q.75 vv.8-9: الْقَمَرُ exact ×2 consecutive in cosmic chain. Q.74 vv.19-20: فَقُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ / ثُمَّ قُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ — near-verbatim curse ×2 with ثُمَّ escalation. Same pattern as Q.75 vv.34-35 (أَوْلَى لَكَ) and Q.78 vv.4-5 (سَيَعْلَمُونَ). Six structural variants confirmed.

uniform_rhyme_no_break.note: Q.114: [-aːs] uniform across 6 verses. Q.108: [-ar] uniform across 3 verses. Both have 5-signal pivots. All other surahs break rhyme at or near pivot.

bipartite_prosodic_architecture.note: Q.107: [-iːn] vv.1–4 / [-uːn] vv.5–7 — two sustained zones of equal weight divided precisely at structural pivot. No other surah in dataset produces two clean rhyme zones simultaneously. Closest: Q.111 ab-chain-then-break, but Q.107 sustains both zones rather than fragmenting.

syntactic_suspension_device.note: Q.103: oath 1-verse span. Q.106: lam-purpose 2-verse span. Q.82: إِذَا ×4 4-verse span. Q.100: two suspension units — oath ×5 (vv.1–5→v.6) and إِذَا ×2 (vv.9–10→v.11). First surah with two suspension units. Both Q.100 resolutions introduced by إِنَّ+رَبّ. All suspension devices resolve at structural pivots. Q.95: four oath objects (vv.1–3) resolve at v.4 (لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا); pivot at v.5 (ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَاهُ) — oath resolution creates boundary, pivot is verse immediately following. Q.81 vv.1–13: longest إِذَا chain in sequence (13 verses), more than double the previous maximum (Q.82: 4v, Q.84: 5v). The suspension spans cosmic/animal/human/judicial/eschatological domains before resolving to a single soul (v.14). The apodosis (أَحْضَرَت) remains within the [-at] zone — unique case where chain and resolution are phonetically indistinguishable.

pure_2x2_mirror_no_center.note: Q.106: 4-verse surah with no geometric center — pure ABBA inverted mirror, equal-weight halves. Every other 4-verse surah (Q.112) has a boundary pivot. Q.106 has no single unmirrored center verse; the seam falls between halves.

three_pivot_architecture.note: Q.82: first surah in dataset producing three independent 5-signal convergence zones (v.5 protasis seam / v.9 center / v.19 terminal). Emerges at 19-verse length. Sub-7-verse surahs produce 1–2 pivots; Q.82 suggests pivot density scales with verse count.

widest_inclusio_span.note: Q.82 نَفْسٌ: 14-verse span (v.5 → v.19). Previous dataset maximum was full-surah spans of 3–7 verses. Wider surahs carry longer-range lexical frames.

terminal_consonant_echo_rhyme.note: Q.105: [-iːl] ×4 then [-uːl] — terminal /l/ preserved, vowel nucleus shifts iː→uː. Neither continuation nor full break — phonetic resolution. Distinct from: uniform rhyme (Q.114/Q.108), full terminal break (Q.109/Q.107), dispersing rhyme (Q.113). New rhyme closure strategy in dataset.

simile_close.note: Q.105 v.5: كَعَصْفٍ مَأْكُولٍ — comparative كَ closes the surah with a simile rather than a declaration, verdict, or divine name. First figurative/comparative surah close in dataset.

two_concurrent_5_signal_candidates.note: Q.106: v.3 + v.4. Q.105: v.3 (center pivot) + v.5 (terminal). Both are 4–5 verse surahs. Pattern: short surahs with no clear single center can distribute 5-signal density across pivot and terminal simultaneously. Q.85: vv.10–11 (compound seam, geometric center) + v.9 (narrative capstone, ش-ه-د thread closes). Both score 5/5 independently. Primary: vv.10–11 (geometric center + complete moral partition); secondary: v.9 (narrative capstone). Largest surah (22 verses) with two 5/5 candidates — consistent with longer surahs distributing convergence density.

compound_pivot.note: Q.104 vv.4–5: الْحُطَمَة spans verse boundary — named at v.4-end (كَلَّا + punishment) then interrogated at v.5 (وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ). Only compound pivot in dataset.

participial_triplet_close.note: Q.104 vv.6/8/9: الْمُوقَدَة/مُؤْصَدَة/مُمَدَّدَة — concentrated at close with escalating gemination. Q.101 vv.7/9/11: رَاضِيَة/هَاوِيَة/حَامِيَة — distributed across consequence structure. Both share [-iːa]-class terminal. Two variants: concentrated-close and distributed.

kalla_as_pivot_marker.note: كَلَّا (rejection particle) appears in Q.82, Q.89, Q.96, Q.102, Q.104 — always at or near structural pivot. Q.82 v.9 (center of 3-pivot ring). Q.89 vv.17+21 (double-pillar frame bracketing moral indictment unit). Q.96 vv.6/15/19 (triple pillar system). Q.102 vv.3–5 (graduated triple). Q.104 v.4 (compound pivot). No surah in dataset uses كَلَّا decoratively or in passing. Confirmed structural marker — presence of كَلَّا is the dataset's most reliable heuristic for pivot location before five-pass analysis. Q.83 v.18: كَلَّا ×4 — the most structurally loaded instance of كَلَّا in the dataset. Sits at the geometric seam (36v → midpoint 18.5); its كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَابَ opening is the exact syntactic mirror of كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَابَ at v.7, pivoting the surah from condemned to righteous. The progressive loading (×1 at v.7 / ×2 at v.14 / ×3 at v.15 / ×4 at v.18) creates a crescendo toward the pivot. Q.80 v.23: كَلَّا lَمَّا يَقْضِ مَا أَمَرَهُ — geometric center of 42-verse surah (norm +0.036); surah also has كَلَّا at v.11 (rebuke→Quran authentication pivot). Both كَلَّا instances mark prosodic zone transitions. The dual كَلَّا is unique: two instances each marking a distinct structural hinge. Q.78 vv.4-5: كَلَّا/ثُمَّ كَلَّا — double warning before creation evidence unit; structural hinge (challenge before proof). Only case where كَلَّا ×2 functions as escalating pre-evidence challenge. Q.75 v.20: كَلَّا ×2 + بَلْ double correction at compound seam — opens moral diagnosis unit at geometric center; confirms كَلَّا as pivot/seam marker rather than single-point pivot here.

asymmetric_weight_distribution.note: Q.103: word counts 2→5→17 across vv.1–3 — monotonic increase. Only surah in dataset where maximum prosodic weight falls on the terminal verse rather than the pivot. Every other surah: pivot carries compression or maximum density.

r_consonant_terminal_thread.note: Q.103: عَصْرِ/خُسْرٍ/صَبْرِ — terminal /r/ across all three verses with vowel nucleus variation throughout. No full terminal rhyme. New type: consonantal-terminal-thread-only, distinct from all previous rhyme categories in dataset.

withheld_apodosis.note: Q.102 v.5: لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ — conditional clause with permanently deferred apodosis. Only syntactically incomplete verse in dataset. Distinct from syntactic suspension (Q.106/Q.82/Q.103) where suspended clauses are resolved at pivot. Here the resolution is withheld indefinitely.

root_register_shift_at_pivot.note: Q.102: ع-ل-م (knowing) ×3 in vv.3–5 → ر-أ-ي (seeing) ×2 in vv.6–7. Full root-register substitution at structural pivot. The conditional 'if you knew' is answered not with knowledge but with sight. Parallel to dominant-word-substitution patterns but operating at the level of semantic register rather than specific term.

double_ma_adraka_frame.note: Q.101: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا at v.3 (suspends 7 verses) and v.10 (resolves in 1 verse). Phrase used as double structural bracket at both opening-close and body-close. Only surah in dataset where وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا appears at both structural boundaries.

triple_consecutive_opening_repetition.note: Q.101 الْقَارِعَة ×3 (vv.1–3): named→questioned→challenged. Q.97 لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر ×3 (vv.1–3): location→question→definition. Both: term exhausted at pivot, absent from remaining verses. Both: three distinct grammatical functions across three consecutive positions. Pattern confirmed at n=2.

four_zone_rhyme_architecture.note: Q.100: four distinct rhyme zones — [-ħan] (vv.1–3), [-ʕan] (vv.4–5), [-uːd/-iːd] (vv.6–8), [-uːr/-iːr] (vv.9–11). Zone-break at v.6 aligns with all structural boundaries. Most segmented prosodic architecture in dataset. Previous max: Q.102 (4 zones across 8v); Q.107 bipartite (2 zones). Q.100 is the first where zone-boundaries map precisely to thematic units.

suffix_declension_as_structure.note: Q.99: -هَا (earth, vv.1–5) → -هُم (people, v.6) → -هُ (individual, vv.7–8). Rhyme family /a+h/ maintained across all 8 verses; only grammatical person shifts. Suffix tracks thematic subject-scale: cosmic → collective → individual. No equivalent in dataset.

paired_terminal_lexical_architecture.note: Q.98: three distinct terminal words (الْبَيِّنَة, الْقَيِّمَة, الْبَرِيَّة) each appearing exactly twice, each pair corresponding to a distinct thematic unit. Terminal rhyme and thematic structure are co-extensive. The [-iyya] family is a lexical, not merely phonetic, architecture. No equivalent in dataset.

identical_phrase_antithetical_deployment.note: Q.98: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا at v.6 (Hellfire) and v.8 (gardens). Identical phrase, antithetical eschatological referents. Parallel to Q.99 خَيْرًا↔شَرًّا substitution couplet, but here the frame phrase is identical while the context reverses. Only surah in dataset using the same exact phrase for both punishment and reward.

command_inversion.note: Q.96: اقْرَأْ (v.1, recite — external/vocal) ↔ وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ (v.19, prostrate/draw near — physical/inward). Both direct second-person imperatives to same addressee. Surah arc encoded in opening/closing command pair. No equivalent in dataset.

nine_verse_open_vowel_zone.note: Q.96 vv.6–14: alif maqsura [-a] terminal across 9 consecutive verses — longest single-terminal rhyme zone in dataset. Maps precisely onto transgression + confrontation block. Q.93 vv.1–8: 8-verse alif maqsura zone (second-longest in dataset) spanning oath + consolation + benefaction units — terminal is prosodically inert as a boundary marker within the zone; thematic units in vv.1–8 are detectable only through passes 1–4.

superlative_morphological_frame.note: Q.95: أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ (v.4, apodosis open) ↔ أَحْكَمِ الْحَاكِمِينَ (v.8, surah close). Both أَفْعَل superlative form. Frame: created best ↔ judged by the most just. Most semantically charged morphological frame in dataset.

tier1.label: Quranic surahs
tier1.criteria: multi-axis convergence + variable rhyme architecture + inverted mirror + single-verse 5-signal pivot

tier2.label: Psalm 23
tier2.criteria: confirmed inclusio + unmirrored center + discourse pivot — NO rhyme architecture

tier2b.label: Mu'allaqah (Imru' al-Qays)
tier2b.criteria: discourse mobility + كَأَنَّ bridge — monorhyme structurally inert — NO inclusio — NO inverted mirror
tier2b.note: Arabic verse; highest surface similarity to Quran; lowest structural equivalence ratio relative to that similarity

tier3.label: Nawawi Hadith 1
tier3.criteria: sequential parallelism only — NO rhyme, NO chiasm, NO cross-block threading

tier4.label: Bukhari narrative prose
tier4.criteria: local features only — NO architecture at any level

four_tier_control_model.key_differentiator: Rhyme BEHAVIOR (variable vs. monorhyme) is the single most reliable separator between Tier 1 and all other tiers. Presence of Arabic rhyme alone (CT-004) does not approach Tier 1.

three_zone_rhyme_coextensive.note: Q.94: three rhyme zones ([-ak]×4 / [-an]×2 / [-ab]×2) perfectly co-extensive with three thematic units (gift / promise / command) — no lag at any boundary. The rhyme-zone cuts ARE the thematic-unit boundaries. Only such case in dataset. Compare Q.100 (four zones, 11 verses — partial lag) and Q.107 (two zones, 7 verses — confirmed lag at v.5).

leitwort_frames_direct_address_units.note: Q.93: رَبُّكَ appears at structurally symmetric positions (vv.3/5/11) framing both direct-address units (consolation vv.3–5; command vv.9–11) while absent from both impersonal units (oath vv.1–2; benefaction narration vv.6–8). The leitwort thus marks discourse mode rather than thematic content — its presence signals direct address to the prophet; its absence signals impersonal register. Most precise positional leitwort deployment in dataset where a term marks opening/closing of non-consecutive units.

cross_unit_lexical_bridge.note: A content word crosses a thematic boundary, encoding the argument's causal or moral consequence without explicit statement. Four variants confirmed: Q.91 سَوَّاهَا (antithetical-root bridge: creation-fashioning [v.7] ↔ destruction-levelling [v.14]; same root, same terminal sound, opposite semantic valences; the only cross-unit prosodic signal in a uniform [-aha] environment). Q.92 مَالَهُ (identical-noun antithetical: hoarded wealth avails nothing [v.11] ↔ given wealth produces purification [v.18]). Q.93 يَتِيم (lexical-identity: object of divine care [v.6] → object of human obligation [v.9]). Q.94 burden/labor (semantic-opposition: removed burden [vv.2–3] → commanded labor [v.7]). Q.88: root ر-ف-ع — مَرْفُوعَةٌ (v.13, raised couches of Paradise) ↔ رُفِعَت (v.18, how the sky was raised); passive ptcp in garden ↔ passive perfect in creation. The raised furnishings of reward correspond to the raised sky of divine power. Q.86 ر-ج-ع (رَجْعِهِ v.8 resurrection ↔ الرَّجْعِ v.11 sky-return): only case where oath object carries same root as preceding unit's doctrinal pivot; bridge straddles exact terminal-zone boundary ([-ir] ends v.10, [-ajʕ/ opens v.11).

full_surah_uniform_terminal.note: Every verse carries the same terminal without exception. Q.87: alif maqsura [-a] ×19 — zero pre-terminal consonant repetition across consecutive verses; maximum consonant variety within complete vowel uniformity. Q.92: alif maqsura [-a] ×21. Q.91: feminine suffix [-aha] ×15. In all three cases full structural detection requires passes 1–4 only — prosody cannot serve as boundary marker. All three are phonologically distinct ([-a] vs [-aha]) but structurally equivalent. Three confirmed instances; the pattern clusters in the late Meccan surah sequence Q.87–Q.92. Q.72: [-adan] tanwīn 28/28 — 100% terminal unity; uniquely absolute in analyzed sequence. Previous entries (Q.87/Q.91/Q.92) all near-uniform; Q.72 achieves complete unity with zero exceptions across 28 verses.

three_layer_nested_antithesis.note: Q.92: three nested levels of binary opposition each encoded in antithetical near-identical sound: Layer 1 cosmic (vv.1–3): night↔day / male↔female — oath triplet. Layer 2 behavioral (vv.5–10): giver/believer ↔ withholder/denier — الْحُسْنَى ×2 exact + لِلْيُسْرَى/لِلْعُسْرَى near-rhyme. Layer 3 eschatological (vv.15–17): الْأَشْقَى ↔ الْأَتْقَى — أَفْعَل superlative antithetical pair. The surah's argument descends as a single antithesis from cosmic to behavioral to eschatological.

divine_person_shift_at_warning.note: Q.92: divine first-person plural نَا used for authority declarations (vv.7,10,12,13) then shifts to sole first-person singular at the warning verse (v.14: فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ). The 'We' announces sovereignty; the 'I' delivers the personal warning. Pattern not yet confirmed in other surahs in dataset.

lasawfa_yardhaa_companion_pair.note: Q.92 v.21 (وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَى) and Q.93 v.5 (وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضَى) share the identical construction لَسَوْفَ + يَرْضَى at the close of the righteous trajectory. Both surahs close their promise of satisfaction with the same emphatic future + contentment verb. Q.92 and Q.93 are structurally paired companion surahs confirmed by this shared terminal construction.

seven_oath_sequence.note: Q.91: seven consecutive oath objects across vv.1–7 — longest oath preamble in dataset. Structured in three groups: solar-temporal (vv.1–4), cosmic structural pair (vv.5–6), human soul (v.7). Progressive movement: cosmic exterior → human interior. The oath's structural logic terminates in the soul as the apodosis target. Previous longest oath sequences: Q.92 3-verse, Q.93 2-verse, Q.95 1-verse. Q.91's 7-verse oath unit is the dataset's length outlier for oath preambles.

verbal_bridge_antithetical_same_root.note: Q.91 vv.7/14: root س-و-ي with identical terminal sound (-هَا) deployed at both the oath culmination (سَوَّاهَا — the One Who fashioned the soul) and the narrative punishment climax (فَسَوَّاهَا — levelled them to the ground). Same root, opposite semantic valences: divine formation ↔ divine annihilation. In a full-surah uniform [-aha] terminal environment, this is the only terminal-level structural signal capable of marking a cross-unit boundary. Variant of cross_unit_lexical_bridge but specifically: verbatim root + terminal + antithetical semantics at structurally symmetric positions across major unit boundary.

walayli_oath_echo.note: Q.91 v.4 (وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَاهَا) and Q.92 v.1 (وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَى) share a near-verbatim oath construction. In Q.92 the night-covering is the surah's opening; in Q.91 it is the fourth element of a 7-verse solar oath sequence, with the feminine pronoun suffix (-hā) referring back to the sun. Q.91 and Q.92 may form a structural pair or sequence: Q.91 contains Q.92's opening construction as one element within a larger solar frame.

onomatopoeic_intraverse_climax.note: Intra-verse root reduplication or onomatopoeia marking the point of maximum thematic intensity — distinct from terminal rhyme signaling. Q.91 v.14: دَمْدَمَ (quadrilateral reduplicated — rumbling/crushing) at narrative punishment climax. Q.99 v.1: زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزَالَهَا (root repetition opening the surah with seismic intensity). Q.95 v.5: أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ (morphological escalation at verdict pivot). Pattern: phonetic density is concentrated at the structurally loaded verse when the terminal environment cannot provide prosodic differentiation.

ashaqa_superlative_echo.note: Q.91 v.12: أَشْقَاهَا — the most wicked of them (Thamud); names the agent who slaughtered the she-camel and triggered divine punishment. Q.92 v.15: لَا يَصْلَاهَا إِلَّا الْأَشْقَى — none shall enter the fire except the most wretched; names the eschatological agent of self-destruction. Both: root ش-ق-ي in superlative form (أَفْعَل pattern) naming the agent of the worst possible transgression in the surah's argument. Q.91's الْأَشْقَى is historical-specific (named actor in Thamud narrative); Q.92's الْأَشْقَى is universal-eschatological (type, not individual). Compare الأتقى (Q.92 v.17) which is Q.91's زَكَّاهَا moral counterpart. Cross-surah superlative antithetical pair: الأشقى (Q.91/Q.92) ↔ الأتقى (Q.92).

kadhaba_denial_frame.note: Root ك-ذ-ب used as denial frame in consecutive surahs with different register. Q.91: كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ (v.11) + فَكَذَّبُوهُ (v.14) — historical denial frame bracketing the Thamud narrative unit; denial → punishment. Q.92 v.16: الَّذِي كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّى — characterizes الْأَشْقَى eschatologically; the same root that frames Q.91's narrative punishment defines Q.92's eschatological category. Pattern: ك-ذ-ب in Q.91 is historical narrative; in Q.92 it is universal verdict. Consistent with Q.91/Q.92 structural relationship: Q.91 exemplum → Q.92 verdict.

compound_seam_pattern.note: Q.85 vv.10–11: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ×2 exact consecutive; torturers condemned / believers rewarded — complete eschatological partition; each verse carries phonetically isolated terminal ([-iːq]/[-iːr]). Compound seam: pivot spreads across two consecutive verses. Q.86 vv.9–10: Day of secrets declared (يَوْمَ تُبْلَى السَّرَائِرُ) + negated human resources (فَمَا لَهُ مِن قُوَّةٍ وَلَا نَاصِرٍ). Compound seam: pivot spreads across two consecutive verses. Q.87 vv.9–10: فَذَكِّرْ (command) + سَيَذَّكَّرُ مَن يَخْشَى (reception declared). Q.90 vv.11–12: فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ الْعَقَبَةَ (failure declared) + وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ (interrogated). Q.93 vv.8–9: unit-boundary seam. Q.94 vv.5–6: verbatim central doubling. Now confirmed: vc=8–20 range. Q.87 is smallest compound seam surah at 19 verses. Q.73 vv.10-11: conduct command (patience/avoidance) seam to divine leave-Me sovereignty — midpoint 10.5 = exact geometric center (offset 0.000). Seventh compound seam confirmed.

dual_morphology_generates_rhyme_zone.note: Q.90 vv.8–10: the Arabic dual morphological suffix (-ayn) produces a three-verse rhyme zone (عَيْنَيْنِ/شَفَتَيْنِ/النَّجْدَيْنِ) where grammar and rhyme are identical. The two eyes/two lips/two paths are grammatically dual (pairs) and phonetically dual (each terminal ends in -ayn). The morphological form of the gift IS the sound of the gift's terminal. Only confirmed case in dataset where a single grammatical category (dual number) generates and exhausts a complete rhyme zone. The moral duality (two paths, النَّجْدَيْنِ, good ↔ evil) is encoded in the same grammatical-phonetic structure as the physical duality (two eyes, two lips).

maf3alah_morphological_echo.note: Q.90: the مَفْعَلَة (maf'ala) pattern noun appears at two structurally distinct positions. (1) vv.14–16: circumstantial nouns of condition defining the mercy acts of the pass — مَسْغَبَة (hunger-famine)/مَقْرَبَة (near-relation)/مَتْرَبَة (dust-destitution). (2) vv.18–19: الْمَيْمَنَة (right-companions)/الْمَشْأَمَة (left-companions) — binary destiny. The same grammatical form used for the mercy-conditions reappears in the destiny-conditions. Morphological echo connecting acts of mercy (pass) to their eschatological recompense (destiny). Within the surah, the form creates a sublexical link between the deeds and their judgment.

la_uqsimu_direct_oath.note: Q.90: every other oath surah in dataset opens with waw al-qasam (وَ) — impersonal, grammatical, speaker invisible. Q.90 opens with divine first-person declaring the oath directly: لَا أُقْسِمُ بِهَٰذَا الْبَلَدِ ('I swear by this city'). Speaker present from word 1. Additionally, v.2 inserts the Prophet as addressee within the oath unit itself ('وَأَنتَ حِلٌّ بِهَٰذَا الْبَلَدِ' — and you are free within it). No other oath unit in dataset inserts the addressee as a qualifier within the oath chain. The oath is uniquely voiced (first-person) and uniquely relational (Prophet addressed within oath object). The الْبَلَد exact repeat (vv.1–2) is an intra-oath-unit inclusio rather than a surah-spanning frame. Q.84 v.16: فَلَا أُقْسِمُ بِالشَّفَقِ — same divine first-person oath formula, mid-surah (after the بَلَى correction of v.15), opens the cosmic oath unit. The فَ connects the oath directly to the correction as consequence. Both Q.90 and Q.84 use the formula at structurally significant mid-surah positions rather than as surah openers. Q.81 v.15: فَلَا أُقْسِمُ بِالْخُنَّسِ — follows apodosis of 13-verse إِذَا chain; divine first-person enters at the geometric center (v.15 of 29); marks the compound seam pivot. In Q.81 and Q.84 the formula follows the cosmic chain apodosis; in Q.90 it opens the surah. All three deploy it at structurally significant positions rather than as routine oath openers. Q.75 vv.1-2: لَا أُقْسِمُ ×2 in consecutive verses — only case in sequence where the oath formula itself repeats across consecutive verses. Two oath objects (Day of Resurrection / reproaching soul).

five_zone_rhyme_architecture.note: Q.90: five distinct rhyme zones ([-ad]×7, [-ayn]×3, [-qabah]×3, [-abah]×3, varied×4). Most varied rhyme architecture in dataset for a surah of this length (vc=20). Compare Q.101 (9 zones in 11 verses — highest zone density) and Q.82 (11 zones in 19 verses). Q.90 is notable for zone regularity: each of zones 2–4 is exactly 3 verses with high phonetic consistency, before the final 4-verse zone fragments as the destiny bifurcates. The decreasing-regularity architecture encodes the surah's argument: early zones are phonetically sealed (oath/gifts/pass), the destiny unit is phonetically open.

double_kalla_architecture.note: Q.89: كَلَّا appears exactly twice at vv.17 and 21. Each instance faces both backward and forward: v.17 refutes false inference (vv.15–16) AND opens moral indictment; v.21 closes indictment AND opens eschatological consequence. The two pillars bracket the moral indictment block: [كَلَّا]+[four charges vv.17–20]+[كَلَّا]. Compare Q.96 triple كَلَّا (vv.6/15/19): three pillars distributed across a longer text. Q.89's double deployment creates a tighter binary frame around a single moral unit. Both Q.89 and Q.96 produce one كَلَّا 5-signal primary pivot and secondary 5-signal zones.

insan_distal_frame.note: Q.89: الْإِنسَانُ appears exactly twice at structurally symmetric positions. v.15: deluded man; falsely interprets divine testing as personal honor/humiliation. v.23: regretful man; remembers on the Day but too late. Between the two occurrences, discourse shifts to collective second-person plural 'you' (vv.17–20) — the individual الْإِنسَانُ frames the collective indictment. Structurally equivalent to: Q.90 الْبَلَد ×2 intra-unit frame; Q.92 الْأَشْقَى/الْأَتْقَى distal superlative pair. Pattern: a key content noun appears exactly twice marking the opening and close of the surah's moral argument arc.

eschatological_unit_phonetic_instability.note: Rapid terminal shifts at eschatological/destiny units — the Day resists phonetic uniformity. Q.89 Zone 5 (vv.21–26): [-akk]/[-aff]/[-a]/[-aːtiː]/[-ahad]×2 — six verses, five terminals. Q.90 Zone 5 (vv.17–20): four verses, four terminals — no single sound dominates. Q.92 vv.14–21: varied terminals in the warning/righteous section. Pattern: thematic units that describe the Day of Judgment or eschatological bifurcation resist the phonetic regularity that characterizes earlier structural units in the same surah. The phonetic instability appears to encode the rupture of familiar order.

reflexive_interrogative_apodosis.note: Q.89 v.5: هَلْ فِي ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٌ لِّذِي حِجْرٍ — unique oath resolution. Every other oath surah in dataset delivers an apodosis (declaration/verdict/creation). Q.89 reflects the oath chain back at the listener as a challenge: 'Is there not in that an oath for one of perception?' The audience is invited to confirm the oath's sufficiency rather than receiving a verdict. ذَٰلِكَ anaphoric to the four-verse oath chain. لِذِي حِجْرٍ positions the rational audience as the implicit arbiter. The apodosis IS the challenge to respond.

r_consonant_oath_terminal_unity.note: Oath unit unified by final /r/ consonant across varied vowel environments. Q.89 vv.1–5: five distinct terminals ([-ajr]/[-aʃr]/[-atr]/[-asr]/[-ijr]) — consonant /r/ holds, vowel varies. Q.103 vv.1–3: عَصْرِ/خُسْرٍ/صَبْرِ — terminal /r/ across all three verses, vowel nucleus variation throughout. Both: /r/ acts as consonantal-terminal thread providing unity without uniformity. Previously registered for Q.103 as 'r_consonant_terminal_thread' — Q.89 confirms it as a feature of oath-unit prosody specifically.

root_exhaustion_at_pivot.note: A content root appears near the pivot, then is absent from all subsequent verses. Q.89: ك-ر-م ×2 at v.15 (human falsely claims honor), antithetical gap at v.16, negated at pivot v.17 (لَا تُكْرِمُونَ — you do not honor), absent from vv.18–30. The correction uses the same root falsely invoked in the delusion. Q.93: رَبُّكَ triple frame (vv.3/5/11) exhausted after final command verse — root present only at structurally marked positions. Pattern variant: root exhaustion completes at the pivot or at structurally symmetric positions, functioning as a lexical closure marker.

morphological_rhyme_zone.note: A grammatical morphological pattern generates a complete rhyme zone — grammar and rhyme are the same phenomenon. Q.88 vv.13–16: مَفْعُولَةٌ passive ptcp ×4 — garden furnishings all passive; the form of divine arrangement IS the terminal sound. Q.88 vv.17–20: passive perfect [-at] ×4 — how things were created/raised/set/levelled; the grammar of divine creation IS the terminal. Q.90 vv.8–10: dual suffix [-ayn] ×3 — two eyes/lips/paths grammatically dual AND phonetically dual; first confirmed case of dual number generating a rhyme zone. Q.91 (full-surah [-aha] suffix): feminine pronoun chain across all 15 verses. Pattern: morphological form encodes the semantic content at the level of sound. Q.84: three grammar-as-rhyme zones in one surah — (a) passive perfect feminine [-at] ×5 (vv.1–5: cosmic submission encoded in passive morphology), (b) [-aq] ×4 (vv.16–19: oath zone), (c) imperfect plural [-uːna] ×4 (vv.20–23: ongoing disbelief encoded in imperfect morphology). Most concentrated grammar-as-rhyme system in dataset. Q.79 zone 2 (vv.6-14): [-ah] feminine nouns ×9 — the eschatological convulsion + deniers + rebuttal unit; blasts, trembling hearts, deniers speech, earth surface all in feminine [-ah]; 9 consecutive. Also zone 4 (vv.27-32): [-aha] pronominal suffix ×6 — the sky/earth creation unit; all pronouns refer back to السَّمَاء/الأَرض.

hal_ataka_direct_prophet_address.note: Q.88: هَلْ أَتَاكَ حَدِيثُ الْغَاشِيَةِ — most intimate/conversational opening in dataset. Every other surah analyzed opens with a structural element (oath: وَ/لَا أُقْسِمُ; creation declaration; imperative; or third-person narration). Q.88 opens with direct second-person address to the Prophet as its very first word — presupposing an ongoing conversation and prior relationship. The Prophet is re-addressed at v.21 (فَذَكِّرْ imperative). The two direct addresses (v.1/v.21) frame the entire eschatological and creation content (vv.2–20) as material delivered between two moments of prophetic address. Q.85 v.17: هَلْ أَتَاكَ حَدِيثُ الْجُنُودِ — same construction at v.17, opens second historical reference block after divine attribute unit (contrast Q.88 where it opens the surah). Both introduce historical punishment reports. Cross-surah syntactic construction confirmed. Q.79 v.15: هَلْ أَتَاكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَى — opens the Moses narrative unit mid-surah; Prophet addressed to receive narrative. In Q.88 the construction opens the surah; in Q.85 it opens the pit narrative; in Q.79 it transitions from eschatological rebuttal to historical exemplar.

content_noun_antithetical_bridge.note: A single content noun appears at structurally symmetric positions across the surah's central thematic boundary, deploying the same word with opposite semantic valences to encode the surah's binary argument. Q.88: عَيْنٌ (spring) — boiling (v.5, condemned) ↔ flowing (v.12, rewarded); water as medium of both punishment and reward. Q.92: مَالَهُ (his wealth) — given generously (v.11, the righteous) ↔ withheld/squandered (v.18, the wretched). Q.93: يَتِيم (orphan) — Prophet was orphan (v.6, past state) ↔ do not oppress the orphan (v.9, command). Q.95: أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ (lowest of the low) — the human reduced to worst state after being created in best form (v.5 ↔ v.4). Pattern confirmed in Q.88, Q.92, Q.93, Q.95 — a content noun crosses the pivot to carry the argument's binary logic within a single lexical unit.

closing_acceleration_2_2_2.note: Q.88: the final six verses (vv.21–26) divide into three consecutive 2-verse terminal zones ([-ir] duty / [-ar] rejection / [-um] reckoning) — 2+2+2 accelerating cadence. Compare Q.94's 4+2+2 architecture (4-verse burden block + 2-verse ease doubling + 2-verse command close). Q.88's closing acceleration is tighter: three pairs, each thematically distinct, each phonetically sealed, producing a compression of distinct units as the surah approaches its close.

command_fulfillment_inclusio.note: Q.87 vv.1/15: اسْمَ رَبِّكَ (v.1, opening imperative) ↔ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ (v.15, success description). Only inclusio in dataset where the framing phrase is FULFILLED rather than merely repeated. The surah's opening command (exalt the name of your Lord) is executed at the mirror center (the righteous one mentions the name of his Lord and prays). Possessive shift -كَ (Prophet commanded) → -هِ (righteous one described) marks command→universal application. Compare Q.112 أَحَدٌ (purely lexical frame), Q.114 النَّاسِ (word-frame at open/close): those are lexical anchors; Q.87's is syntactic and semantic.

three_unit_root_thread.note: Q.87: root ذ-ك-ر spans three distinct thematic units (vv.9/10/15) with a narrative arc: command (فَذَكِّرْ v.9) → reception (سَيَذَّكَّرُ v.10) → practice (وَذَكَرَ v.15). The root tracks the reminder's complete journey: imperative → passive future → active perfect. Most distributed single-root structural signal in dataset. Compare: Q.93 يَتِيم (two units), Q.92 مَالَهُ (two units), Q.88 عَيْن (two units) — all are two-unit bridges. Q.87 ذ-ك-ر is the only confirmed three-unit root thread.

bal_plural_indictment_pivot.note: بَلْ + second-person plural imperfect pivots from positive declaration to collective indictment. Q.87 v.16: بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا — after success declaration (v.14). Q.89 v.17: كَلَّا بَلْ لَا تُكْرِمُونَ الْيَتِيمَ — after false human inference (vv.15–16). Q.87: بَلْ opens directly. Q.89: كَلَّا precedes. Both mark the structural pivot from the surah's positive arc to its corrective charge. Second confirmed cross-surah syntactic pivot pattern in sequence, after وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَى close (Q.92/Q.93).

imperative_surah_opening.note: Q.87 سَبِّحِ: sole surah in dataset opening with a direct imperative command. Every other surah analyzed opens with: oath (waw al-qasam, لَا أُقْسِمُ, or هَلْ أَتَاكَ), creation/declaration (لَمْ يَكُن, تَبَّتْ), or divine attribute/interrogative. Q.87 opens on the Prophet's obligation before any divine testimony or oath is invoked. The scriptural authentication (vv.18–19) closes the surah by grounding that obligation in the scriptures of Abraham and Moses — the command is first, the authority is last.

ma_adraka_construction.note: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ construction appears in three surahs with distinct structural placements. Q.86 v.2: within oath unit before apodosis — interrupts oath chain, interrogates oath object before resolution (unique: only pre-apodosis placement). Q.90 vv.11–12: at structural pivot — وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ mā l-ʿaqabah names the pivot object immediately before the pivot. Q.101 vv.3/10: double-frame — at surah opening and post-pivot close (double_ma_adraka_frame, unique). Placement varies: within oath (Q.86) / at pivot (Q.90) / as double structural bracket (Q.101). Q.83: double وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ (vv.8/19) — the most structurally explicit deployment in the sequence; both instances interrogate the two cosmic record locations (sijjīn / ʿilliyyūn) symmetrically, confirming the binary record architecture as the surah's structural spine. Q.82 vv.17–18: consecutive double وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ with ثُمَّ escalation — sole case in sequence where the construction appears twice back-to-back (no intervening verse). The ثُمَّ repeats the unanswerable question, deepening register while preserving terminal. Compare Q.83 vv.8/19 and Q.101 vv.3/10 (separated deployments). Q.74 v.27: وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا سَقَرُ — single deployment at geometric center as pivot verse; Saqar interrogated through the Prophet; triggers discourse shift from divine 1st-person (v.26) to 2nd-person. Confirms: ma_adraka always marks a structural turning point — n=6.

root_concentration_masdar_doubling.note: High-density root repetition using masdar or verbal noun doubling at structural peaks. Q.86 vv.15–16: ك-ي-د ×3 (يَكِيدُونَ كَيْدًا / أَكِيدُ كَيْدًا) — densest root concentration in fewest verses in dataset; masdar terminal ×2 exact consecutive. Q.99 vv.1–2: ز-ل-ز-ل ×2 (زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزَالَهَا — surah title root as masdar at opening). Q.100 vv.1–3: ع-د-و root chain across participial series (verbal-noun-driven opening). Each instance places root concentration at a thematic peak or structural opening.

dual_oath_architecture.note: Two distinct oath units separated by a central argument block (vv.1–3 and vv.11–12), lexically anchored by exact verbatim repetition of وَالسَّمَاءِ at both oath-opening positions. First unit resolves to universal guardian (v.4); second resolves to Quranic authority (v.13). Only confirmed verbatim cross-unit inclusio at oath-opening positions in dataset. The ر-ج-ع root bridge (vv.8/11) creates an analogical link between the units' doctrinal pivots.

d_consonant_vowel_alternation.note: Q.85: /d/ consonant dominates 18/22 terminals while vowel alternates structurally: [-uːd] ×9 (Trench/historical/narrative content) ↔ [-iːd] ×9 (divine attribute/Quranic content). Only surah in sequence where a single terminal consonant dominates >50% of verses while its vowel encodes thematic register. Distinct from full-uniform surahs (Q.87/Q.91/Q.92) and distinct-zone surahs (Q.88/Q.90/Q.86).

shad_three_terminal_thread.note: Q.85: ش-ه-د root at three distinct terminal positions (v.3 مَشْهُودٍ / v.7 شُهُودٌ / v.9 شَهِيدٌ). Complete witnessing arc: oath object → oppressors as witnesses (moral inversion) → Allah as Witness (capstone). Root's internal vowel (/uː/→/iː/ v.3–7→v.9) tracks the surah's dominant /d/-system vowel alternation. Only root appearing at three distinct terminal positions in a single surah in sequence.

majid_divine_quran_bridge.note: Q.85: الْمَجِيدُ (v.15, definite divine Throne title) ↔ قُرْآنٌ مَجِيدٌ (v.21, indefinite Quranic predicate). Only case in sequence where a divine attribute root reappears as a descriptor of the Quran within the same surah. Grammatical shift (definite→indefinite) marks the transfer of the attribute from the divine realm to the Quranic realm.

dual_bal_adversative_close.note: Q.85 vv.19/21: two consecutive بَلْ adversatives in final four verses. First بَلْ: disbelief declared (v.19). Second بَلْ: Quranic authentication (v.21). Both pivot from rejection evidence to a counter-declaration. Unique closing device in dataset.

rabb_multi_unit_thread.note: Q.84: root ر-ب-ب at four structural positions spanning three distinct units — vv.2/5 (رَبِّهَا: sky/earth's cosmic obedience), v.6 (رَبِّكَ: human accountability), v.15 (رَبَّهُ: divine omniscience/correction). Longest single-root structural spine in dataset (four positions), surpassing ش-ه-د ×3 (Q.85) and ذ-ك-ر ×3 (Q.87). The بَلَى correction at v.15 seals the thread as the surah's theological hinge.

masrura_antithetical_terminal.note: Q.84: identical lexical word مَسْرُورًا at terminal position of two structurally opposed verses (v.9: righteous returns in happiness / v.13: condemned had been in happiness). Only case in sequence where a full lexical word (not just root or morphological form) appears at the terminal position of two verses deployed antithetically. Moral argument encoded in sound identity: same word, opposite meanings across the structural boundary.

internal_unit_verbatim_phrase_inclusio.note: Q.84 vv.2/5: full phrase وَأَذِنَتْ لِرَبِّهَا وَحُقَّت repeated verbatim at the terminal position of both verses within the cosmic submission unit. Only case in sequence where a full phrase (not just root or single word) is repeated verbatim as a terminal within a single unit, functioning as an internal inclusio bracketing the two cosmic objects (sky v.2 / earth v.5).

fa_amma_wa_amma_binary_partition.note: Q.84 vv.7/10: formal فَأَمَّا / وَأَمَّا partition structure for the two human destinies. Only surah in this sequence using this formal binary partition device. كِتَابَهُ exact root ×2 bridges both cases; فَسَوْفَ ×2 introduces both fates with identical grammatical structure. Q.80 vv.5/8: أَمَّا مَنِ اسْتَغْنَى (self-sufficient) ↔ وَأَمَّا مَن جَاءَكَ يَسْعَى (striving); partition frames Prophet rebuke unit; يَزَّكَّى ×2 (vv.3/7) internally frames the partition within the [-a] zone. Compare Q.84: the partition divides eschatological outcomes; here it divides present social/spiritual attention. Q.79 vv.37/40: فَأَمَّا مَن طَغَى (transgressor→Hell) / وَأَمَّا مَن خَافَ (fearful→Paradise). Used for eschatological binary verdict. Q.79 shares this with Q.80 (rebuke unit) and Q.84 (eschatological close).

binary_record_exact_mirror.note: Q.83: sijjīn unit (vv.7–9) ↔ 'illiyyūn unit (vv.18–21) — most explicitly mirrored block pair in sequence; confirmed by three independent verbatim bridges: (i) كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَابَ opens both in exact syntactic form; (ii) وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ exact ×2 (vv.8/19); (iii) كِتَابٌ مَرْقُومٌ exact ×2 (vv.9/20). 'Illiyyūn unit asymmetrically extends by v.21: condemned record merely filed; righteous record has a privileged witness audience.

laughter_syntactic_inversion.note: Q.83 vv.29/34: near-identical syntactic structure with subject and object precisely inverted. Criminals laughed AT believers → believers laugh AT disbelievers. يَضْحَكُونَ terminal unchanged — laughter sounds identical; only direction reverses. The surah's moral argument delivered in a syntactic mirror at the terminal position. عَلَى الْأَرَائِكِ يَنظُرُونَ (vv.23/35) extends the reversal into posture: paradise observing posture = eschatological triumph posture.

binary_oscillation_rhyme_system.note: Q.83: persistent binary terminal oscillation between [-iːna/-uːna] (plural suffix) and [-iːm/-iːn/-uːm] (singular noun) throughout all 36 verses; no zone of more than two consecutive verses in the same non-plural terminal. Structural argument carried by exact terminal-word repetitions at strategic positions rather than zone separation. Produces the highest count of exact terminal repetitions in the sequence (four). Contrasts sharply with Q.84's grammar-as-rhyme zones and Q.85's vowel-alternation system.

wayl_double_verdict_frame.note: Q.83: only surah in sequence to open with وَيْلٌ. The verdict returns at v.10 (وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ). Commercial crime (cheating) ↔ theological crime (denying the Day) both framed by the same verdict word. Present condemnation (v.1, undated) → eschatological condemnation (v.10, يَوْمَئِذٍ) using identical word. Structural argument: commercial fraud and theological denial diagnosed as a single failure of moral accountability.

four_exact_terminal_repetitions_record.note: Q.83: four confirmed exact terminal word/phrase repetitions — highest count in sequence. (a) سِجِّينٍ/سِجِّينٌ ×2 consecutive vv.7/8; (b) كِتَابٌ مَرْقُومٌ exact phrase ×2 vv.9/20; (c) عَلَى الْأَرَائِكِ يَنظُرُونَ exact phrase ×2 vv.23/35; (d) يَضْحَكُونَ exact ×2 antithetical vv.29/34. Previous high was two (Q.84: وَحُقَّت ×2 and مَسْرُورًا ×2). Binary oscillation system makes zone-based separation impossible so exact repetition becomes the primary structural device.

al_infitar_inshiqaq_twin_architecture.note: Q.82/Q.84: Five confirmed structural parallels — highest cross-surah correspondence in sequence. (a) إِذَا السَّمَاءُ + passive perfect opening verb (انفَطَرَت/انشَقَّت); (b) Five-verse [-at] passive perfect zone (both vv.1–5); (c) نَفْسٌ as apodosis subject (Q.82 v.5; Q.84 v.6); (d) يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنسَانُ direct address immediately following [-at] zone (both v.6); (e) فَ creation chain in challenge unit. Shared architecture frames distinct theological arguments: Q.82 challenges through divine generosity (الْكَرِيمِ); Q.84 through labor toward the Lord (كَادِحٌ إِلَى رَبِّكَ).

naim_jahim_antithetical_terminal.note: Q.82 vv.13–14: نَعِيمٍ [-iːm] and جَحِيمٍ [-iːm] — exact terminal and morphological form (indefinite tanwīn) in consecutive antithetical couplet. Most phonetically compressed binary verdict in sequence: pleasure and Hellfire are phonetically indistinguishable at the terminal; the moral difference is encoded entirely in semantics. [-iːm] also at v.6 (الْكَرِيمِ): the divine Generous sounds like both outcomes, framing the stakes of the challenge across the whole surah.

yawm_al_din_triple_close.note: Q.82 vv.15/17/18: يَوْمَ الدِّينِ exact phrase ×3 in the surah's final five verses. Phrase introduced in condemnation (v.15) then subjected to consecutive double وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ interrogation (vv.17–18). ثُمَّ escalation marks not a new question but the deepening of an unanswerable one. The الدِّينِ root threads the surah: denied at pivot v.9 → approaching at vv.15–18 → the Day's absolute sovereignty declared at v.19.

consecutive_wama_adraka_thumma.note: Q.82 vv.17–18: sole case in sequence where وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ appears twice consecutively (no intervening verse) with ثُمَّ as the only variation. All other double deployments (Q.83 vv.8/19, Q.101 vv.3/10) are separated by intervening units. The consecutive form encodes the incomprehensibility of the answer: the first asking is insufficient; ثُمَّ repeats the same unanswerable question. Compare the separated deployments in Q.83 which produce structural equation of the two record units rather than escalation.

sawwa_fa_creation_chain.note: Root س-و-ي in فَ creation chain across three surahs in the sequence. Q.82 v.7: خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ (direct 2nd-person creation address). Q.87 v.2: الَّذِي خَلَقَ فَسَوَّى (3rd-person creation description in imperative surah). Q.91 v.7: وَنَفْسٍ وَمَا سَوَّاهَا (creation oath at structural pivot, bridging creation/destruction mirror). The root threads the creation-challenge vocabulary across the Q.91–Q.82 window. Q.79 v.28: رَفَعَ سَمْكَهَا فَسَوَّاهَا — sky raised and proportioned; within the creation challenge unit (vv.27-33). فَسَوَّى here applied to the sky (السَّمَاء), compared to Al-A'la (earth/humans) and Al-Inshiqaq. Q.75 v.38: فَخَلَقَ فَسَوَّى — creation argument close; bridges with نُسَوِّيَ at v.4 (opening rebuttal). The proportioning root س-و-ي frames the surah's resurrection argument: We can proportion even fingertips (v.4) ↔ We created and proportioned him (v.38).

alima_nafsu_apodosis.note: Exact phrase عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ marks the transition from cosmic dissolution chain to individual accountability in both Q.81 (v.14: مَا أَحْضَرَت) and Q.82 (v.5: مَا قَدَّمَتْ وَأَخَّرَت). Most consistent cross-surah structural device for this transition in sequence. Q.84 uses يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنسَانُ (v.6) as the equivalent pivot. All three surahs share the cosmic chain → individual soul accountability structure.

passive_perfect_at_zone_cosmic.note: Passive perfect feminine [-at] zone opens cosmic dissolution chains in three consecutive surahs. Q.81: 14 verses (longest in sequence; apodosis within zone — unique). Q.82: 5 verses (apodosis breaks zone). Q.84: 5 verses (apodosis breaks zone). The zone encodes cosmic submission in grammar: passive voice = something was done to these objects by divine command. Q.81's apodosis within the zone makes the rupture and its consequence phonetically indistinguishable. Q.77 vv.8-12: طُمِسَتْ/فُرِجَتْ/نُسِفَتْ/أُقِّتَتْ/أُجِّلَتْ — passive perfect feminine ×5; matches Q.84 and Q.82 zone lengths exactly. The إِذَا chain opens with فَإِذَا (v.8) exactly as Q.84.

binary_oath_prosodic_architecture.note: Q.81 vv.15–18: four oath objects divide into two phonetically distinct pairs. Stellar pair (vv.15–16): بِالْخُنَّسِ/الْكُنَّسِ [-is]×2. Temporal pair (vv.17–18): عَسْعَسَ/تَنَفَّسَ [-as]×2. Only oath unit in sequence where objects are organized into two phonetically separated pairs. Compare Q.86 (dual_oath_architecture) which also has binary oath structure but of a different type.

alamin_terminal_inclusio.note: Q.81 vv.27/29: الْعَالَمِينَ exact terminal ×2 frames the closing unit; v.28 يَسْتَقِيمَ [-iːma] is near-exact medial term. Three-verse closing terminal inclusio unique in sequence. Reminder to the worlds (v.27) ↔ Lord of the worlds (v.29): scope and sovereignty share identical terminal. Simultaneously the ش-و-ء root threads vv.28–29 as a lexical close: surah closes on both a terminal frame and a lexical thread.

karim_rajim_antithetical_zone_frame.note: Q.81 vv.19/25: zone 4 (vv.19–25) opens with كَرِيمٍ (noble, Gabriel) and closes with رَجِيمٍ (expelled, devil). Both [-iːm] terminal. Maximally opposed in meaning, near-identical in sound. Most semantically opposed terminal pair within a single zone in sequence. Compare نَعِيمٍ/جَحِيمٍ (Q.82 vv.13–14) which are consecutive rather than zone-framing.

mawuuda_judicial_within_chain.note: Q.81 vv.8–9: two-verse judicial scene (الْمَوْءُودَة questioned for her sin) embedded within the 13-verse cosmic dissolution chain. Only instance in sequence where a specific human victim is named and interrogated within a cosmic dissolution chain. Vv.8–9 sit at the midpoint of the 13-verse chain. The passive grammar of قُتِلَت is identical to the passive grammar of the cosmic events — the girl was killed sounds phonetically indistinguishable from the sea was set aflame.

third_person_prophet_opening.note: Q.80 v.1: عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّى — surah opens in third-person narration about the Prophet's action before the Prophet is addressed. No other surah in the analyzed sequence opens this way. Pivot to direct second-person rebuke at v.3 (وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ). The surah narrates the Prophet's conduct to a presumed witness audience before addressing him.

dual_grammar_generated_zones_flanking_pi.note: Q.80: Zone 3 (vv.17–23: [-ahu]×7, masculine object suffix — divine action on man) and Zone 4 (vv.25–31: [-an]×7, indefinite tanwīn — food produce) are both seven verses, both grammar-generated, and flank the كَلَّا pivot at v.23. Only case in sequence where two grammar-generated rhyme zones of identical length bracket the structural pivot. The device in Zone 3 (pronoun suffix as rhyme) also appears in Q.86 vv.7–8 ([-ak] ×2, 2nd-person suffix zone).

exact_terminal_within_single_zone_intern.note: Q.80 vv.3/7: يَزَّكَّى ×2 as exact terminal word within zone 1 ([-a]×10, vv.1–10), opening and closing the أَمَّا/وَأَمَّا binary partition. All prior exact terminal repetitions in dataset (مَسْرُورًا Q.84, يَضْحَكُونَ Q.83, يَنظُرُونَ Q.83, مَرْقُومٌ Q.83) cross unit boundaries; this is the first case confined within a single prosodic zone as an internal frame device. Q.79 vv.19/26: يَخْشَى ×2 within zone 3 (alif maqsura ×12, vv.15-26) — opens the fear invitation to Pharaoh (v.19) and closes the lesson for whoever fears (v.26); frames the Moses narrative internally. Confirms device in Q.80 (يَزَّكَّى ×2) as cross-surah pattern. Two instances now confirmed: both in immediate sequence (Q.79/Q.80) and both within their surah's longest prosodic zone.

wujuh_yawmaidhin_binary_verdict.note: وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ ×2 as binary verdict device: Q.88 vv.2/8 (first instance in sequence; two face-sets open the surah's two halves); Q.80 vv.38/40 (closing binary verdict after eschatological rupture). Both place both face-types in the same terminal zone: Q.88 uses active participle [-ah]; Q.80 uses feminine [-ah]. The device announces binary eschatological outcome through the same formulaic construction. Q.75 vv.22/24: وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ نَاضِرَةٌ / وَوُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ بَاسِرَةٌ — binary faces; with the additional feature that the first two positive-face verses (vv.22/23) share the most phonetically proximate terminal pair in the sequence (نَاضِرَةٌ/نَاظِرَةٌ, differing only in ض/ظ).

ihi_possessive_severance_zone.note: Q.80 vv.34–37: [-ihi]×4 — family severance unit unified by third-person masculine possessive suffix. His brother / his mother and father / his wife and children / what is adequate for him. The grammar of possessive belonging marks the bonds that will be severed. Only surah in sequence where the possessive pronoun suffix functions as the terminal marker for a unit thematically about the breakdown of those bonds. Compare [-ahu] zone 3 (vv.17–23): object suffix tracked divine action on man; here possessive suffix tracks man's severance from kin.

mataan_lakum_exact_cross_surah_phrase.note: مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ وَلِأَنْعَامِكُمْ: exact verbatim phrase at the same structural position in Q.80 v.32 and Q.79 v.33 — both close the creation/provision enumeration unit and are immediately followed by فَإِذَا جَاءَتِ opening the eschatological rupture unit. Longest confirmed verbatim cross-surah phrase in the sequence. Only confirmed case of a full phrase functioning as an architectural position marker at the same structural slot in two separate surahs.

withheld_disclosure_surah_frame.note: Q.79 opens and closes on withheld disclosure: (1) oath chain vv.1-5 — five agents named but apodosis suspended across 5 verses, delivered only at v.6; (2) Hour interrogation vv.42-46 — the Hour's timing asked and withheld from even the Prophet (مُنتَهَاهَا إِلَى رَبِّكَ, v.44). The surah's opening and closing structural frames are both organized around the suspension of knowledge.

mawa_exact_antithetical_terminal.note: Q.79 vv.39/41: الْمَأْوَى ×2 — Hell is the refuge / Paradise is the refuge. Both binary verdict outcomes close with the exact same lexeme. More structurally extreme than Q.82 نَعِيمٍ/جَحِيمٍ (which share terminal sound pattern but are different words): here a single word is the terminal for both eschatological destinations, differing only in what precedes. The two fates are phonetically identical. Q.78 vv.22/39: مَآبًا ×2 — Hell as forced return ↔ voluntary return to Lord. Pattern confirmed in consecutive surahs Q.79/Q.78. Q.76 vv.9/22: جَزَاءً ×2 — righteous refuse it from humans (v.9) ↔ God gives it to them (v.22). Antithetical exact terminal in same-word device now confirmed in four consecutive surahs Q.76-Q.79 (جَزَاءً / مَآبًا / الْمَأْوَى / كُلُوا).

cognate_accusative_oath_zone.note: Q.79 vv.1-5: masdar terminals repeat root of their oath participle in 3 consecutive cases (النَّاشِطَاتِ نَشْطًا / السَّابِحَاتِ سَبْحًا / السَّابِقَاتِ سَبْقًا, vv.2-4). Densest مَفْعُول مُطْلَق (cognate accusative) concentration in the sequence. The oath objects echo their own roots at the terminal — each action described by its own root. Q.77 vv.2-4: الْعَاصِفَاتِ عَصْفًا / النَّاشِرَاتِ نَشْرًا / الْفَارِقَاتِ فَرْقًا — cognate ×3 in oath zone, identical count to Q.79. Both surahs use 6-verse oath zones with ×3 cognate accusatives at the same density. Pattern confirmed across consecutive surahs Q.79/Q.77 (with Q.78 intervening and using [-an] tanwīn without cognate).

dual_alif_maqsura_zones_within_surah.note: Q.79: Zone 3 (vv.15-26: alif maqsura ×12, Moses narrative) and Zone 5 (vv.34-46: alif maqsura ×13, eschatological verdict+Hour close). Two alif maqsura zones of near-equal length (12+13v) within the same surah, separated by creation unit [-aha]×6. Moses narrative and eschatological close prosodically twinned. No other analyzed surah produces two alif maqsura zones of comparable length within one surah.

kubra_cross_zone_structural_bridge.note: Q.79: الْكُبْرَى ×2 at structurally symmetric positions — v.20 (greatest sign shown to Pharaoh, zone 3 Moses narrative climax) ↔ v.34 (greatest Overwhelming Calamity, zone 5 eschatological opening). The superlative bridges the historical exemplar's peak and the eschatological consequence across 14 verses. Also bridges zones 3 and 5, twinning Moses' greatest sign with the Day's greatest event.

duha_longest_span_terminal_bridge.note: Q.79: ضُحَاهَا ×2 at vv.29/46 — 17-verse span. v.29 (zone 4 creation: the sky's extracted brightness) ↔ v.46 (zone 5 close: the morning of the Day they see the Hour). Longest-span exact terminal bridge in the sequence. Sky's daytime light ↔ the final morning of reckoning.

three_nested_discourse_layers.note: Q.79 vv.16-19: deepest discourse nesting confirmed in sequence. Layer 1: divine narrator reports God calling Moses. Layer 2 (v.17): God's direct command to Moses (go to Pharaoh). Layer 3 (v.18-19): God commands Moses to speak to Pharaoh (فَقُلْ). Three simultaneous discourse frames: narrator / God→Moses / Moses→Pharaoh. Q.72 v.1: divine→Prophet(قُلْ)→jinn(إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا) — three layers activated simultaneously in single verse; more compressed than Q.79 spread across four verses. Q.71 v.23: divine text / Noah's prayer / people's speech (وَقَالُوا) with idols named in their own voice — three layers at complaint/naming boundary.

longest_sustained_terminal_zone.note: Q.78 vv.6-40: [-an]×35 — longest terminal zone in analyzed sequence. Previous max: Q.81 [-at]×14. Spans 6 thematic units without break.

four_internal_exact_terminal_pairs_singl.note: Q.78: مَآبًا ×2 / جَزَاءً ×2 / حِسَابًا ×2 / كِذَّابًا ×2 — all within the [-an] zone. Highest count of internal exact terminal pairs in one zone. All four are antithetically meaningful.

binary_prosodic_architecture_surah.note: Q.78: [-uːna]/[-iːm] (vv.1-5: questioners) ↔ [-an]×35 (vv.6-40: everything else). Strictest binary terminal architecture in sequence.

communal_to_individual_speech_arc.note: Q.78: opens with communal questioning (v.1 يَتَسَاءَلُونَ) → closes with individual solitary wish (v.40 يَا لَيْتَنِي كُنتُ تُرَابًا). Communal debate resolves into the disbeliever's lone voice.

thematic_seam_without_prosodic_break.note: Q.78 v.16→17: creation evidence close (أَلْفَافًا) → Day declared (مِيقَاتًا) with no change in terminal class. Provision and Judgment share the same terminal zone.

wayl_refrain_structural_spine.note: Q.77: وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ verbatim ×10 at vv.15/19/24/28/34/37/40/45/47/49. Most sustained exact refrain in analyzed sequence. Not a rhyme device but a complete sentence inserted as structural separator dividing the surah into units. Refrain's [-iːna] terminal is shared by adjacent evidence units (vv.16-18 and v.44), making deniers/criminals/righteous prosodically indistinguishable. Compare wayl_double_verdict_frame (Q.83) — the same وَيْلٌ verdict appears ×2 in Q.83 as a framing device; Q.77 expands this to ×10 as the entire surah's spine.
